blocks:
Sūrat Āl ‘Imrān

Medinan, and it is 200 verses.

Sūrat Āl ‘Imrān

It is entirely Medinan, and it is two hundred verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الم (1) Alif Lām Mīm. The discussion concerning its interpretation (تأويله) has already passed at the beginning of Sūrat al-Baqarah. And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: As for ** الم ** in Āl ‘Imrān, its meaning is: I am Allah, the Glorious (المجيد).

(٢) اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ. (2) Allah—there is no god except Him—is the Living, the Sustainer.

(٣) نَزَّلَ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ (3) He has sent down to you the Book (meaning) the Qur’an, in portions (نجوماً), ** بِالْحَقِ (meaning) with justice (بالعدل), truthfulness (والصدق), and the established proofs (والحجج المحققة) that it is from Allah, مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ (meaning) of the Books, ** وَأَنْزَلَ التَّوْراةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ ** entirely (جملة) upon Moses and Jesus.

(٤) ** مِنْ قَبْلُ (4) before the sending down of the Quran ** هُدىً لِلنَّاسِ (a guidance for mankind) generally, and for their people specifically وَأَنْزَلَ الْفُرْقانَ (and He sent down the Criterion)—that by which He distinguishes between truth and falsehood.

مِنْ قَبْلُ هُدىً لِلنَّاسِ وَأَنْزَلَ الْفُرْقانَ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ اللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَاللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انْتِقامٍ (4) before [this], as a guidance for mankind, and He sent down the Criterion. Indeed, there is a severe punishment for those who defy the signs of Allah, and Allah is all-mighty, avenger.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Quran is the entirety (جملة) of the Book (الكتاب), and الْفُرْقانَ (the Criterion) is the decisive (المحكم) part that is obligatory to act upon.

And in al-Jawami', narrated from him (peace be upon him): ** الْفُرْقانَ ** (the Criterion) is every decisive verse (آية محكمة) in the Book.

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): الْفُرْقانَ (the Criterion) is every decisive matter (أمر محكم), and الكتاب (the Book) is the entirety (جملة) of the Quran, which confirms those prophets who came before Him.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The Quran was named فرقاناً (Criterion) because its verses (الآيات) and chapters (السّور) are separated (متفرّق), having been sent down not on tablets (الألواح) nor on scrolls (الصحف). Whereas the Torah (التوراة), the Gospel (الإنجيل), and the Psalms (الزبور) were all sent down entirely (جملة) on tablets (الألواح) and paper (الورق).

(٥) ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ (5) Indeed, those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah— from His revealed Books and others ** لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ there is a severe punishment for them, because of their disbelief ** وَاللهُ عَزِيزٌ ** and Allah is all-mighty, dominant (غالب), Who is not prevented from inflicting punishment ** ذُو انْتِقامٍ the Lord of retribution. severe (شديد), such that no avenger (منتقم) is capable of its like إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَخْفى عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلا فِي السَّماءِ ** (6) Indeed nothing is hidden from Allah in the earth or in the heaven. He expressed the universe (العالم) through them (the earth and the heaven) because the senses (الحسّ) do not go beyond them.

(٦) هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَوِّرُكُمْ فِي الْأَرْحامِ كَيْفَ يَشاءُ (6) He it is who shapes you in the wombs however He wishes. [He shapes you] from the various forms, whether beautiful or ugly, male or female. So how can anything be hidden from Him? In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, desires to create a creature, He gathers every form (صورة) between him [the creature] and Adam, and then He creates him upon the form (صورة) of one of them. Therefore, let no one say concerning his child, "This one does not resemble me, nor does he resemble any of my forefathers." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, desires to create the drop (النطفة)—which is from that upon which the covenant (الميثاق) was taken from the loins of Adam, or that which becomes manifest (ما يبدو له) to Him concerning it—and places it in the womb, He moves the man toward intercourse and reveals (أوحى) to the womb: "Open your door so that My creation, My effective decree (قضائي النافذ), and My destiny (قدري) may enter you." The womb then opens its door, and the drop (النطفة) reaches the womb and remains therein, transforming for forty days. Then it becomes a clot (علقة) for forty days. Then it becomes a lump of flesh (مضغة) for forty days. Then it becomes flesh through which intertwined veins run. Then Allah sends two creating angels (ملكين خلّاقين) who create in the wombs whatever He wills. They enter the woman's belly through the woman's mouth and reach the womb. In it is the ancient spirit (الروح القديمة) transferred from the loins of men and the wombs of women. They breathe into it the spirit of life and permanence (البقاء), and they fashion for it hearing, sight, all the limbs (الجوارح), and everything within the belly, by the permission of Allah the Almighty. Then Allah reveals to the two angels: "Write upon him My decree (قضائي), My destiny (قدري), and My effective command (نافذ أمري), and stipulate for Me the possibility of alteration (البداء) in what you write." They say: "O Lord, what shall we write?" He said: Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, reveals to them: "Raise your heads toward his mother's head." They raise their heads, and behold, the Tablet (اللوح) strikes the mother's forehead. They look into it and find in the Tablet his form (صورته), his adornment (زينته), his term (أجله), his covenant (ميثاقه), whether wretched or felicitous (شقيّاً أو سعيداً), and all his affairs. He (peace be upon him) said: One of them dictates to his companion, and they write everything in the Tablet, stipulating the possibility of alteration (البداء) in what they write. Then they seal the writing and place it between his eyes. Then they make him stand upright in his mother's belly. He said: Sometimes he becomes rebellious (عتا) and turns upside down, but this only happens in the case of every rebellious (عات) or defiant (مارد) one. When the time for the child's exit arrives, whether complete or incomplete, Allah the Almighty reveals to the womb: "Open your door so that My creation may exit to My earth and My command may be executed in him, for the time of his exit has arrived." He said: The womb then opens the door for the child. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sends an angel called Zajir (زاجر). He rebukes him with a rebuke (زجرة) from which the child is startled, so he turns over, and his legs become above his head and his head is at the bottom of the belly, so that Allah may facilitate the exit for the woman and the child. He said: If he is detained, the angel rebukes him with another rebuke, and he is startled by it, and the child falls to the ground, weeping, startled by the rebuke. I say: His saying "to create the drop (النطفة)" means to create it as a complete human being. And His saying "or that which becomes manifest (ما يبدو له) to Him concerning it" means what becomes manifest to Him concerning its creation, so that its creation is not completed, by Him making it a miscarriage (سقطاً). And His saying "He moves the man" means by casting desire (الشهوة) upon him. And the revelation (إيحاؤه) of the Glorified One to the womb is an allusion (كناية) to His creation of it upon natural obedience (الإطاعة طبعاً). So fataraddad (فتردد)—with the omission of one of the two tā’s (التّائين)—means it transforms from one state to another. Yaqtaḥimān (يقتحمان) means they enter violently. And the ancient spirit (الروح القديمة) is an allusion (كناية) to the vegetative soul (النفس النباتية). And the conjunction of permanence (البقاء) upon life (الحياة) indicates that the animal soul (النفس الحيوانية) is immaterial (مجرّدة عن المادة) and remains in that existence (النشأة), whereas the vegetative soul (النفس النباتية) by itself does not remain. We have verified the meaning of alteration (البداء) in our book entitled al-Wafi (الوافي). And the striking of the Tablet (اللوح) upon his mother's forehead is as if it is an allusion (كناية) to the manifestation of his mother's states, attributes, and character traits from her forelock (ناصيتها) and the form (صورتها) upon which she was created, as if they are all written upon her. The states that the child ought to possess are derived from his mother's forelock, and that is written in accordance with what is there, due to the suitability (المناسبة) that exists between him and her. This is because the essence of the spirit (جوهر الروح) flows onto the body according to its readiness and acceptance of it, and the body's readiness is dependent upon the states of the souls (نفسي) of the two parents, their attributes, and their character traits, especially the mother who nurtures him in accordance with what came to him from his father's loins. Thus, her forelock encompasses his paternal and maternal states—I mean, what suits them both according to the requirement of his essence. And the placing of the sealed writing between his eyes is an allusion (كناية) to the manifestation of his attributes and character traits from his forelock (ناصيته) and the form (صورته) upon which he was created, and that he is knowledgeable of them at that time through the knowledge of his Creator concerning them, due to his annihilation (فنائه) after the annihilation of his attributes in his Lord, because he has not yet entered the world of causes (عالم الأسباب), borrowed attributes (الصفات المستعارة), and metaphorical choice (الاختيار المجازي). However, he is not conscious of his knowledge, for the consciousness of a thing is one matter, and the consciousness of consciousness is another matter. And al-ʿutuww (العتوّ) is arrogance (الاستكبار) and transgression of the limit (مجاوزة الحدّ), and al-marūd (المرود) is close to it. There is no god except Him, since no one other than Him knows the totality of what He knows, nor is anyone capable of doing the like of what He does. ** الْعَزِيزُ in His majesty (جلاله), ** الْحَكِيمُ in His actions. (He is) the All-mighty, the All-wise.

(٧) هُوَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ مِنْهُ آياتٌ مُحْكَماتٌ (7) He it is Who has sent down to you the Book. Parts of it are definitive verses whose expressions have been made firm (أُحْكِمَتْ عِبَارَاتُهَا) by being preserved from ambiguity (الإِجْمَال). ** هُنَّ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ ** —they are the Mother of the Book— its foundation (أَصْلُهُ); others are referred back to them. ** وَأُخَرُ مُتَشابِهاتٌ and others are ambiguous. ambiguous (مُحْتَمَلَات) whose intended meaning is not clear except through examination and contemplation, so that the merit of the Divine scholars (الْعُلَمَاءِ الرَبَّانِيِّينَ) may become apparent in deducing their meanings and referring them back to the definitive verses (الْمُحْكَمَات), and so that they may thereby attain the knowledge of Allah the Almighty and His Oneness (تَوْحِيدِهِ). Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the definitive (الْمُحْكَم) and the ambiguous (الْمُتَشَابِه). He said: "The definitive is that which is acted upon, and the ambiguous is that which is confusing (اشْتَبَهَ) to the ignorant person." Other narrations concerning their interpretation have already preceded in the Fourth Introduction (الْمُقَدِّمَةِ الرَّابِعَة). In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation (تَأْوِيلِهِ): The definitive verses (الْمُحْكَمَات) are the Commander of the Faithful (أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) and the Imams (peace be upon them), and the ambiguous verses (الْمُتَشَابِهَات) are so-and-so and so-and-so. فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ زَيْغٌ But as for those in whose hearts is a deviation a deviation (مَيْل) from the truth, like the innovators (الْمُبْتَدِعَة), so they follow its ambiguous parts (فَيَتَّبِعُونَ مَا تَشَابَهَ مِنْهُ), clinging to its apparent meaning (ظَاهِرِهِ) or to a false interpretation (تَأْوِيلٍ بَاطِل). ** ابْتِغاءَ الْفِتْنَةِ seeking discord seeking (طَلَب) to tempt (يَفْتِنُوا) people away from their religion through doubt (التَّشْكِيك), confusion (التَّلْبِيس), and contradicting the definitive (الْمُحْكَم) with the ambiguous (الْمُتَشَابِه). In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The discord (الْفِتْنَةِ) here means disbelief (الْكُفْر). وَابْتِغاءَ تَأْوِيلِهِ and seeking its interpretation. and seeking its interpretation (وَابْتِغَاءَ تَأْوِيلِهِ), meaning seeking to interpret it according to what they desire (يَشْتَهُونَهُ). وَما يَعْلَمُ تَأْوِيلَهُ ** But none knows its interpretation and none knows its interpretation (تَأْوِيلَهُ)—that which it must be understood to mean— ** إِلَّا اللهُ وَالرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ except Allah and those firmly grounded in knowledge. who have become established (تَثَبَّتُوا) and rooted (تَمَكَّنُوا) in it. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the interpretation (تَأْوِيل) of the entire Quran. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): We are those firmly grounded in knowledge (الرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ), and we know its interpretation (تَأْوِيلَهُ). And in a narration: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is the most excellent of those firmly grounded in knowledge (الرَّاسِخِينَ فِي الْعِلْمِ). Allah Almighty has taught Him all that He sent down to Him, both the revelation (التَّنْزِيل) and the interpretation (التَّأْوِيل). Allah would not send down anything to Him without teaching Him its interpretation. And His successors (أَوْصِيَاؤُهُ) after Him know all of it. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition, he said: "Then, Allah, majestic is His mention, due to the vastness of His mercy and His compassion for His creation, and His knowledge of what the changers (الْمُبَدِّلُونَ) would introduce by altering His speech, divided His speech into three parts. He made one part of it known by the scholar (الْعَالِم) and the ignorant (الْجَاهِل); and a part that is not known except by one whose mind is pure (صَفَا ذِهْنُهُ), whose perception is subtle (لَطُفَ حِسُّهُ), and whose discernment (تَمَيُّزُهُ) is sound, among those whose breast Allah has expanded for Islam; and a part that is not known except by Allah, His Prophets, and those firmly grounded in knowledge (الرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ). He only did this so that the people of falsehood (أَهْلُ الْبَاطِل), who have seized the inheritance of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم concerning the knowledge of the Book, would not claim what He did not grant them. And so that necessity (الِاضْطِرَار) would lead them to obey the one whom He appointed over their affairs. But they acted arrogantly, refusing His obedience out of pride (تَعَزُّزًا), fabricating lies against Allah Almighty, and being deceived by the multitude who supported and aided them, and thus they opposed Allah, majestic is His Name, and His Messenger." يَقُولُونَ آمَنَّا بِهِ They say, 'We believe in it; These firmly grounded ones (الرَّاسِخُونَ), who know the interpretation (التَّأْوِيل), say: "We believe in the ambiguous (الْمُتَشَابِه)." ** كُلٌّ مِنْ all of it is from our Lord.' all of it—the definitive (الْمُحْكَم) and the ambiguous (الْمُتَشَابِه)—is from our Lord (عِنْدَ رَبِّنَا), meaning from Allah, the Wise, Whose speech does not contradict itself. ** وَما يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ And none takes admonition except those who possess intellect. This is praise for those firmly grounded (الرَّاسِخِينَ) due to the soundness of their minds (جَوْدَةِ الذِّهْن) and the excellence of their contemplation (حُسْنِ التَّدَبُّر), and an indication of what prepared them for guidance to its interpretation (تَأْوِيلِهِ), which is the purification of the intellect (تَجَرُّدُ الْعَقْل) from the veils of sensation (غَوَاشِي الْحِس). In al-Tawhid and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: "Know that those firmly grounded in knowledge (الرَّاسِخِينَ فِي الْعِلْمِ) are those whom Allah has made independent of plunging into the barriers (السُّدَد) placed before the unseen (الْغُيُوب). Thus, they adhered to affirming the entirety of the hidden unseen (الْغَيْبِ الْمَحْجُوب) whose interpretation they did not know, and they said: ** آمَنَّا بِهِ كُلٌّ مِنْ عِنْدِ رَبِّنا (We believe in it; all of it is from our Lord). So Allah Almighty praised their admission of inability to grasp what they had not encompassed with knowledge, and He named their abandonment of delving deeply into what they were not tasked to research as firmness (رُسُوخًا) on their part. Therefore, confine yourself to that, and do not estimate the greatness of Allah according to the measure of your intellect, lest you be among the perished (الْهَالِكِينَ)." In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever refers the ambiguous parts (مُتَشَابِه) of the Quran back to its definitive parts (مُحْكَم) is guided to a straight path (صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيم)." Then he (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, among our narrations there is the ambiguous (مُتَشَابِه), like the ambiguous of the Quran, and the definitive (مُحْكَم), like the definitive of the Quran. So refer their ambiguous parts back to their definitive parts, and do not follow their ambiguous parts while neglecting their definitive parts, lest you go astray (تَضِلُّوا)."

(٨) رَبَّنا لا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنا (8) Our Lord! Do not make our hearts swerve from the path of truth (نهج الحق) toward following the ambiguous (المتشابه) with an interpretation that You do not approve of (لا ترتضيه). And the swerving (الزيغ) was attributed to Allah only because it is caused by His testing (امتحانه) and His forsaking (خذلانه). ** بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنا after You have guided us to the truth (الحق). ** وَهَبْ لَنا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً and bestow on us mercy from Your side. through divine success (التوفيق) and assistance (المعونة). ** إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْوَهَّابُ Indeed, You are the All-munificent. for every request (سؤال).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) in the tradition (حديث) of Hisham: O Hisham, Allah has indeed recounted concerning righteous people (قوم صالحين) that they said: ** رَبَّنا لا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنا وَهَبْ لَنا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْوَهَّابُ (Our Lord! Do not make our hearts swerve after You have guided us, and bestow on us mercy from Your side. Indeed, You are the All-munificent.) when they knew that hearts swerve (تزيغ) and return to their blindness (عماها) and ruin (رداها). Indeed, he has not feared Allah who has not understood (يعقل) from Allah. And whoever has not understood from Allah has not bound his heart to a firm knowledge (معرفة ثابتة) which he perceives (يبصرها) and finds its reality (حقيقتها) in his heart. And no one is like that except he whose saying confirms his action (قوله لفعله مصدّقاً), and whose inner self (سرّه) conforms to his outer self (علانيته). Because Allah Almighty did not indicate the hidden falsehood (الباطل الخفي) of the intellect (العقل) except by an apparent sign (بظاهر منه) and a speaker (ناطق عنه) concerning it.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Increase your saying of: ** رَبَّنا لا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنا (Our Lord! Do not make our hearts swerve after You have guided us) and do not feel safe from swerving (الزيغ).

(٩) رَبَّنا إِنَّكَ جامِعُ النَّاسِ لِيَوْمٍ for the reckoning of the Day and its recompense ** لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ regarding its occurrence. ** إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُخْلِفُ الْمِيعادَ the promised time (الموعد), because Divinity (الإلهيّة) negates breaking it. (9) (Our Lord! You will indeed gather mankind on a day in which there is no doubt. Indeed Allah does not break His promise.)

(١٠) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوالُهُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً وَأُولئِكَ هُمْ وَقُودُ النَّارِ. (10) (Indeed those who are faithless, neither their wealth nor their children shall avail them anything against Allah, and it is they who are the fuel of the Fire.)

(١١) كَدَأْبِ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ ** like their custom (شأنهم). The origin of الدّأب (al-da’b) is toil (الكدح) (1). وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَاللهُ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ This is an intimidation (تهويل) regarding the accountability (المؤاخذة) and an increase in warning (تخويف) for the disbelievers (الكَفَرَة). (11) (Like the wont of Pharaoh’s clan and those who were before them: they denied Our signs, so Allah seized them for their sins, and Allah is severe in retribution.)

** قُلْ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا سَتُغْلَبُونَ وَتُحْشَرُونَ إِلى جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهادُ (12) Say to the faithless, 'Soon you will be overcome and mustered into hell, and what an evil resting place!' And it was recited with the yā' (الياء) in both (verbs).

In al-Majma', it is attributed to the narration of our companions (أصحابنا): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم afflicted Quraysh at Badr and arrived in Medina, He gathered the Jews in the market of Banu Qaynuqa' (سوق قَيْنُقاع) and said: "O assembly of Jews, beware of Allah (and fear) the like of what descended upon Quraysh on the Day of Badr, and submit (become Muslim) before what descended upon them descends upon you. You have certainly known that I am a sent Prophet; you find that in your Book."

They said: "O Muhammad, do not let it deceive you that you met a people who were inexperienced (أغماراً) (2), who had no knowledge of warfare, and you seized an opportunity from them. By Allah, if you were to fight us, you would know that we are the (true) people (of war)."

So Allah revealed this Ayah. And Allah certainly did that and fulfilled His promise by the killing of Banu Qurayzah, the expulsion of Banu al-Nadir, the conquest of Khaybar, and the imposition of the jizyah (الجزية) upon those who remained among them. And the polytheists (المشركون) were overcome, and this is among the proofs (دلائل) of Prophethood.

footnote (1) *Kadaha* (*كدح*) in work, like (*منع*), means he strove for himself, whether for good or evil. And *al-kadḥ* (*الكدح*) with *fatḥ* (*بفتح*) is the work and the striving in earning for the Hereafter and the world.

footnote (2) A man who is *ghamr* (*غمر*): one who has not experienced matters (*الأمور*) [ṣād].

(١٣) قَدْ كانَ لَكُمْ آيَةٌ (13) There was certainly a sign for you a miraculous indication of the truthfulness of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم فِي فِئَتَيْنِ الْتَقَتا يوم بدر ** فِئَةٌ تُقاتِلُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ a party fighting in the way of Allah, in His religion and obedience, and they were the Messenger and his companions ** ** وَ فِرْقَة ** أُخْرى كافِرَةٌ and another party that was faithless. and a group (فِرْقَة) that was faithless, and they were the polytheists of Mecca ** يَرَوْنَهُمْ مِثْلَيْهِمْ seeing them as twice their own number, The polytheists saw the Muslims as twice the number of the polytheists (and they [the polytheists] were close to a thousand), or twice the number of the Muslims (and they [the Muslims] were three hundred and ten-some). This was after He had reduced them in their eyes until they were overcome, as a reinforcement (مَدَداً) from Allah for the believers. If the believers had seen the polytheists as twice the number of the believers (when they were actually three times their number), they would have stood firm for them due to the victory which Allah promised them in His saying: ** فَإِنْ يَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ مِائَةٌ صابِرَةٌ يَغْلِبُوا مِائَتَيْنِ If there be a hundred patient men among you, they will overcome two hundred, And the reading with the tā’ (التاء) supports this, so it is said. However, the support (التأييد) is only valid if the address is directed to the believers, not the polytheists ** رَأْيَ الْعَيْنِ a manifest sight. a clear, direct observation (رؤية ظاهرة معاينة) ** وَاللهُ يُؤَيِّدُ بِنَصْرِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** And Allah confirms with His help whomever He wishes. just as He confirmed the people of Badr. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ in that—in the reduction and the increase [of numbers] and the victory of the few over the many— لَعِبْرَةً لِأُولِي الْأَبْصارِ There is indeed a lesson in that for those who have insight. there is indeed a lesson (لَعِبْرَةً) for those who have insight (لِأُولِي الْأَبْصارِ), meaning: an admonition (لَعِظَةً) for those with inner vision (ذوي البصائر).

(١٤) زُيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ حُبُّ الشَّهَواتِ ** (Fair-seeming has been made for mankind the love of [sensual] desires) meaning, the desired things (المشتهيات). He named them desires (شهوات) as an exaggeration and an allusion to the fact that they became engrossed in loving them, to the extent that they loved the desires (شهوات) themselves, like His saying, the Almighty, narrating from Sulayman (Solomon): ** إِنِّي أَحْبَبْتُ حُبَّ الْخَيْرِ ** (Indeed I have loved the love of [these] good things) ** مِنَ النِّساءِ وَالْبَنِينَ وَالْقَناطِيرِ الْمُقَنْطَرَةِ مِنَ الذَّهَبِ وَالْفِضَّةِ (including women, and children, and hoarded heaps of gold and silver) The qintar (القنطار) is the measure of a bull's hide filled with gold, as mentioned in al-Majma' (المجمع) narrated from both of them [Imams]. And al-muqantarah (المقنطرة) is derived from it for emphasis, like their saying: "a thousand compiled (ألف مؤلف)." ** وَالْخَيْلِ الْمُسَوَّمَةِ (and well-bred horses) meaning, the marked (المعلّمة) or the pastured (المرعية). ** وَالْأَنْعامِ ** (and cattle) meaning, camels (الإبل), cattle (البقر), and sheep (الغنم). ** وَالْحَرْثِ ذلِكَ مَتاعُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَاللهُ عِنْدَهُ حُسْنُ الْمَآبِ (and tillage. That is the provision of the life of the world, but Allah is with Whom is the best return.) [The best return (حُسْنُ الْمَآبِ)] is the place of return (المرجع). And this is an encouragement to exchange what is with Him of true, eternal pleasures for the fleeting, deficient (المخدجة) (١) desires.

(١٥) قُلْ أَأُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِخَيْرٍ مِنْ ذلِكُمْ (15) Say, 'Shall I inform you of something better than that?' He intends by this to affirm that the reward of Allah is better than the pleasures of this world. ** لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَأَزْواجٌ مُطَهَّرَةٌ For those who are Godwary, with their Lord are gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them [forever], and purified spouses, purified from what is considered repulsive (يُسْتَقْذَرُ) in women. ** وَرِضْوانٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَاللهُ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبادِ and Allah's beatitude. And Allah is watchful over His servants, [watchful] over their deeds, so He rewards the good-doer and punishes the evil-doer according to their deserts (اسْتِحْقَاقِهِمْ).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): People have not enjoyed any pleasure in this world or the Hereafter that is greater for them than the pleasure of women, and this is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: ** زُيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ حُب الشَّهَواتِ مِنَ النِّساءِ وَالْبَنِينَ The love of [sensual] desires, including women and children, has been made appealing to mankind, until the end of the Ayah. Then He said: And the people of Paradise do not enjoy anything in Paradise that is more desirable to them than sexual intercourse (النكاح), neither food nor drink. It was said: He has alerted [us] through this Ayah to the ranks (مَرَاتِب) of His blessings, the lowest of which is the enjoyment (مَتَاع) of the world, and the highest of which is the beatitude (رِضْوَان) of Allah, due to His saying: وَرِضْوانٌ مِنَ اللهِ أَكْبَرُ (and Allah's beatitude is greater), and the middle of which is Paradise and its bliss (نَعِيم).

(1) Akhdajat (أَخْدَجَتْ): Its rain decreased, and [it refers to] the she-camel [giving birth] to a deficient offspring, even if it was complete, it is mukhrij (مُخْدَج) [Q].

** الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنا إِنَّنا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنا ذُنُوبَنا وَقِنا عَذابَ النَّارِ (16) Those who say, 'Our Lord, we have indeed believed. So forgive us our sins, and save us from the punishment of the Fire.' ** الصَّابِرِينَ وَالصَّادِقِينَ وَالْقانِتِينَ وَالْمُنْفِقِينَ وَالْمُسْتَغْفِرِينَ بِالْأَسْحارِ (17) The patient, the truthful, the obedient, the charitable, and those who plead for forgiveness at dawn. [They are] those who pray at the time of dawn (السحر). This is according to al-Majma' narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). He (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever seeks forgiveness seventy times at the time of dawn (السحر) is among the people of this Ayah." And in al-Faqih and al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever says in his witr prayer, when he performs the witr, 'استغفر الله وأتوب إليه' (I seek forgiveness from Allah and I repent to Him) seventy times while standing, and persists in this until a year passes for him, Allah will record him with Him among the الْمُسْتَغْفِرِينَ بِالْأَسْحارِ (those who plead for forgiveness at dawn), and forgiveness from Allah the Almighty will become incumbent upon him." It is said that the specification of the dawns (الأسحار) is because supplication therein is closer to being answered, for worship at that time is more difficult (أشق), the soul (النفس) is purer (أصفى), and the heart (الروع) is more focused (أجمع), especially for those who keep vigil (لِلْمُتَهَجِّدِينَ).

(١٨) شَهِدَ اللهُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ (18) Allah bears witness that there is no god except Him, He made clear His Oneness for a people by His manifestation in everything and His making His Essence known in every light and shade, and for a people by establishing the proofs (الدلائل) indicating it, and for a people by sending down the verses (الآيات) speaking of it. وَالْمَلائِكَةُ and the angels, (and the angels bear witness) by affirmation essentially (ذاتاً) for a people, actively (فعلاً) for a people, and verbally (قولاً) for a people. And those endowed with knowledge (أُولُوا الْعِلْمِ) (bear witness) by faith (الايمان), direct observation (العيان), and clear exposition (البيان). He likened the manifestation (الظهور) and the making manifest (الاظهار) in terms of unveiling (الانكشاف) and disclosure (الكشف) to the testimony of the witness (الشاهد). قائِماً بِالْقِسْطِ maintaining justice. (meaning) establishing justice (العدل).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Those endowed with knowledge (أُولُوا الْعِلْمِ) are the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Successors (الأوصياء), and they are maintainers of justice (قيام بالقسط), and al-Qist (القسط) is justice (العدل). لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ there is no god except Him, is an affirmation and a preparation for His saying: الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ. the All-mighty, the All-wise.

(١٩) إِنَّ الدِّينَ عِنْدَ اللهِ الْإِسْلامُ (19) Indeed, the religion with Allah is Islam. There is no religion acceptable (مرضي) to Allah except the religion of Islam, which is al-Tawhid (monotheism) and seeking protection (التدرع) through the Law (الشرع) brought by Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Islam precedes al-Iman (faith), and based on it, they inherit from one another and intermarry, while based on al-Iman, they are rewarded."

وَمَا اخْتَلَفَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ And those who were given the Book did not differ regarding Islam ** إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ بَغْياً بَيْنَهُمْ except after knowledge had come to them, out of envy among themselves. out of envy (حسداً) and seeking leadership (طلباً للرئاسة), not due to any ambiguity (شبهة) in it. وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِآياتِ اللهِ فَإِنَّ اللهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ And whoever denies the signs of Allah—Allah is indeed swift at reckoning. This is a threat (وعيد) to those among them who disbelieve.

(٢٠) فَإِنْ حَاجُّوكَ (3:20) Then if they argue with you concerning the religion (الدين) and dispute with you regarding it, after you have established the proofs (الحجج) for them, ** فَقُلْ أَسْلَمْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّهِ (3:20) say, 'I have submitted my will to Allah, meaning: I have sincerely devoted my soul (نفسي) and my entirety to Him; I do not associate anyone else with Him in it. It was said that He expressed the soul (النفس) using the face (الوجه) because it is the noblest of the apparent limbs (الأعضاء الظاهرة) and the manifestation of the powers (القوى) and the senses (الحواس). وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِ (3:20) and [so has] anyone who follows me.' and whoever followed me has submitted (أسلم). ** وَقُلْ لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ وَالْأُمِّيِّينَ (3:20) And say to those who were given the Book and the illiterates, those who have no Book (كتاب) like the polytheists (مشركي) of the Arabs, ** أَسْلَمْتُمْ ** (3:20) 'Have you submitted?' as I have submitted, now that the proof (الحجة) has been made clear to you? Or are you still upon your disbelief (كفركم)? And similar to this is His saying: ** فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ فَإِنْ أَسْلَمُوا فَقَدِ اهْتَدَوْا (3:20) Then if they submit, they are certainly guided, they have benefited themselves by removing themselves from error (الضلال). ** وَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّما عَلَيْكَ الْبَلاغُ (3:20) but if they turn away, then your duty is only to communicate. they have not harmed you, since your duty is only to communicate (تبلّغ), and you have already communicated. وَاللهُ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبادِ (3:20) And Allah is watchful over His servants. [This implies] promise (وعد) and threat (وعيد).

(٢١) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ النَّبِيِّينَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَيَقْتُلُونَ الَّذِينَ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْقِسْطِ مِنَ النَّاسِ فَبَشِّرْهُمْ بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ (21) Indeed those who defy the signs of Allah and kill the prophets unjustly, and kill those who enjoin justice among the people—inform them of a painful punishment.

It was said that they are the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) whose predecessors killed the prophets and their followers from the worshippers (عباد) of the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل) during his era صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And they were pleased with this and intended to kill the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers, but Allah protected them. A similar instance has preceded in Surat al-Baqarah. And it was read (يقاتلون الذين) (who fight those who...).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked which people would receive the severest punishment on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). He said: "A man who killed a prophet, or a man who enjoined the good (أمر بمعروف) or forbade the evil (نهى عن منكر)." Then He recited: ** وَيَقْتُلُونَ النَّبِيِّينَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَيَقْتُلُونَ الَّذِينَ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْقِسْطِ مِنَ النَّاسِ** (and kill the prophets unjustly, and kill those who enjoin justice among the people). Then He said: "The Children of Israel killed forty-three prophets at the beginning of the day in a single hour."

Then one hundred and twelve men rose up from the worshippers (عباد) of the Children of Israel, and they enjoined the good upon those who killed them and forbade them from the evil (نهوهم عن المنكر). So, they were all killed by the end of the day on that same day. And this is what Allah the Almighty mentioned.

** أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ** (22) They are the ones whose works have failed in this world and the Hereafter.

since they did not attain through them (their works) praise and commendation, and their blood and wealth were not protected (تحقن), and they did not deserve through them the reward (الأجر) and recompense (الثواب). وَما لَهُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ and they have no helpers

who might ward off the punishment (العذاب) from them.

(٢٣) ** أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا نَصِيباً مِنَ الْكِتابِ** (23) Have you not regarded those who were given a share of the Book?

It was said that He means by it the Jewish scholars (أحبار اليهود) who were given an ample share of the Torah (التوراة) or from the category of the revealed Books (الكتب المنزلة).

** يُدْعَوْنَ إِلى كِتابِ اللهِ** (They are invited to the Book of Allah)

which is the Torah (التوراة).

** لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ** (that it may judge between them)

It was said that He means concerning the prophethood of our Prophet. And it was said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered their study house (مدرسهم) and invited them. So some of them said to Him: "Upon which religion are you?" He said: "Upon the creed (ملّة) of Abraham عليه السلام." They said: "Indeed, Abraham was a Jew." He said: "Indeed, the Torah (التوراة) is between us and you," but they refused. And it was said that it was revealed concerning stoning (الرّجم), and they differed concerning it, and it has a story whose mention will come when interpreting His saying, the Glorified:

** يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ قَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولُنا يُبَيِّنُ لَكُمْ كَثِيراً مِمَّا كُنْتُمْ تُخْفُونَ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** O People of the Book! Our Apostle has certainly come to you, clarifying for you much of what you used to conceal of the Book,

from Surat al-Ma'idah.

ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّى فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمْ then a faction of them turns away,

expressing astonishment at their turning away, despite their knowledge that returning to the Book of Allah is obligatory (واجب).

** وَهُمْ مُعْرِضُونَ** and they are disregardful.

from following the truth (الحق).

(24) ** ذلِكَ That turning away (التولّي) and aversion (الاعراض) ** بِأَنَّهُمْ قالُوا لَنْ تَمَسَّنَا النَّارُ إِلَّا أَيَّاماً مَعْدُوداتٍ (24) That is because they said, 'The Fire will not touch us except for a number of days,' due to their making the punishment light upon themselves. ** وَغَرَّهُمْ فِي دِينِهِمْ ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ and what they used to fabricate deceived them in their religion. namely, that the Fire will not touch them except for a few days, or that their fathers, the Prophets (الأنبياء), will intercede for them, or that He, the Exalted, promised Jacob (يعقوب) that He would not punish his children except for the fulfillment of an oath—meaning His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ ** 'I will surely fill hell with jinn and humans, all together.' and what is alluded to by His saying, the Glorified: وَإِنْ مِنْكُمْ إِلَّا وارِدُها.

(٢٥) فَكَيْفَ إِذا جَمَعْناهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ (25) How will it be when We gather them for a day in which there is no doubt, [This is] a magnification of what will befall them in the Hereafter, and a refutation of their saying: لَنْ تَمَسَّنَا النَّارُ إِلَّا أَيَّاماً (The Fire shall not touch us except for a few days). It is narrated: The first banner of the banners of disbelief to be raised on the Day of Resurrection is the banner of the Jews, and Allah will disgrace them before the witnesses, then He will command them to the Fire. وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ما كَسَبَتْ and every soul will be paid in full for what it has earned, [meaning] the recompense for what it earned, وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ and they will not be wronged.

(٢٦) قُلِ اللهُمَ (26) Say: 'O Allah,' The mīm (الميم) in it is a substitute for the yā’ (ياء), and for that reason, they do not combine. ** مالِكَ الْمُلْكِ Owner of all sovereignty! Meaning, He owns the genus of sovereignty (جنس الملك). He disposes of it with the disposal of owners over what they possess. ** تُؤْتِي الْمُلْكَ You grant sovereignty You give what You will of sovereignty ** مَنْ تَشاءُ وَتَنْزِعُ الْمُلْكَ to whomever You wish, and You strip sovereignty You reclaim what You will of it ** مِمَّنْ تَشاءُ from whomever You wish. So the first sovereignty (الملك) is general, while the latter two are specific, being parts of the whole. وَتُعِزُّ مَنْ تَشاءُ You exalt whomever You wish, in this world (الدنيا) and in the religion (الدين). ** وَتُذِلُّ مَنْ تَشاءُ بِيَدِكَ الْخَيْرُ ** and You abase whomever You wish. All good is in Your hand. You grant it to Your allies (أولياءك) despite Your enemies (أعدائك). ** إِنَّكَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ.** Indeed You have power over all things.

(٢٧) تُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهارِ وَتُولِجُ النَّهارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ (27) You make the night pass into the day, and You make the day pass into the night. [This means] that You decrease the night and make that decrease an increase in the day, and You decrease the day and make that decrease an increase in the night. ** وَتُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ (and You bring forth the living from the dead) means the believer (المؤمن) from the disbeliever (الكافر), and وَتُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِ (and You bring forth the dead from the living) means the disbeliever (الكافر) from the believer (المؤمن). This is according to al-Majma' narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both).

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, when the believer (المؤمن) dies, he is not dead (ميتاً), and the dead (الميت) is only the disbeliever (الكافر)." Then He (peace be upon him) interpreted the verse with what was mentioned [above]. وَتَرْزُقُ مَنْ تَشاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ (and You provide for whomever You wish without reckoning) means without restriction (بلا تقتير) and without fear of decrease (ولا مخافة نقصان).

(28) لا يَتَّخِذِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الْكافِرِينَ أَوْلِياءَ (The believers must not take the faithless for allies) They were forbidden from befriending (موالاتهم) them due to kinship, or pre-Islamic friendship (صداقة جاهلية), or similar reasons, so that their love and hatred are only for the sake of Allah. This has been repeated in the Quran: ** لا تَتَّخِذُوا الْيَهُودَ وَالنَّصارى أَوْلِياءَ لا تَجِدُ قَوْماً يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ (Do not take the Jews and the Christians for allies. You will not find a people who believe in Allah and the Last Day) ...the Ayah. And love for the sake of Allah (الحب في الله) and hatred for the sake of Allah (البغض في الله) is a great principle (اصل كبير) among the principles (أصول) of faith (الايمان). ** مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (instead of the believers) The meaning is that in befriending the believers (موالاة المؤمنين), they have sufficient scope (مندوحة) to dispense with befriending the faithless (موالاة الكافرين), so they should not prefer them over the believers. ** وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَ اللهِ فِي شَيْءٍ (and whoever does that has nothing to do with Allah) Meaning, he has nothing to do with the guardianship (ولاية) of Allah in any way; that is, he is completely stripped (منسلخ) of the guardianship of Allah. This is a rational matter, because befriending a friend and befriending his enemy are contradictory, as it was said:

You love my enemy, then you claim that I am

your friend—indeed, your judgment (الرأي) is far astray. ** إِلَّا أَنْ تَتَّقُوا مِنْهُمْ تُقاةً (unless you fear a danger from them) Except that you fear from their direction a fear (خوفاً) or a matter that must be feared. It was also recited as تَقِيَّةً (Taqiyyah). They are forbidden from befriending them outwardly and inwardly at all times, except at the time of fear (وقت المخافة). For, displaying friendship (إظهار الموالاة) at that time is permissible due to the necessity of opposition (بالمخالفة), as it was said: "Be in the middle, but walk on the side."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "And He commanded you to employ al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة) in your religion, for Allah says: ** لا يَتَّخِذِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ...the Ayah." He (peace be upon him) said: "Beware, and again beware, of exposing yourself to destruction and of abandoning al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة) which I have commanded you to observe. For then you would be shedding your own blood and the blood of your brethren, exposing your blessings and their blessings to removal, and humiliating them in the hands of the enemies of Allah's religion, while Allah the Almighty has commanded you to honor them (بإعزازهم)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to say: "There is no faith (إيمان) for one who has no Taqiyyah (تقيّة)." And He (peace be upon him) would say: Allah said: ** إِلَّا أَنْ تَتَّقُوا مِنْهُمْ تُقاةً.** (unless you fear a danger from them.)

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة) is Allah's shield (ترس) between Him and His creation.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة) applies to everything that the son of Adam is compelled to do, and which Allah has made lawful for him. And the narrations (الأخبار) concerning this are innumerable.

** وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللهُ نَفْسَهُ وَإِلَى اللهِ الْمَصِيرُ (And Allah warns you to beware of Himself, and toward Allah is the ultimate return.) So do not expose yourselves to His wrath (سخطه) by violating His decrees (أحكامه) and befriending His enemies. This is a great threat (تهديد عظيم) and a severe warning (وعيد شديد).

(٢٩) قُلْ إِنْ تُخْفُوا ما فِي صُدُورِكُمْ (Say: 'Whether you hide what is in your breasts) regarding the allegiance to the disbelievers and other things, ** أَوْ تُبْدُوهُ يَعْلَمْهُ اللهُ (or disclose it, Allah knows it.) He does not fail regarding it, وَيَعْلَمُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (And He knows whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth,) so He knows your secret and your manifest deeds. ** وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (and Allah is powerful over all things.') so He is capable of punishing you if you do not cease from what you were forbidden from. It is said the Ayah is an explanation for the Almighty's saying: وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللهُ نَفْسَهُ (And Allah warns you of Himself). It is as if He said: He warns you of Himself because He is characterized by intrinsic knowledge (علم ذاتي) that encompasses all known things (المعلومات كلها) and intrinsic power (قدرة ذاتية) that covers all capabilities (المقدورات) entirely. So, do not dare to disobey Him, for there is no act of disobedience except that He is aware of it, and capable of punishing for it.

(٣٠) يَوْمَ تَجِدُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ما عَمِلَتْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ مُحْضَراً وَما عَمِلَتْ مِنْ سُوءٍ تَوَدُّ لَوْ أَنَّ بَيْنَها وَبَيْنَهُ أَمَداً بَعِيداً**. (30) The day every soul will find present whatever good it has done, and whatever evil it has done, it will wish there were a great distance between it and that [evil].

يَوْمَ ** is the ظرف (adverb of time/circumstance) for تَوَدُّ (it will wish). That is, every soul will wish on the Day when it finds the scrolls of its deeds, or the recompense of its deeds, whether good or evil, present, ** لَوْ أَنَّ بَيْنَها (if there were between it) and that [evil deed], which is referred to by the pronoun هُ (it), ** أَمَداً بَعِيداً (a distant span). Or (it is related to) an implied word, such as (اذكر - remember). And تَوَدُّ (it will wish) is a حال (circumstantial qualifier) from the pronoun in عَمِلَتْ مِنْ سُوءٍ (it has done evil), or it is the خبر (predicate) for ** ما عَمِلَتْ مِنْ سُوءٍ.

And تَجِدُ (it will find) is restricted to ** ما عَمِلَتْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ (whatever good it has done). ** وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللهُ نَفْسَهُ ** (And Allah warns you of Himself) is repeated for emphasis (للتأكيد) and reminder (والتذكير). ** وَاللهُ رَؤُفٌ بِالْعِبادِ (And Allah is All-kind to the servants) is an indication that He, the Exalted, only forbade them and warned them out of kindness (رأفة) toward them and consideration (مراعاة) for their well-being (صلاحهم). And that He is indeed the Possessor of forgiveness (مغفرة) and the Possessor of punishment (عقاب); His mercy is hoped for and His punishment is feared.

(٣١) قُلْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ اللهَ فَاتَّبِعُونِي يُحْبِبْكُمُ اللهُ. (31) Say, 'If you love Allah, then follow me, that Allah may love you.'

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Is the religion anything but love?" Then He recited this Ayah.

I say: Love (المحبة) from the servant is the inclination of the soul toward a thing due to a perfection (كمال) it perceives in it, such that this inclination carries the soul toward that which brings it closer to that thing. And love from Allah is His satisfaction (رضاه) with the servant and the removal of the veil (كشف الحجاب) from his heart. When the servant knows that true perfection (الكمال الحقيقي) belongs only to Allah, and that everything he perceives as perfection (كمال) in himself or others is from Allah, through Allah, and unto Allah, then his love (حبّه) will be only for Allah and in Allah. This necessitates the desire for His obedience (طاعته) and the yearning for that which brings him closer to Him. Thus, the sign (علامة) of love (المحبّة) is the desire for obedience (الطاعة) and worship (العبادة), and the utmost striving (الاجتهاد البليغ) in following the one who was a means (وسيلة) for him to the knowledge of Allah the Almighty and His love—namely, the one who was knowledgeable of Allah (عارفاً بالله), loving Him, and beloved by Him. For indeed, whoever possesses these attributes only attained them through obedience (الطاعة) in the specific manner (الوجه المخصوص), and that is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and those who follow his path (يحذو حذوه). So, whoever loves Allah must necessarily follow the Messenger in his worship (عبادته), conduct (سيرته), character (أخلاقه), and states (أحواله), until Allah loves him. For it is through this that proximity (التقرّب) to Allah is achieved, and through proximity, the love of Allah the Almighty for him is achieved, just as He the Almighty said: "And My servant continues to draw near to Me through supererogatory acts (النوافل) until I love him."

Also, since the Messenger is the Beloved (حبيب) of Allah, everyone who claims the love (محبّة) of Allah must necessarily love the Messenger, because the beloved of the Beloved is beloved. The love of the Messenger is only realized through following him and traversing his path (سبيله) in word (قولاً), deed (عملاً), character (خلقاً), state (حالاً), conduct (سيرة), and belief (عقيدة). The claim of loving Allah cannot be realized except through this, for he (the Messenger) is the axis (قطب) of love and its manifestation (مظهرها). Whoever does not have a share (نصيب) in following him will not have a share in love. Whoever follows him with true following (حقّ المتابعة), his inner being (باطنه), his secret (سرّه), his heart (قلبه), and his soul (نفسه) will correspond to the inner being, secret, heart, and soul of the Messenger. And He (the Messenger) is the manifestation (مظهر) of Allah's love. Thus, due to this correspondence (المناسبة), it is necessary that this follower receives a portion (قسط) of Allah's love commensurate with his share of the following. So, Allah casts His love upon him, and the light (نور) of that love flows from the inner being of the Messenger to him, making him beloved by Allah and loving Him. Whoever does not follow him, his inner being contradicts the inner being of the Messenger, so he distances himself from the description of being beloved (وصف المحبوبية), and the removal of love from his heart is as swift as can be, for if Allah did not love him, he would not be loving Him. And those whom Allah and the Messenger commanded to be loved and followed are in the same ruling as the Messenger, and they are the Imams, the Successors (الأئمة الأوصياء) (peace be upon them).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "Whoever is pleased to know that Allah loves him, let him act in obedience to Allah and follow us. Have you not heard the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: قُلْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ اللهَ فَاتَّبِعُونِي يُحْبِبْكُمُ اللهُ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ (Say, 'If you love Allah, then follow me, that Allah may love you and forgive you your sins')? By Allah, no servant ever obeys Allah except that Allah includes our following (اتباعنا) in his obedience. And by Allah, no servant ever follows us except that Allah loves him. And by Allah, no one ever abandons following us except that he hates us. And by Allah, no one ever hates us except that he disobeys Allah. And whoever dies disobeying Allah, Allah will disgrace him and cast him upon his face in the Fire."

وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ (and forgive you your sins) by overlooking what has passed from you. ** وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful) toward whoever seeks His love through His obedience and the following of those whom Allah and His Prophet commanded to be followed. It is narrated that it was revealed when the Jews said: "We are the sons of Allah and His beloved ones." And it is said that it was revealed concerning the delegation of Najran when they said: "We only worship the Messiah out of love for Allah." And it is said that it was revealed concerning groups of people during his era صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم who claimed that they loved Allah, so they were commanded to provide confirmation (تصديقاً) for their claim through action (العمل).

** قُلْ أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَالرَّسُولَ فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا ** (Say, 'Obey Allah and the Apostle.' But if they turn their backs,) It admits of the past tense (المضيّ) and the present/future tense (المضارعة), meaning, "But if you turn away (فَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا)." فَإِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْكافِرِينَ (then Allah does not love the faithless.) He is not pleased with them, nor does He praise them. It was said that He did not say, "Nor does He love them" (referring to the specific people who turned away) for the purpose of generality and to indicate that turning away (التولي) is disbelief (كفر), and that by this aspect (الحيثيّة), it negates the love of Allah, the Almighty, and that His love is specific to the believers (المؤمنين).

(٣٣) إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى آدَمَ وَنُوحاً وَآلَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَآلَ عِمْرانَ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ (33) Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah, the progeny of Abraham and the progeny of Imran above all the nations.

(He chose them) through the prophethood (الرسالة), the spiritual characteristics (الخصائص الروحانية), and the physical merits (الفضائل الجسمانية). For this reason, they were strengthened for what others were not strengthened for. Since He made obedience to the Messengers obligatory and clarified that it is what brings about the love of Allah, He followed that up by explaining their merits, encouraging (obedience) to them. By this (Ayah), evidence is derived for their superiority over the angels.

And the progeny of Abraham (آل إبراهيم) are Ishmael and Isaac and their children. And the progeny of Imran (آل عمران) are Moses and Aaron, the sons of Imran ibn Yishar (يصهر) ibn Qahath (فاهث) ibn Levi ibn Jacob; and Jesus and his mother Mary (peace be upon her), the daughter of Imran ibn Matthan (ماثان). Matthan ends with twenty-seven fathers up to Judah (يهود) ibn Jacob. Between the two Imrans (العمرانين) there are one thousand eight hundred years, so it is said.

I say: Our Prophet and his Household (peace be upon them) have certainly entered into the progeny of Abraham (آل إبراهيم).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He recited this Ayah and then said: "We are from them, and we are the remainder (بقيّة) of that progeny (العترة)."

In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Muhammad ibn Ash'ath ibn Qays al-Kindi (may the curse of Allah be upon him) said to al-Husayn (peace be upon him): "O Husayn, son of Fatimah (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both), what sanctity (حرمة) do you have from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that others do not have?" Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) then recited this Ayah: إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى آدَمَ وَنُوحاً وَآلَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَآلَ عِمْرانَ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ ذُرِّيَّةً بَعْضُها مِنْ بَعْضٍ (Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah, the progeny of Abraham and the progeny of Imran above all the nations, (34) a progeny, some of them derived from the others) until the end of the Ayah. Then he said: "By Allah, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is certainly from the progeny of Abraham (آل إبراهيم), and the Guiding Progeny (العترة الهادية) are certainly from the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them)."

In al-Uyun, in the narration concerning the difference between the Progeny (العترة) and the Nation (الأمّة): Al-Ma’mun asked: "Did Allah favor the Progeny (العترة) over the rest of mankind?" Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) replied: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty made clear the superiority of the Progeny (العترة) over the rest of mankind in the decisive verses (محكم) of His Book." Al-Ma’mun asked Him: "Where is that in the Book of Allah?" Al-Rida (peace be upon him) replied to him: "In His saying, the Almighty: إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى آدَمَ وَنُوحاً وَآلَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَآلَ عِمْرانَ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ (Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah, the progeny of Abraham and the progeny of Imran above all the nations)."

And al-Qummi said that the Scholar (العالم) (peace be upon him) said: It was revealed: وَآلَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَآلَ عِمْرانَ and the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ (above all the nations), but they dropped the progeny of Muhammad (peace be upon them) from the Book.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "And the progeny of Muhammad (آل محمّد) was there, but they erased it."

In al-Majma': And in the recitation (قراءة) of the Household (أهل البيت): "and the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ (above all the nations)."

They also said that the progeny of Abraham (peace be upon them) are the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), who are his Household (أهله). And it is necessary that those whom Allah the Almighty chose be purified (مطهّرين), infallible (معصومين), and free from vices (القبائح), because He, glory be to Him, does not choose or select anyone except those who are thus. End of his statement.

I say: Based on this recitation (القراءة), it would be of the type where the specific is conjoined to the general (عطف الخاص على العام), like the conjunction of the progeny of Imran (آل عمران), in both its meanings, to (the progeny of) Abraham (peace be upon them).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the meaning of the progeny of Muhammad (peace be upon them). He replied: "The progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) are those whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, forbade Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from marrying."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) are His descendants (ذريته), His Household (أهل بيته), the Imams (الأئمة), the Successors (الأوصياء), and His Progeny (عترته), the Companions of the Cloak (أصحاب العباء). And His Nation (أمّته) are the believers who affirmed what He brought from Allah, those who adhere firmly to the Two Weighty Things (الثقلين) which they were commanded to adhere to: the Book of Allah and His Progeny (عترته), His Household (أهل بيته), whom Allah removed all impurity (الرجس) from and purified them with a thorough purification (تطهيراً). And they are the two successors (الخليفتان) over the Nation (الأمة) after Him."

(٣٤) ذُرِّيَّةً بَعْضُها مِنْ بَعْضٍ (34) a progeny, some of them from the others. The progeny (الذّرّية) applies to both the singular and the plural. It means that they are a single, continuous progeny (ذريّة واحدة متسلسلة), some of which branches off from others (متشعّبة من بعض).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in His explanation: "Indeed, those whom Allah chose (اصطفاهم) are some of them from the lineage (نسل) of others."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "What is the proof (الحجة) in the Book of Allah that the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد) are His people (أهل بيته), the blessings of Allah be upon them?" He said: "The saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى آدَمَ وَنُوحاً وَآلَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَآلَ عِمْرانَ (33) Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah, the progeny of Abraham and the progeny of Imran. And the Household of Muhammad (وآل محمد)—it was revealed thus: عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ ذُرِّيَّةً بَعْضُها مِنْ بَعْضٍ وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (33-34) over all the nations, (34) a progeny, some of them from the others. And Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing." He (peace be upon him) said: "And the progeny (الذّرّية) of a people is not except their offspring (نسلهم) from their loins (أصلابهم)."

وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ And Allah is all-hearing of the sayings (أقوال) of people, ** عَلِيمٌ all-knowing of their deeds (أعمالهم). Thus, He chooses (فيصطفي) whoever is upright in saying and deed.

إِذْ قالَتِ (35) When she said Mention when she said—the All-hearing of the saying of the wife of Imran, the All-knowing of her intention (بنيّتها).

(٣٥) إِذْ قالَتِ امْرَأَتُ عِمْرانَ (35) When the wife of 'Imran said She is the wife of 'Imran son of Mathan, the mother of Maryam the Virgin, the grandmother of 'Isa, daughter of Qafudha. The well-known (opinion) is that her name is Hannah (حنّة), as will come (in a narration) from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He said to a Christian man, "As for the mother of Maryam, her name is Marthar, and she is Wahibah (وهيبة) in Arabic." رَبِّ إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لَكَ ما فِي بَطْنِي مُحَرَّراً 'My Lord, I dedicate to You as a vow what is in my belly, to be set free [for Your service].' set free (معتقاً) for the service of the Holy House (بيت المقدّس), I shall not occupy him with anything. ** فَتَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي So accept it from me. what I have vowed. ** إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ السَّمِيعُ Indeed You are the All-hearing, of my saying. ** الْعَلِيمُ** the All-knowing. of my intention.

(٣٦) فَلَمَّا وَضَعَتْها قالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّي وَضَعْتُها أُنْثى وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما وَضَعَتْ (36) When she delivered her, she said, 'My Lord, I have delivered a female'—and Allah knew better what she had delivered—

This is an interjection (اعتراض). And the Almighty's saying وَلَيْسَ الذَّكَرُ كَالْأُنْثى (and the male is not like the female) is a continuation of the speech of the wife of 'Imran. And it was read as بما وَضَعْتُ (what I delivered), meaning it is part of her speech, comforting herself, meaning: perhaps Allah has a secret in it, or the female was better.

And he narrated it in al-Majma' from 'Ali (peace be upon him), and in al-Kafi and al-Qummi from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "Indeed, Allah revealed to 'Imran: 'I am granting you a sound, blessed male who will heal the born-blind (الأكمه) and the leper (الأبرص) and revive the dead by Allah's permission, and I will make Him a Messenger to the Children of Israel.' So 'Imran informed his wife, Hannah, who is the mother of Maryam, of this. When she became pregnant with her, she thought her fetus was a boy. So when she delivered her, she said: رَبِّ إِنِّي وَضَعْتُها أُنْثى وَلَيْسَ الذَّكَرُ كَالْأُنْثى (My Lord, I have delivered a female, and the male is not like the female) because a girl cannot be a Messenger. The Almighty Allah says: وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما وَضَعَتْ (and Allah knew better what she had delivered). When Allah granted Maryam 'Isa (peace be upon him), He was the one about whom 'Imran was given the glad tidings and whom He was promised. Therefore, if we say something concerning a man among us, and it is fulfilled in his child or his grandchild, do not deny that."

And al-'Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) something similar to it.

And from al-Sadiq (may the blessings of Allah be upon him): "Indeed, the consecrated one (المحرّر) remains in the church and does not leave it. ** فَلَمَّا وَضَعَتْها قالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّي وَضَعْتُها أُنْثى وَلَيْسَ الذَّكَرُ كَالْأُنْثى (When she delivered her, she said, 'My Lord, I have delivered a female, and the male is not like the female') because the female menstruates, so she must leave the mosque, whereas the consecrated one (المحرّر) does not leave the mosque." And from one of the two (peace be upon them): "She vowed what was in her womb for the church, that he would serve the worshippers. ** وَلَيْسَ الذَّكَرُ كَالْأُنْثى (and the male is not like the female) in service." He said: She grew up (نشبت) (1) and used to serve them and contend with them until she reached maturity. Then Zakariyya was commanded to make a curtain (حجاباً) for her, separating her from the worshippers.

وَإِنِّي سَمَّيْتُها مَرْيَمَ (and I have named her Maryam)

She said that seeking nearness (تقرّباً) to Allah and requesting that He protect her and make her righteous, so that her actions would correspond to her name, for Maryam in their language means the worshipper (العابدة).

** وَإِنِّي أُعِيذُها بِكَ وَذُرِّيَّتَها** (and I commend her and her progeny to Your protection)

I grant her refuge through Your preservation.

مِنَ الشَّيْطانِ الرَّجِيمِ (from the accursed Satan)

The expelled one (المطرود). The origin of al-rajm (الرّجم) is throwing stones.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace): "There is no newborn child except that Satan touches him when he is born, so he cries out screaming from his touch, except Maryam and her son." It was said that its meaning is that Satan covets the misguidance (إغواء) of every newborn, such that he is affected by Satan's covetousness for him, except Maryam and her son, for Allah protected them by the blessing of this seeking of refuge (الاستعاذة).

(٣٧) ** فَتَقَبَّلَها رَبُّها بِقَبُولٍ حَسَنٍ (37) Then her Lord accepted her with a gracious acceptance,**

meaning, He was pleased with her in fulfillment of the vow, in place of the male child, with a gracious manner by which vows are accepted, which was establishing her in the place of the male child and receiving her immediately after her birth, before she grew up and became fit for the service (للسّدانة). It is narrated: When Hannah (حنّة) gave birth to her, she wrapped her in a cloth, carried her to the Mosque, and placed her with the scholars (الأحبار), saying: "Take this vowed offering." They competed for her because she was the daughter of their Imam and the keeper of their sacrifice, for the Children of Mathan (بني ماثان) were the chiefs and kings of the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل). So Zachariah (زكريا) said: "I have a greater right to her, as her maternal aunt is with me." But they refused, insisting on casting lots. They were twenty-seven men. They went to a river and cast their pens (أقلامهم) into it. Zachariah's pen floated, and their pens sank. So, he took charge of her.

I say: In the narration of our companions (أصحابنا): the wife of Zachariah was her sister, not her maternal aunt. This was narrated by al-Qummi (القمّيّ) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). And there will come from Tafsir al-Imam (تفسير الإمام) also that which indicates this.

وَأَنْبَتَها نَباتاً حَسَناً and made her grow in a good manner,

This is a metaphor for raising her in a way that perfected her in all her states.

وَكَفَّلَها (and committed her to the care of) (It is read with tashdid (بالتشديد), meaning Allah committed her to the care of)

** زَكَرِيَّا Zachariah.**

(It is read with al-qasr (بالقصر) wherever it occurs)

** كُلَّما دَخَلَ عَلَيْها زَكَرِيَّا الْمِحْرابَ Whenever Zachariah entered the sanctuary to [see] her,**

meaning, the chamber (الغرفة) that was built for her, or the Mosque, or its most honorable and foremost place. It was named thus because it is the place where the devil is fought (محلّ محاربة الشيطان), as if she was placed in the most honorable spot in the Holy House (بيت المقدس).

وَجَدَ عِنْدَها رِزْقاً he found provisions with her.

This is the answer (جواب) to ** كُلَّما** (Whenever). It is narrated: No one but him would enter upon her, and when he left, he would lock seven doors upon her. Yet, he would find with her the fruits of winter in summer, and vice versa.

I say: And the like of this will come in the narration of our companions (أصحابنا).

** قالَ يا مَرْيَمُ أَنَّى لَكِ هذا He said, 'O Mary, whence comes this to you?'**

meaning, from where do you have this provision that comes out of its season, while the doors are locked upon you?

** قالَتْ هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ She said, 'It is from Allah.**

so do not deem it unlikely.

** إِنَّ اللهَ يَرْزُقُ مَنْ يَشاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ Indeed Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning.'**

Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: Indeed, Fatimah (فاطمة) guaranteed to Ali (peace be upon him) the household chores, kneading, baking, and sweeping the house. And Ali (peace be upon him) guaranteed to her what was outside the door, such as carrying firewood and bringing food. One day, he said to her: "O Fatimah, do you have anything?" She replied: "No, by Him Who has magnified your right, we have had nothing for three days with which to feed you." He said: "Why did you not inform me?" She said: "The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) forbade me from asking you for anything. He said: 'Do not ask your cousin for anything; if he brings you something freely, [take it], otherwise, do not ask him.'" He (Ali, peace be upon him) said: So Ali (peace be upon him) went out and met a man and borrowed a dinar (دينار) from him. Then he returned when it was evening and met Miqdad ibn al-Aswad (مقداد بن الأسود). He said to al-Miqdad: "What brought you out at this hour?" He replied: "Hunger, by Him Who has magnified your right, O Commander of the Faithful (يا أمير المؤمنين)." He said: "That is what brought me out, and I have borrowed a dinar and I shall prefer you with it." So, he gave it to him. Then he returned and found the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) sitting, and Fatimah was praying, and between them was something covered. When she finished, she uncovered it, and behold, it was a bowl (جفنة) of bread and meat. He said: "O Fatimah, whence comes this to you?" She said, 'It is from Allah. Indeed Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning.' Then the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: "Shall I not tell you about your likeness and her likeness?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Like Zachariah (زكريا) when he entered the sanctuary (المحراب) to [see] Mary (مريم) and he found provisions with her. He said, 'O Mary, whence comes this to you?' She said, 'It is from Allah. Indeed Allah provides for whomever He wishes without any reckoning.'" So, they ate from it for a month, and it is the bowl (الجفنة) from which the Qa'im (القائم) will eat, and it is with us.

In al-Kafi (الكافي), this narration is presented in another manner, and through the path of the general populace (العامّة), it is presented in a third manner, which al-Zamakhshari (الزمخشري) and al-Baydawi (البيضاوي) and others have included in their Tafsirs (تفاسيرهم).

footnote (١) نشب في الشيء إذا وقع فيما لا مخلص له ونشب الشيء في الشيء من باب تعب نشوباً علق به فهو ناشب (مجمع) (1) نشب في الشيء (nashiba fi al-shay’) means when one falls into something from which there is no escape. And نشب الشيء في الشيء (nashiba al-shay’ fi al-shay’) is from the pattern of تعب (taʿiba), نشوباً (nushūban), meaning it clung to it, so it is ناشب (nāshib) ( مجمع - Majma').

(٣٨) هُنالِكَ In that place (المكان) or time (الوقت), ** دَعا زَكَرِيَّا رَبَّهُ when he saw the honor (كرامة) of Maryam and her status (منزلتها) with Allah. (38) There Zachariah supplicated to his Lord.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): She was the most beautiful of women, and she used to pray, and the prayer niche (المحراب) would illuminate from her light. Then Zachariah entered upon her, and behold, she had the fruit of winter in summer and the fruit of summer in winter. So he said: أَنَّى لَكِ هذا (Whence is this for you?) She said: قالَتْ هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ (It is from Allah). هُنالِكَ دَعا زَكَرِيَّا رَبَّهُ.

And in Tafsir al-Imam regarding Surat al-Baqarah: Zachariah (peace be upon him) said to himself that the One Who is able to bring Maryam the fruit of winter in summer and the fruit of summer in winter is certainly able to grant me a child, even though I am an old man (شيخاً) and my wife is barren (عاقراً). So ** هُنالِكَ دَعا زَكَرِيَّا رَبَّهُ**.

قالَ رَبِّ هَبْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً (He said, 'My Lord! Grant me a good offspring from Your presence.')

a blessed child (ولداً مباركاً), just as You granted her to Hannah (حنة). It was said that he had Ishā’ (ايشاع), daughter of ‘Imrān ibn Māthān, the sister of Hannah (حنة). So he desired to have a child from her, like the child of her sister Hannah, in honor (الكرامة) with Allah. ** إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ الدُّعاءِ**. (Indeed You are the Hearer of supplication.)

(٣٩) فَنادَتْهُ And it was read (وقرئ) فناداه (in the masculine form, بالتذكير) ** الْمَلائِكَةُ وَهُوَ قائِمٌ يُصَلِّي فِي الْمِحْرابِ أَنَّ اللهَ ** And it was read (وقرئ) with the kasrah on the hamzah (بكسر الهمزة) ** يُبَشِّرُكَ And it was read (وقرئ) with the fathah on the ya' and the dammah on the shin (بفتح الياء وضم الشين), and likewise in what follows بِيَحْيى مُصَدِّقاً بِكَلِمَةٍ مِنَ اللهِ meaning, by Jesus (بعيسى), as will come shortly. وَسَيِّداً (and a master) who will rule over his people (يسود قومهم) and surpass them (ويفوقهم). And he was superior (فائقاً) to all people in that he never intended (ما همّ) a disobedience (بمعصية).

In Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him): It means a chief (رئيساً) in obedience to Allah over the people of His obedience.

وَحَصُوراً (and chaste/restrainer) meaning one who greatly restrains (مبالغاً في حصر) the soul from desires (الشهوات) and amusements (والملاهي). It is narrated (روي): that in his childhood (في صباه) he passed by some boys who invited him to play, and he said: "I was not created for play." And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He is the one who does not approach women (لا يأتي النساء). The mention of the two narrations (الروايتين) will come in Surat Maryam, if Allah wills. وَنَبِيًّا مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ (and a prophet, among the righteous) being counted among them (كائناً من عدادهم) or arising from them (ناشئاً منهم).

(٣٩)** فَنادَتْهُ الْمَلائِكَةُ وَهُوَ قائِمٌ يُصَلِّي فِي الْمِحْرابِ أَنَّ اللهَ يُبَشِّرُكَ بِيَحْيى مُصَدِّقاً بِكَلِمَةٍ مِنَ اللهِ وَسَيِّداً وَحَصُوراً وَنَبِيًّا مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ ** (39) Thereupon the angels called out to him while he stood praying in the sanctuary: 'Allah gives you the good news of John, who will confirm a Word of Allah, and will be a master, and chaste, and a prophet, among the righteous.'

In Tafsir al-Imam, regarding His saying: وَاسْتَشْهِدُوا شَهِيدَيْنِ مِنْ رِجالِكُمْ (And call to witness two witnesses from among your men), Allah did not join any children (صبياناً) with men of complete intellect (رجال كاملي العقول) except these four: Jesus son of Mary, John son of Zechariah, and al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace and blessings be upon them). Then he mentioned their story, then he said: The first confirmation (أول تصديق) of John for Jesus was that Zechariah was the only one who would ascend to Mary in that sanctuary (الصومعة). He would ascend to her by a ladder, and when he descended, he would lock the door upon her. Then He opened a small aperture (كوة صغيرة) for her above the door, through which the wind would enter upon her. When he found Mary pregnant (وقد حبلت), that distressed him, and he said to himself: "No one but me used to ascend to her, and now she is pregnant! Now I will be disgraced among the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل); they will surely believe that I impregnated her." So he came to his wife and told her that. She said: "O Zechariah, do not fear, for Allah will only do good to you. Bring Mary to me so I may look at her and ask her about her condition." So Zechariah brought her to his wife. Allah spared Mary the burden (مؤنة) of answering the question. When she entered upon her sister (who was the elder, while Mary was the younger), Zechariah's wife did not stand up for her. So Allah the Almighty permitted John, while He was in his mother's womb, to strike his hand in her belly, startling her (وأزعجها), and He called out to her: "O mother! The Mistress of the women of the worlds (سيدة نساء العالمين) is entering upon you, carrying the Master of the men of the worlds (سيدة رجال العالمين), and you do not stand up for her?" So she was startled and stood up for her. And John, while He was in his mother's womb, prostrated (سجد) to Jesus son of Mary. That was his first confirmation of Him. That is the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them) that they are the masters of the youth of Paradise (سيدا شباب أهل الجنة), except for the two sons of the maternal aunt (ابني الخالة), Jesus and John.

** قالَ رَبِّ أَنَّى يَكُونُ لِي غُلامٌ (40) He said, 'My Lord, how shall I have a son [This is] an ordinary expression of finding something remote (استبعاد عادي) and an inquiry (استفهام). ** وَقَدْ بَلَغَنِيَ الْكِبَرُ when old age has overtaken me?' [It] has affected my age and weakened me. وَامْرَأَتِي عاقِرٌ (and my wife is barren), meaning she does not give birth (لا تلد), derived from al-ʿaqr (العقر) which means cutting off (القطع). قالَ كَذلِكَ (He said: "Thus"), meaning similar to the creation of a child from the decrepit old man (الشيخ الفاني) and the barren old woman (العجوز العاقر). ** اللهُ يَفْعَلُ ما يَشاءُ ... Allah does whatever He wishes.' [He does] from the wonders (العجائب) that break the norm (الخارقة للعادة).

(٤١) قالَ رَبِّ اجْعَلْ لِي آيَةً (41) He said, 'My Lord, appoint a sign for me.' a sign (علامة) by which I may know the pregnancy, so that I may receive it with gratitude (بالشكر). ** قالَ آيَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ النَّاسَ ثَلاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ He said, 'Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three days.' that you will not be able to speak to the people for three [days]. It was said: He was only prevented from speaking to them specifically so that he might dedicate the duration to the remembrance of Allah and His gratitude, fulfilling the right of the blessing. It is as if He said: Your sign (آيَتُكَ) is that you restrain your tongue except from gratitude (الشكر). Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: When Zachariah prayed to his Lord to grant him a child, and the angels called out to him with what they called out to him, he wished to know that that voice was from Allah. So, He revealed to him that the sign (آية) of that was that he should restrain his tongue from speech for three days. When he restrained his tongue and did not speak, he knew that none could make him unable to do that except Allah. And that is the saying of Allah: رَبِّ اجْعَلْ لِي آيَةً (My Lord, appoint a sign for me). إِلَّا رَمْزاً except by gestures. by signaling (إشارة). Al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two (Imams) (peace be upon them): So he would gesture with his head. وَٱذْكُرْ رَبَّكَ كَثِيراً (and remember your Lord often,) It was said: meaning during the days of inability to speak to people, and this confirms what preceded it and clarifies the purpose thereof. ** وَسَبِّحْ بِالْعَشِيِ and glorify [Him] in the evening from al-Zawāl (midday) or al-ʿAsr (afternoon) until sunset. وَالْإِبْكارِ (and in the morning.) from the rising of al-Fajr (dawn) until al-Ḍuḥā (forenoon).

(42) وَإِذْ قالَتِ الْمَلائِكَةُ يا مَرْيَمُ إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفاكِ وَطَهَّرَكِ وَاصْطَفاكِ عَلى نِساءِ الْعالَمِينَ (42) And when the angels said, 'O Mary, Allah has chosen you, and purified you, and chosen you over the women of the world.'
