They spoke to her verbally because she was one spoken to (by angels) (مُحَدَّثَة); she spoke to them and they spoke to her before the first choosing (الاصطفاء الأول). (The first choosing was) His acceptance of her from her mother—and no female had been accepted before her—and dedicating her entirely to worship, and enriching her with the provision of Paradise, dispensing with earning (a livelihood), and purifying her from what is deemed repulsive among women.

And the second (choosing) is her guidance, and the sending of the angels to her, and specializing her with the sublime miracles (الكرامات السنية), such as the child without a father, and clearing her from what the Jews accused her of by the child speaking, and making her and her son a sign (آية) for the worlds.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The meaning of the Ayah: ** اصْطَفاكِ (has chosen you) from the progeny of the prophets, ** وَطَهَّرَكِ (and purified you) from fornication (السفاح), and وَاصْطَفاكِ (and chosen you) for the birth of Jesus without a male (partner) (فحل).

٤٣ يا مَرْيَمُ اقْنُتِي لِرَبِّكِ وَاسْجُدِي وَارْكَعِي مَعَ الرَّاكِعِينَ (43) O Mary, be obedient to your Lord, and prostrate, and bow with those who bow. [Meaning:] in their congregation, or be among their number. She was commanded with the prayer by mentioning its pillars (أركانها).

Al-Qummi [said]: It is only: اركعي واسجدي (bow and prostrate), and he counted it among what occurred in the Quran with transposition (التقديم والتأخير).

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: Fatimah was named محدّثة (the one spoken to) because the angels used to descend from the heaven and call out to her, just as they called out to Maryam bint ‘Imran. They would say: O Fatimah, إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفاكِ وَطَهَّرَكِ وَاصْطَفاكِ عَلى نِساءِ الْعالَمِينَ (Indeed Allah has chosen you and purified you, and chosen you over the women of the worlds.) O Fatimah, اقْنُتِي لِرَبِّكِ وَاسْجُدِي وَارْكَعِي مَعَ الرَّاكِعِينَ (be obedient to your Lord, and prostrate, and bow with those who bow.) So she would speak to them and they would speak to her. One night she said to them: Is not Maryam bint ‘Imran the one preferred over the women of the worlds? They replied: Indeed, Maryam was the mistress (سيدة) of the women of her world, and Allah, Mighty is His Glory, made you the mistress (سيدة) of the women of your world and her world, and the mistress (سيدة) of the women of the former and the latter generations.

** ذلِكَ مِنْ أَنْباءِ الْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهِ إِلَيْكَ وَما كُنْتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ يُلْقُونَ أَقْلامَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ يَكْفُلُ مَرْيَمَ (44) That is one of the reports of the Unseen which We reveal to you, and you were not with them when they cast their pens [to determine] which of them should take charge of Mary. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They cast lots with them [the pens] when she was orphaned from her father. وَما كُنْتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ يَخْتَصِمُونَ** ...nor were you with them when they contended. Competing out of rivalry for her guardianship.

** إِذْ قالَتِ الْمَلائِكَةُ يا مَرْيَمُ إِنَّ اللهَ يُبَشِّرُكِ بِكَلِمَةٍ مِنْهُ اسْمُهُ الْمَسِيحُ (45) When the angels said, "O Mary, Allah gives you the good news of a Word from Him, whose name is the Messiah, It was said that its origin in Hebrew is مشيحا (Mashiha), and its meaning is "the Blessed One" (المبارك). ** عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ Jesus son of Mary. It was said that it is the Arabized form (معرب) of ايشوع (Ishūʿ). ** وَجِيهاً honorable Al-Qummi (said): Possessing face (وجه) and high standing (جاه) in this world (الدُّنْيا) through prophethood (النبوة) and messengership (الرسالة). ** وَالْآخِرَةِ and in the Hereafter, through intercession (الشفاعة) and high rank (علو الرتبة). ** وَمِنَ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ and one of the nearest [to Allah]. to Allah by His raising Him to the heaven (السماء), the companionship of the angels (الملائكة), and the elevation of His rank (علو درجته) in Paradise (الجنة).

(٤٦) وَيُكَلِّمُ النَّاسَ كلام الأنبياء فِي الْمَهْدِ حال كونه طفلاً وَكَهْلاً من غير تفاوت (46) and He will speak to the people in the cradle and in adulthood, (This refers to) the speech of the prophets (كلام الأنبياء), while He is a child (طفلاً), and in adulthood, without difference (in the quality of speech). It is said that in this there is proof (دليل) of His descent (نزوله) because He was raised up before He reached adulthood (يكتهل). وَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ and [He will be] one of the righteous. It is said that the mention of His differing, contradictory states (أحواله المختلفة المتنافية) is a guidance (إرشاد) indicating that He is far removed (بمعزل) from divinity (الألوهية).

(٤٧) قالَتْ رَبِّ أَنَّى يَكُونُ لِي وَلَدٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِي بَشَرٌ قالَ كَذلِكِ اللهُ يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ إِذا قَضى أَمْراً فَإِنَّما يَقُولُ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ. (47) She said, 'My Lord! How shall I have a child, when no human has touched me?' He said, 'So it is. Allah creates whatever He wishes. When He decrees a matter, He just says to it, "Be," and it is.' Just as He is able to create things gradually through causes (بأسباب) and materials (ومواد), He is also able to create them instantly (دفعة) without that.

(٤٨) ** وَيُعَلِّمُهُ الْكِتابَ (48) And He will teach him the Book, And it was recited with the nūn (النون), meaning the ability to write (الكتبة) or the genus of the revealed Books (جنس الكتب المنزلة). وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَالتَّوْراةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ and the wisdom, and the Torah and the Evangel. The two Books (التوراة and الإنجيل) were specified due to their merit. (٤٩) ** وَرَسُولاً (49) and [appoint him] an apostle And He will send him as an apostle ** إِلى بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ to the Children of Israel, [who will say]: In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was sent specifically to the Children of Israel, and his prophethood was in Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس). أَنِّي قَدْ جِئْتُكُمْ بِآيَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ‘I have certainly brought you a sign from your Lord: A proof testifying to the validity of my prophethood. ** أَنِّي أَخْلُقُ لَكُمْ I will create for you I will determine and fashion something. And it was recited innī (إنّي) with a kasrah (بالكسر). مِنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ out of clay the figure of a bird, Like its form. فَأَنْفُخُ فِيهِ فَيَكُونُ طَيْراً and then I will breathe into it, and it will become a bird, A living, flying bird. ** بِإِذْنِ اللهِ by Allah’s leave. By His command. This draws attention to the fact that its vivification is from Allah, not from him (Jesus). And it was recited ṭā’iran (طائراً). ** وَأُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ I will heal the blind The blind person (الأعمى). ** وَالْأَبْرَصَ وَأُحْيِ الْمَوْتى بِإِذْنِ اللهِ and the leper, and I will revive the dead, by Allah’s leave. He repeated ** بِإِذْنِ اللهِ to repel the illusion of divinity, for vivification is not of the genus of human actions. وَأُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما تَأْكُلُونَ وَما تَدَّخِرُونَ فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ and I will inform you about what you eat and what you store in your houses. About the unseen matters of your conditions concerning which you have no doubt. ** إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ There is indeed a sign in that for you, should you be faithful.’** Believers, not obstinate rejecters.

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Jesus (peace be upon him) used to say to the Children of Israel: ** إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللهِ إِلَيْكُمْ (I am an apostle of Allah to you) and أَنِّي أَخْلُقُ لَكُمْ مِنَ الطِّينِ كَهَيْئَةِ الطَّيْرِ فَأَنْفُخُ فِيهِ فَيَكُونُ طَيْراً بِإِذْنِ اللهِ وَأُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ وَالْأَبْرَصَ (I will create for you out of clay the figure of a bird, and then I will breathe into it, and it will become a bird, by Allah’s leave. I will heal the blind and the leper). And the akmah (الأكمه) is the blind person (الأعمى). They said: "We see what you do as nothing but magic. Show us a sign so that we may know that you are truthful." He said: "Do you agree that if I inform you ** بِما تَأْكُلُونَ وَما تَدَّخِرُونَ فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ (about what you eat and what you store in your houses) before you leave, and what you stored during the night, you will know that I am truthful?" They said: "Yes." And he would say: "You ate such-and-such, and you drank such-and-such, and you raised/stored such-and-such." So, some of them accepted it and believed, and some of them disbelieved. And there was a sign for them in that, if they were believers.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated maqṭū‘an (مقطوعاً): Jesus remained until he reached seven or eight years of age, and he began informing them about what they ate and what they stored in their houses. He stayed among them, reviving the dead, healing the blind (الأكمه) and the leper (الأبرص), and teaching them the Torah. And Allah revealed the Evangel (الإنجيل) to him when Allah intended a proof against them.

And narrated marfū‘an (مرفوعاً): The companions of Jesus asked him to revive a dead person for them. So, he brought them to the grave of Sam son of Noah (سام بن نوح) and said to him: "Arise by Allah’s leave, O Sam son of Noah!" The narrator said: The grave split open. Then he repeated the words, and he moved. Then he repeated the words, and Sam son of Noah came out. Jesus said to him: "Which do you prefer, to remain or to return?" He said: "O Spirit of Allah (يا روح الله), rather I return. I still feel the burning sensation of death (حرقة الموت)—or he said, the sting of death (لذعة الموت)—in my chest until this very day."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: Did Jesus son of Mary revive anyone after his death such that he had food, provision, a lifespan, and children? He replied: "Yes. He had a friend who was a brother to him for the sake of Allah, the Exalted. Jesus (peace be upon him) used to pass by him and stay with him. Jesus was absent from him for a while, then he passed by to greet him. His mother came out to him, and he asked her about him. She said: 'He died, O Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'Would you like to see him?' She said: 'Yes.' He said to her: 'Then tomorrow I will come to you so that I may revive him for you by Allah, the Exalted’s, leave.' When the next day came, he came to her and said: 'Come with me to his grave.' So, they set off until they reached his grave. Jesus stood there, then supplicated to Allah, the Exalted. The grave split open, and her son came out alive. When his mother saw him behind her, they both wept. Jesus had mercy on them both and said: 'Do you wish to remain with your mother in the world?' He said: 'O Prophet of Allah, with food, provision, and a lifespan, or without food, provision, or a lifespan?' Jesus said to him: 'With food, provision, and a lifespan; you will live for twenty years, marry, and have children.' He said: 'Yes, then.' So, Jesus handed him over to his mother, and he lived for twenty years and had children."

I say: Things similar to what proceeded from Jesus, and even more numerous and wondrous than them, proceeded from our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, as narrated in al-Ihtijaj from al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them both).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a long ḥadīth (حديث) of his: Quraysh gathered before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and asked him to revive their dead for them. So, he sent Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) with them and said to him: "Go to the graveyard (الجبانة) and call out the names of these people whom they are asking about, with your loudest voice: 'O so-and-so, O so-and-so, O so-and-so! Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم tells you: Arise by Allah, the Exalted’s, leave!'" So, they arose, shaking the dust from their heads. Quraysh approached them, asking them about their affairs. Then they informed them that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had been sent as a Prophet, and they said: "We wish we had reached him so that we could believe in him." He (peace be upon him) said: And indeed, he healed the blind (الأكمه) and the leper (الأبرص) and the insane, and the beasts, the birds, the jinn (الجن), and the devils (الشياطين) spoke to him.

(٥٠) وَمُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيَّ مِنَ التَّوْراةِ وَلِأُحِلَّ لَكُمْ بَعْضَ الَّذِي حُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ (and confirming what is before me of the Torah, and so that I may make lawful for you some of what was forbidden to you)

in the Law (شريعة) of Moses (peace be upon him), such as fats (الشحوم), the thurūb (١), fish, camel meat, and working on the Sabbath (السبت). This is what has been said.

And al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: There were four hundred years between David and Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon them). The Law (شريعة) of Jesus (peace be upon him) was that He was sent with Monotheism (التوحيد) and Sincerity (الإخلاص), and with what Noah, Abraham, and Moses (peace be upon them) had enjoined. The Gospel (الإنجيل) was revealed to Him, and the Covenant (الميثاق) that was taken from the Prophets (النبيين) was taken from Him. And He was legislated for in the Book (الكتاب) the establishment of prayer (الصلاة) along with the religion (الدين), the commanding of the right (الأمر بالمعروف), the forbidding of the wrong (النهي عن المنكر), the prohibition of the forbidden (تحريم الحرام), and the permission of the lawful (تحليل الحلال). And in the Gospel (الإنجيل), admonitions (مواعظ) and parables (أمثال) were revealed to Him, but it contained no retribution (قصاص), no rulings on prescribed punishments (أحكام حدود), and no obligatory inheritance (فرض مواريث). And a lightening of what was upon Moses (peace be upon him) in the Torah (التوراة) was revealed to Him, which is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in what Jesus son of Mary said to the Children of Israel: وَلِأُحِلَّ لَكُمْ بَعْضَ الَّذِي حُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ (and so that I may make lawful for you some of what was forbidden to you). And Jesus (peace be upon him) commanded those with Him, among the believers who followed Him, to believe in the Law (شريعة) of the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإنجيل).

I say: The abrogation (نسخ) of some rulings (أحكام) of the Torah (التوراة) does not contradict its confirmation (تصديقه), just as the abrogation (نسخ) of some parts of the Quran by others does not lead to contradiction therein. This is because abrogation (النسخ) is, in reality, a declaration of the termination of the ruling's duration and a specification (تخصيص) regarding the times.

وَجِئْتُكُمْ بِآيَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (and I have brought you a sign from your Lord.)

Perhaps He repeated this saying because the first was a preparation for the proof (الحجة) and the second was an establishment (تقريباً) of the ruling (الحكم), and for this reason, what followed it was connected by the fā’ (الفاء). And it was said: Rather, the intent is: I have brought you another proof (حجة) testifying to the validity of my prophethood, which is His saying:

** إِنَّ اللهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ** (Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord.)

For this is the call of Truth (دعوة الحق) upon which the Messengers (الرسل) are unanimous, and which distinguishes between the Prophet (النبيّ) and the sorcerer (الساحر). And what lies between them is an interjection (اعتراض). فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (So be wary of Allah and obey me. So be wary of Allah) regarding opposition (المخالفة), ** وَأَطِيعُونِ** (and obey me) in what I call you to.

(١) The tharb (الثرب) is a thin fat that covers the stomach (الكرش) and the intestines (الأمعاء). (From him [the author])

(٥١) إِنَّ اللهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ (51) 'Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord' is an indication of perfecting knowledge through the true belief, whose ultimate goal is Monotheism (التوحيد). ** فَاعْبُدُوهُ 'so worship Him.' is an indication of perfecting action by adhering strictly to obedience (الطاعة), which is the performance of the commands (الأوامر) and the abstention from the prohibitions (النواهي). ** هذا صِراطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ 'This is a straight path.' is an indication that the combination of the two matters is the path (الطريق) testified for its straightness (الاستقامة).

(٥٢) فَلَمَّا أَحَسَّ عِيسى مِنْهُمُ الْكُفْرَ (52) When Jesus sensed their faithlessness, When He heard and saw that they were disbelieving. This is how al-Qummi narrated it from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). قالَ مَنْ أَنْصارِي إِلَى اللهِ He said, 'Who will be my helpers for Allah?' Who are my helpers toward His path? قالَ الْحَوارِيُّونَ The Disciples said, The Hawari (disciple) of a man is his sincere follower (خالصته), derived from al-Hawr (الحور), which is pure whiteness (البياض الخالص).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked why the Disciples (الحواريون) were named the Disciples (الحواريين). He said: "As for the common people (عند الناس), they were named Disciples (حواريين) because they were fullers (قصَّارين) who purified clothes from dirt by washing, and it is a name derived from pure white bread (الخبز الحوار). But as for us (عندنا), the Disciples (الحواريون) were named the Disciples (الحواريين) because they were sincere (مخلصين) in themselves and purified others from the dirt of sins (أوساخ الذنوب) through admonition (الوعظ) and remembrance (التذكير)."

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from him (peace be upon him): They were twelve men, and the best and most knowledgeable among them was ألوقا.

نَحْنُ أَنْصارُ اللهِ 'We are Allah's helpers. The helpers of His religion. آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ We believe in Allah, and bear witness that we are Muslims.' Be a witness for us before Allah on the Day of Resurrection, when the Messengers bear witness for and against their peoples.

(٥٣) رَبَّنا آمَنَّا بِما أَنْزَلْتَ وَاتَّبَعْنَا الرَّسُولَ فَاكْتُبْنا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ ** (53) Our Lord! We believe in what You have sent down, and we follow the Apostle. So write us down with the witnesses. [Witnesses] to Your Oneness (بوحدانيتك), or with the witnesses (الشاهدين) who are with the people and for them. (٥٤) وَمَكَرُوا (54) And they planned, meaning those from the Jews from whom Jesus (عيسى) perceived disbelief (الكفر), by appointing someone against him to kill him by treachery (غيلة). وَمَكَرَ اللهُ and Allah planned, when He raised Jesus (عيسى) and cast his likeness (شبهه) upon the one who intended to assassinate him, until he was killed instead of him, as narrated by the general populace (العامّة)... And it has also passed in Tafsir al-Imam (تفسير الإمام) عليه السلام in Surat al-Baqarah: "Or upon one of his close companions (خواصه), so that he might be with him in his rank (درجته)," as mentioned by al-Qummi (القمّيّ), and it will come shortly. And al-Makr (المكر - planning/cunning), since it is originally a stratagem (حيلة) by which one draws another into harm (مضرّة), is not attributed to Allah, the Exalted (الله تعالى), except by way of correspondence (المقابلة) and pairing (الازدواج), or in the sense of requital (المجازاة), as passed down from Imam al-Rida عليه السلام. ** وَاللهُ خَيْرُ الْماكِرِينَ and Allah is the best of planners. [He is] the strongest of them in planning (مكراً), the most effective of them in cunning (كيداً), and the most capable of them in punishment (العقاب) from where the punished one does not anticipate it.

(٥٥) إِذْ قالَ اللهُ يا عِيسى إِنِّي مُتَوَفِّيكَ (55) When Allah said, 'O Jesus! I will take you away, I will complete your term (أجلك), and postpone you until your appointed time (أجلك المسمى), protecting you from their killing; or I will seize you (قابضك) from the earth (from توفيت مالي—I seized/took my wealth); or I will cause you to die (مميتك) from the desires (الشهوات) that hinder ascension (العروج) to the realm of the unseen (عالم الملكوت). ** وَرافِعُكَ إِلَيَ and raise you up toward Myself, to the place of My honor (كرامتي) and the abode of My angels (ملائكتي). ** وَمُطَهِّرُكَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا and cleanse you of the faithless, from the evil of their proximity (جوارهم). وَجاعِلُ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوكَ from the Muslims (المسلمين) and the Christians (النصارى) ** فَوْقَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا from the Jews (اليهود) and the deniers (المكذبين) ** إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ and will place those who follow you above those who are faithless until the Day of Resurrection. They will overcome them by proof (بالمحجة) and by the sword (والسيف). ** ثُمَّ إِلَيَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ Then to Me will be your return, all of you (جَمِيعَاً). Then I will judge between you concerning that about which you used to differ regarding the matter of religion (امر الدين).

(٥٦) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَأُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذاباً شَدِيداً فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَما لَهُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ. (56) But as for those who disbelieve, I will punish them with a severe punishment in the world and the Hereafter, and they will have no helpers. (٥٧) وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ (57) But as for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, He will pay them their full reward. (This is) an explanation of the judgment (الحكم) and a detailing of it. And it was read as fatūffāhum (فتوفّاهم) with the tāʾ (بالتاء). وَاللهُ لا يُحِبُّ الظَّالِمِينَ And Allah does not love the wrongdoers.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in a hadith: Allah sent Jesus son of Mary (عيسى بن مريم) and entrusted Him with the Light (النور), the Knowledge (العلم), the Judgment (الحكم), and all the knowledge of the Prophets before him, and He added the Gospel (الإنجيل) for him. He sent him to Jerusalem (بيت المقدّس) to the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), inviting them to His Book and His wisdom, and to faith in Allah and His Messenger. But most of them refused, except out of transgression (طغياناً) and disbelief (كفراً). When they did not believe, he supplicated to his Lord and resolved upon it, so He transformed some of them into devils (شياطين) to show them a sign (آية) so they might take heed. But that only increased them in transgression and disbelief. So he came to Jerusalem and continued inviting them and encouraging them towards what is with Allah for thirty-three years, until the Jews sought him and claimed that they had tortured him and buried him alive in the earth. And some of them claimed that they killed him and crucified him. But Allah would not grant them authority over him. Rather, it was made to appear so to them (شبّه لهم), and they were not able to torture him and bury him, nor to kill him and crucify him, because if they had been able to do that, it would have been a denial of His saying, but ** رَفَعَهُ اللهُ** (Allah raised him up) after He had taken him (his soul/sleep).

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that he said: Indeed, Jesus (peace be upon him) promised his companions the night Allah raised Him up to Him. So they gathered to him in the evening, and they were twelve men. He brought them into a house, then he came out from a spring (عين) in the corner of the house, shaking the water from his head. He said: "Indeed, Allah has revealed to me that He is raising me up to Him this hour and purifying me from the Jews. Which of you will have my likeness (شبح) cast upon him, so he will be killed and crucified, and thus be with me in my rank (درجتي)?" A young man among them said: "I, O Spirit of Allah (روح الله)." He said: "Then you are he." Then Jesus (peace be upon him) said to them: "Indeed, one of you will disbelieve in me twelve times before morning." A man among them said to him: "I am he, O Prophet of Allah (نبي الله)." Jesus said: "Do you sense that within yourself? Then let it be so." Then Jesus said to them: "Indeed, after me, you will split into three groups (فرق): two groups fabricating lies against Allah will be in the Fire, and one group following Simon (شمعون), truthful concerning Allah, will be in Paradise." Then Allah raised Jesus (peace be upon him) up to Him from the corner of the house while they were looking at Him. Then he (Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام) said: "The Jews came seeking Jesus that very night, so they seized the man to whom Jesus had said, 'Indeed, one of you will disbelieve in me twelve times before morning,' and they seized the young man upon whom the likeness (شبح) of Jesus (peace be upon him) was cast. So he was killed and crucified, and the one to whom Jesus (peace be upon him) had said he would disbelieve twelve times before morning, disbelieved."

(٥٨) ذلِكَ is a reference to what preceded concerning the news of Jesus (عيسى) and others. ** نَتْلُوهُ عَلَيْكَ مِنَ الْآياتِ وَالذِّكْرِ الْحَكِيمِ (58) That We recite to you of the signs and the wise remembrance. That which comprises wisdom (الحكم) or the perfected (المحكم), which is prevented from having defect (الخلل) enter it. He means by it the Quran or the Preserved Tablet (اللوح المحفوظ).

(59) ** إِنَّ مَثَلَ عِيسى عِنْدَ اللهِ كَمَثَلِ آدَمَ** (59) Indeed, the case of Jesus with Allah is like the case of Adam.

meaning, his strange case is like the case of Adam.

** خَلَقَهُ مِنْ تُرابٍ** He created him from dust.

This sentence explains the similitude, clarifying the point of similarity, which is that he (Jesus) was created without a father, just as Adam was created from dust without a father or a mother. He likened his (Jesus') state to that which is closer to silencing the opponent and cutting off the sources of doubt (مواد الشبه). The meaning is that He created his mold (قالبه) from dust.

** ثُمَّ قالَ لَهُ كُنْ ** then He said to him, 'Be,'

meaning, He brought forth a human being, like His saying: ** ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْناهُ خَلْقاً آخَرَ** (then We produced him as another creature). And He decreed his formation from dust, then He formed him.

** فَيَكُونُ** and he was.

meaning, so he came into existence immediately (في الحال).

(60) ** الْحَقُ It is the truth مِنْ رَبِّكَ فَلا تَكُنْ مِنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ. (The truth is from your Lord, so do not be among the skeptical.) (61) ** فَمَنْ حَاجَّكَ Whoever argues with you, from the Christians, ** فِيهِ concerning him, meaning Jesus (peace be upon him), ** مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ after what has come to you of knowledge, meaning from the clear proofs necessitating knowledge, فَقُلْ تَعالَوْا then say: Come! (هلمّوا) with resolve (بالرأي والعزم), نَدْعُ أَبْناءَنا وَأَبْناءَكُمْ وَنِساءَنا وَنِساءَكُمْ وَأَنْفُسَنا وَأَنْفُسَكُمْ We will call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, and ourselves and yourselves. Meaning, let each one of us and you call his soul, and the dearest and closest of his family to his heart, to the مباهلة (mutual imprecation) and commit to it. He only prioritized them over the self because a man risks his life for them and fights on their behalf. ** ثُمَّ نَبْتَهِلْ then we will pray humbly, meaning, we will mutually imprecate by cursing the liar among us. And البهلة (with ḍamma and fatḥa) is the curse (اللعنة). Its origin is abandonment (الترك), derived from their saying: بهلت الناقة (I abandoned the she-camel) when you leave her without a صرار (a cord tied above the udder so that her young does not suckle her). ** فَنَجْعَلْ لَعْنَتَ اللهِ عَلَى الْكاذِبِينَ then we will place the curse of Allah upon the liars. This is an appositional conjunction providing clarification.

It is narrated: When they were invited to the مباهلة, they said, "Let us consider." When they were alone, they said to al-ʿAqib, who was their man of opinion, "What do you think?" He said: "By Allah, you have certainly recognized His prophethood, and He has brought you the decisive word concerning your companion (Jesus). By Allah, no people have ever mutually cursed a Prophet except that they perished. If you refuse anything other than your religion, then make peace with the man and depart." So they came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who had gone out early, embracing al-Husayn (peace be upon him), holding the hand of al-Hasan, with Fatima (peace be upon them) walking behind Him, and Ali walking behind her. He was saying: "When I supplicate, say Āmīn." Then Istafham (a narrator/figure) said: "O assembly of Christians, I see faces that, if they were to ask Allah to remove a mountain from its place, He would remove it. So do not mutually curse, lest you perish." So they submitted to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and offered Him the Jizyah (tribute): two thousand red garments (ḥullah) and thirty iron shields. He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, had they mutually cursed, they would have been transformed into apes and swine, and the valley would have been set ablaze with fire upon them, and Allah would have utterly destroyed Najran and its people, even the birds on the trees." This is how the general public (العامّة) narrated it, and it is proof of His prophethood and the merit of those He brought from His Household (أهل بيته), and their honor (شرفهم)—an honor that no creation can surpass them in—since He made the soul of Ali (peace be upon him) like His own soul.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): No one claimed that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought him under the cloak (الكساء) during the مباهلة with the Christians except Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them). Thus, the interpretation (تأويل) of His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, ** أَبْناءَنا (our sons) was al-Hasan and al-Husayn, ** وَنِساءَنا (our women) was Fatima, and ** وَأَنْفُسَنا** (ourselves) was Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Christians of Najran came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and their leaders were al-Ahtam, al-ʿAqib, and al-Sayyid, their time for prayer arrived, so they began striking the bell (الناقوس) and prayed. The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Messenger of Allah, this is in your mosque!" He said: "Leave them." When they finished, they approached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and asked Him: "To what do you call?" He said: "To the testimony that there is no god but Allah, and that I am the Messenger of Allah, and that Jesus is a created servant who eats, drinks, and relieves himself (يحدث)." They said: "Then who is his father?" The revelation descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He said: "Say to them: What do you say about Adam? Was he a created servant who ate, drank, relieved himself, and married (وينكح)?" The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم asked them, and they said: "Yes." He said: "Then who is his father?" They were confounded. Then Allah revealed: ** إِنَّ مَثَلَ عِيسى عِنْدَ اللهِ كَمَثَلِ آدَمَ خَلَقَهُ مِنْ تُرابٍ (Indeed, the case of Jesus with Allah is like the case of Adam: He created him from dust) the Ayah. And His saying: ** فَمَنْ حَاجَّكَ فِيهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ up to His saying: ** فَنَجْعَلْ لَعْنَتَ اللهِ عَلَى الْكاذِبِينَ The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then said: "Mutually curse me, for if I am truthful, the curse will descend upon you, and if I am lying, it will descend upon me." They said: "You have been fair." So they agreed upon a time for the مباهلة. When they returned to their dwellings, their leaders, al-Sayyid, al-ʿAqib, and al-Ahtam, said: "If He mutually curses us with His people, we will curse Him, for He is not a Prophet. But if He mutually curses us specifically with His Household (أهل بيته), we will not curse Him, for He would not bring forward His Household unless He were truthful." When morning came, they came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and with Him were the Commander of the Faithful, Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). The Christians asked: "Who are these?" They were told: "This is His cousin, His successor (وصيه), and His son-in-law (ختنه), Ali ibn Abi Talib. And this is His daughter, Fatima. And these are His two sons, al-Hasan and al-Husayn (may the blessings of Allah be upon them)." They became afraid and said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "We will give you satisfaction (الرضا), so exempt us from the مباهلة." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم made peace with them on the condition of the Jizyah and they departed.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): Had He said, "Come, let us pray humbly, and we will place the curse of Allah upon you," they would not have responded to the مباهلة. Allah already knew that His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was conveying His message and was not among the liars. Likewise, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم knew that He was truthful in what He said, but He loved to be fair (ينصف) regarding Himself.

(٦٢) إِنَّ هذا لَهُوَ الْقَصَصُ الْحَقُّ وَما مِنْ إِلهٍ إِلَّا اللهُ. (62) This is indeed the true account, and there is no god except Allah. He (Allah) brought the extra particle min (مِنْ) for comprehensive inclusion (للاستغراق), emphasizing the refutation against the Christians (النصارى) regarding their doctrine of the Trinity (تثليثهم). ** وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ And Allah is indeed the All-mighty, the All-wise. No one besides Him equals Him in perfect power (القدرة التامة) and consummate wisdom (الحكمة البالغة) so as to share with Him in divinity (الألوهية).

(63) فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِالْمُفْسِدِينَ (63) But if they turn away, Allah is indeed aware of the agents of corruption.

This is a warning (وعيد) for them. The explicit noun (المظهر) is placed in the position of the implicit pronoun (المضمر) in order to indicate that turning away (التولي) from the proofs (الحجج) and rejecting (الاعراض) Monotheism (التوحيد) is corruption (افساد) of the religion (الدين), and leads to the corruption (افساد) of the soul (النفس), and even to the corruption (افساد) of the world (العالم).

(٦٤) قُلْ يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ تَعالَوْا إِلى كَلِمَةٍ سَواءٍ بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكُمْ أَلَّا نَعْبُدَ إِلَّا اللهَ (64) Say, 'O People of the Book! Come to a common word between us and you: that we will worship no one but Allah, that we unify Him in worship (بالعبادة) and be sincere therein, ** وَلا نُشْرِكَ بِهِ شَيْئاً and associate none as partner with Him, and that we do not set up anyone other than Him as a partner to Him in worship, ** وَلا يَتَّخِذَ بَعْضُنا بَعْضاً أَرْباباً مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ and none of us shall take others as lords besides Allah.' and that we do not say Uzayr is the son of Allah, nor the Messiah is the son of Allah, nor do we obey the rabbis (الأحبار) in what they innovated concerning prohibition (التحريم) and permission (التحليل), because every one of them is a human being like us.

In al-Majma', it is narrated: When the verse ** اتَّخَذُوا أَحْبارَهُمْ وَرُهْبانَهُمْ أَرْباباً مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (They took their rabbis and monks as lords besides Allah) was revealed, Adi ibn Hatim said: "We did not worship them, O Messenger of Allah." He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "Did they not declare things lawful (يحلون) and unlawful (يحرمون) for you, and you accepted their saying?" He said: "Yes." He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "That is it." فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا (But if they turn away) from unification (التوحيد), فَقُولُوا اشْهَدُوا بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ (then say, 'Bear witness that we are Muslims'), meaning, the proof (الحجّة) is established against you, so acknowledge بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ (that we are Muslims) and not you. It is said: Look at the extent of the emphasis on guidance (الإرشاد) and the excellence of the gradual approach (التدرج) in argumentation (الحجاج) observed in this story. First, He clarified the circumstances of Jesus and the stages (الأطوار) that passed over him, which contradict divinity (الالهية). Then, He mentioned what would untie their knot and remove their doubt (شبهتهم). When He saw their obstinacy (عنادهم) and persistence (لجاجهم), He invited them to the mutual imprecation (المباهلة) as a type of miraculous challenge (الاعجاز). Then, when they turned away from it and submitted partially, He returned to guiding them (بالإرشاد) and took an easier and more compelling path by inviting them to what Jesus, the Gospel (الإنجيل), and the rest of the Prophets and Books agreed upon. Then, when this also did not benefit them, and He knew that the signs (الآيات) and warnings (النذر) would not avail them, He turned away from that and said: ** اشْهَدُوا بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ (Bear witness that we are Muslims).

(٦٥) يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لِمَ تُحَاجُّونَ فِي إِبْراهِيمَ وَما أُنْزِلَتِ التَّوْراةُ وَالْإِنْجِيلُ إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ (65) O People of the Book! Why do you argue concerning Abraham, when the Torah and the Evangel were not sent down until after him?

It is narrated: The Jews and the Christians disputed concerning Abraham, and each party claimed that he belonged to them. So they referred the matter to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and [the verse] was revealed. And the meaning is that Judaism and Christianity came into being with the sending down of the Torah and the Evangel upon Moses and Jesus, while Abraham was a thousand years before Moses and two thousand years before Jesus. So how could he follow them? أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ (Do you not exercise your reason?) so that you claim the impossible.

** ها أَنْتُمْ هؤُلاءِ حاجَجْتُمْ فِيما لَكُمْ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلِمَ تُحَاجُّونَ فِيما لَيْسَ لَكُمْ بِهِ عِلْمٌ ** (Ah! You are the ones who argued concerning that of which you had knowledge. Why then do you argue concerning that of which you have no knowledge?)

They were alerted by the particle of warning (حرف التنبيه) to their state of which they were heedless, meaning: you are these fools (الحمقى). And the explanation of your foolishness (حماقتكم) is that you argued concerning that of which you had knowledge, based on what you found in the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإنجيل), out of obstinacy (عناداً) or claiming its mention therein. So why do you argue concerning that of which you have no knowledge, and for which there is no mention in your book concerning the religion of Abraham (دين إبراهيم)? And it was said that هؤلاء (these) is in the meaning of الذين (those who). And it was said that it is an appositive (عطف بيان) for أنتم (you). And Allah knows what you argued about concerning the affair of Abraham (إبراهيم) and his religion, ** وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ** (And you do not know), so do not speak concerning it.

(٦٧) ما كانَ إِبْراهِيمُ يَهُودِيًّا وَلا نَصْرانِيًّا (67) Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian, [This is] an explicit declaration (تصريح) according to what He (Allah) established. ** وَلكِنْ كانَ حَنِيفاً but he was a ḥanīf, meaning, inclined away (مائلاً) from false doctrines (العقائد الزائفة). ** مُسْلِماً a Muslim, meaning, submissive (منقاداً) to Allah the Almighty (لله تعالى). The intention is not that he was upon the religion (ملّة) of Islam (as practiced later), otherwise the obligation (الإلزام) would be shared.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [He was] pure (خالصاً), sincere (مخلصاً), having nothing of idol worship (عبادة الأوثان) in him. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: Not a Jew who prays toward the West (المغرب), nor a Christian who prays toward the East (المشرق). ** وَلكِنْ كانَ حَنِيفاً مُسْلِماً (but he was a ḥanīf, a Muslim) upon the religion (دين) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

I say: Meaning, he used to pray toward the Ka'bah, which is between the East (المشرق) and the West (المغرب), and his religion (دينه) was in agreement (موافقاً) with the religion (دين) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

وَما كانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ nor was he one of the polytheists. [This is] an allusion (تعريض) that they (the Jews and Christians) are polytheists (مشركون) due to their associating (اشراكهم) 'Uzayr (عزيراً) and the Messiah (المسيح) with Him (Allah). [This verse] is a refutation (ورد) of the claim (ادعاء) of the polytheists (المشركين) that they are upon the religion (ملّة) of Abraham.

(٦٨) إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْراهِيمَ ** (Indeed the nearest people to Abraham) means those who are most specific to him (أخصهم به) and closest to him (أقربهم منه), derived from al-Wali (الولي) which signifies closeness (القرب). [They are] لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ (surely those who followed him) from his nation (أمته) ** وَهذَا النَّبِيُ (and this Prophet) specifically (خصوصاً) ** وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (and those who have faith) from his nation (أمته), due to their agreement with him in most of what He legislated for them originally (على الأصالة).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the Imams (الأئمة) and those who followed them.

And in al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Umar ibn Yazid, from him (peace be upon him), he said: "By Allah, you are among the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد)." So I said: "From their very selves (من أنفسهم), may I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك)?" He said: "Yes, by Allah, from their very selves (من أنفسهم)," three times. Then he looked at me and I looked at him, and he said: "O Umar, indeed Allah the Almighty says in His Book: ** إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ** (Indeed the nearest people) [up to the end of] the Ayah."

And in al-Majma, he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, the nearest people to the Prophets (الأنبياء) are those who act most upon what they brought." Then he recited this Ayah. He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, the Wali (ولي) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is he who obeys Allah, even if his lineage (لحمته) is distant, and indeed, the enemy (عدو) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is he who disobeys Allah, even if his kinship (قرابته) is close." وَاللهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (and Allah is the guardian of the faithful.) He undertakes their support (نصرتهم).

(69) ** وَدَّتْ طائِفَةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ لَوْ يُضِلُّونَكُمْ (69) A faction of the People of the Book are eager to lead you astray. It was said that it was revealed concerning the Jews when they invited Hudhayfah, Ammar, and Mu'adh to Judaism (اليهودية). ** وَما يُضِلُّونَ إِلَّا أَنْفُسَهُمْ But they lead no one astray except themselves, And nothing misses them except misguidance, and its evil consequence (وباله) does not return except upon them, since their punishment is multiplied by it. Or, they do not lead astray except their likes (أمثالهم). ** وَما يَشْعُرُونَ and they are not aware. [They are not aware of] its burden (وزره) and the specific allocation (اختصاص) of its harm to them.

(٧٠) يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لِمَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ (70) O People of the Book! Why do you disbelieve in the signs of Allah by what the Torah and the Gospel spoke of and indicated regarding the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ** وَأَنْتُمْ تَشْهَدُونَ while you bear witness that they are the signs of Allah. Or [why do you disbelieve] in what is recited to you from the Quran, while you bear witness to His description (نعته) in the two Books (الكتابين), or [why do you disbelieve] while you know through the miracles (المعجزات) that it is the truth, or [why do you disbelieve] in the miracles (المعجزات) while you bear witness that the appearance of miracles (المعجزات) indicates the truthfulness of the message (الرسالة).

(٧١) ** يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لِمَ تَلْبِسُونَ الْحَقَّ بِالْباطِلِ** (71) O People of the Book! Why do you mix the truth with falsehood

by distortion (بالتحريف) and presenting the falsehood (الباطل) in its guise (في صورته), or by failing in the distinction (المميز) between the two. ** وَتَكْتُمُونَ الْحَقَ and conceal the truth (which is) the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and His description (نعته). ** وَأَنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ while you know [it]? (meaning) you are knowledgeable concerning what you conceal.

(٧٢) وَقالَتْ طائِفَةٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ آمِنُوا بِالَّذِي أُنْزِلَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَجْهَ النَّهارِ ** (72) And a faction of the People of the Book said, 'Believe in what has been sent down to the faithful at the beginning of the day, meaning, show forth belief (الإيمان) in the Quran at the beginning of the day, ** وَاكْفُرُوا آخِرَهُ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ and disbelieve at its end, so that they may turn back. so that they doubt their religion, thinking that you have returned [from it] due to a defect (خلل) that became apparent to you.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم arrived in Medina while He was praying towards Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس), that pleased the people. But when Allah turned Him away from Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس) towards the Sacred House of Allah (بيت الله الحرام), the Jews felt [distress] because of that. And the changing of the Qiblah (القبلة) occurred during the Dhuhr (الظهر) prayer. So they said: "Muhammad prayed the morning prayer (الغداة) facing our Qiblah (قبلتنا)." So ** آمِنُوا بِالَّذِي أُنْزِلَ عَلَى Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم at the beginning of the day, and disbelieve at its end. They meant the Qiblah (القبلة) when the Messenger of Allah faced the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام). ** لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ to our Qiblah (قبلتنا).

(٧٣) وَلا تُؤْمِنُوا إِلَّا لِمَنْ تَبِعَ دِينَكُمْ (73) Do not believe except those who follow your religion. It was said that this is the continuation of the speech of the Jews, meaning: Do not believe (لا تصدقوا) nor acknowledge (ولا تقرّوا) that anyone will be given the like of what you were given, except for the people of your religion. ** قُلْ إِنَّ الْهُدى هُدَى اللهِ Say, 'Indeed, the guidance is the guidance of Allah.' This is an interjection (اعتراض) between the object (المفعول) and its verb (فعله), from the speech of Allah, the Almighty (كلام الله تعالى), and its meaning is that the religion (الدين) is the religion of Allah. ** أَنْ يُؤْتى أَحَدٌ مِثْلَ ما أُوتِيتُمْ that anyone should be given the like of what you were given, Meaning: from knowledge (العلم), wisdom (الحكمة), the Book (الكتاب), the proof (الحجة), the manna (المنّ), the quails (السلوى), the virtues (الفضائل), and the miracles (الكرامات). And an yu'ta (أن يؤتى) was read with madd (المد) indicating interrogation (الاستفهام) (1). أَوْ يُحاجُّوكُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّكُمْ (or that they should argue with you before your Lord) is conjoined (عطف) to His saying: ** أَنْ يُؤْتى أَحَدٌ. The waw (الواو) [in yuhajjukum] is the pronoun referring to ahad (أحد) because it carries the meaning of a plural (الجمع). The meaning is: And do not believe that ** يُحاجُّوكُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّكُمْ (they should argue with you before your Lord), because you are more sincere in religion (انصح ديناً) than them, so they will not have the proof (الحجة) against you. There are other interpretations (وجوه أُخر) regarding the Ayah, and it is among the ambiguous verses (المتشابهات) concerning which nothing has reached us from the People of the House (أهل البيت). ** قُلْ إِنَّ الْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ اللهِ Say, 'Indeed, all grace is in the hand of Allah; Meaning: Guidance (الهداية) and success (التوفيق) are from Him. يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ واسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ. He grants it to whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing.

(٧٤) يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ. (74) He singles out whomever He wishes for His mercy, and Allah is the Possessor of the great grace. (٧٥) وَمِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ مَنْ إِنْ تَأْمَنْهُ بِقِنْطارٍ يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ إِنْ تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينارٍ لا يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ إِلَّا ما دُمْتَ عَلَيْهِ قائِماً (75) Among the People of the Book there is he who, if you entrust him with a talent, will restore it to you, and among them there is he who, if you entrust him with a dinar, will not restore it to you unless you keep standing over him. except for the duration of your persistence over his head, demanding it from him harshly. ** ذلِكَ (meaning the abandonment of restoration) بِأَنَّهُمْ قالُوا due to their saying: لَيْسَ عَلَيْنا فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ سَبِيلٌ there is no way against us concerning the unlettered ones. meaning, there is no punishment (عقاب) or blame (ذم) upon us regarding the affair of those who are not from the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) and who are not upon our religion. ** وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ by claiming that, ** وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ that they are liars, and that is because they deemed lawful the oppression (ظلم) of those who opposed them and said that no sanctity (حرمة) was established for them in the Torah (التوراة).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: When He recited this verse, He said: "The enemies of Allah have lied. There is nothing that existed in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) except that it is beneath my feet, except for the trust (الأمانة), for it must be restored to the righteous (البر) and the wicked (الفاجر)."

footnote (١) It was said that** أَنْ يُؤْتى** is related to an omitted phrase, meaning: you planned that and said: that anyone should be given. The meaning is that envy (*الحسد*) carried you to that. The reading of** أَنْ يُؤْتى** with the interrogative (*الاستفهام*) for reprimand (*التقريع*) supports this interpretation. It was also said that** أَنْ يُؤْتى ** is the predicate (*خبر*) of *inna* (*إنّ*) based on** إِنَّ الْهُدى هُدَى اللهِ** being a substitute (*بدل*) for **الْهُدى**. Thus, the meaning of** أَوْ يُحاجُّوكُمْ** would be: until they argue with you (*حتى يحاجوكم*) and refute your argument (*فيدحض حجتكم*). It was said that there are other sayings (*أقوال أخر*) concerning it, and the knowledge is with Allah (from him, may his secret be sanctified (*منه قده*)).

(٧٦) بَلى is an affirmation of what they denied. Meaning: Nay, but there is a way (of responsibility) upon them. ** مَنْ أَوْفى بِعَهْدِهِ وَاتَّقى فَإِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ (whoever fulfills his pledge and is Godwary, then Allah indeed loves the Godwary.) This is a resumption (of speech), meaning: everyone who fulfills what he pledged, whatever pledge it may be, and is Godwary of Allah in abandoning treachery and deceit, then Allah loves him. The placement of the explicit noun (الظاهر) in the place of the pronoun (المضمر) is an indication that Godwariness (التقوى) is the pivot of the matter.

(٧٧) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ يستبدلون بِعَهْدِ اللهِ بما عاهدوا عليه من الإيمان بالرسول والوفاء بالأمانات وَأَيْمانِهِمْ وبما حلفوا به ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً متاع الدنيا من الرئاسة وأخذ الرشوة والذهاب بمال أخيهم المسلم ونحو ذلك أُولئِكَ لا خَلاقَ لَهُمْ لا نصيب لهم فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَلا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللهُ وَلا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ كناية عن سخطه عليهم واستهانته بهم. (Indeed those who sell Allah’s covenant and their oaths for a paltry gain—they have no share in the Hereafter, and Allah will not speak to them, nor will He look at them on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He purify them, and there is a painful punishment for them.) ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ (Indeed those who sell) means: they exchange (يَسْتَبْدِلُونَ). بِعَهْدِ اللهِ (Allah’s covenant) with what they covenanted upon, such as faith in the Messenger and fulfilling trusts (الْأَمَانَات). ** وَأَيْمانِهِمْ (and their oaths) and with what they swore by. ** ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً (for a paltry gain) the enjoyment (مَتَاع) of this world, such as leadership (الرِّئَاسَة), taking bribes (الرِّشْوَة), taking the wealth of their Muslim brother, and the like. ** أُولئِكَ لا خَلاقَ لَهُمْ (they have no share) meaning: they have no portion (نَصِيب) ** فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَلا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللهُ وَلا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (in the Hereafter, and Allah will not speak to them, nor will He look at them on the Day of Resurrection) This is a metaphor (كِنَايَة) for His wrath (سُخْط) upon them and His disdain (اسْتِهَانَة) for them.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (prayers of Allah be upon him): It means that no good will reach them. He said: The Arabs sometimes say, "By Allah, so-and-so does not look at us," and they only mean by that that no good reaches us from him. وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ (nor will He purify them). It was said: nor will He praise them (يُثْنِي عَلَيْهِمْ). And in Tafsir al-Imam: ** وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ from their sins (ذُنُوبِهِمْ), as previously mentioned.

وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (and there is a painful punishment for them). In al-Amali, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever swears an oath by which he unjustly takes the wealth of his brother will meet Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), while He is wrathful toward him." So Allah sent down confirmation of this in His Book: ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ** (Indeed those who sell) the Ayah.

(٧٨) وَإِنَّ مِنْهُمْ لَفَرِيقاً يَلْوُونَ أَلْسِنَتَهُمْ بِالْكِتابِ (78) And indeed there is a group among them who twist their tongues while [reading] the Book They twist them (their tongues) while reading it, thereby inclining them away from what was revealed toward what is distorted. ** لِتَحْسَبُوهُ مِنَ الْكِتابِ وَما هُوَ مِنَ الْكِتابِ وَيَقُولُونَ هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ وَما هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ (78) so that you may suppose it is from the Book, while it is not from the Book. And they say, 'It is from Allah,' while it is not from Allah. This is an affirmation and an increase in the severity of the condemnation against them. ** وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (78) and they attribute lies to Allah, while they know [it is a lie]. This is an affirmation and a recording against them of lying against Allah and doing so intentionally (التعمد فيه).

Al-Qummi, narrated without a full chain (مقطوعاً), said: The Jews used to fabricate something that was not in the Torah and say that it was in the Torah, so Allah declared them liars.

(٧٩) ما كانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ اللهُ الْكِتابَ وَالْحُكْمَ وَالنُّبُوَّةَ ثُمَّ يَقُولَ لِلنَّاسِ كُونُوا عِباداً لِي مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (79) It is not for any human that Allah should give him the Book, the judgment and the prophethood, and then he should say to the people, 'Be my worshippers instead of Allah's.' In al-Majma': It is said that Abu Rafi' al-Qurazi (القرظي) and al-Sayyid al-Najrani (النجراني) said: "O Muhammad, do you wish that we worship you and take you as a Lord (رباً)?" So he said: "I seek refuge in Allah that anyone other than Allah should be worshipped, or that we should command anything other than the worship of Allah. I was not sent for that, nor was I commanded that." So the verse was revealed. ** وَلكِنْ كُونُوا رَبَّانِيِّينَ وَلكِنْ (But [he would say]: 'Be devoted to the Lord.') He says: "Be Rabbaniyyin (رَبَّانِيِّينَ)." And al-Rabbani (الربّاني) is attributed to al-Rabb (الرب) with the addition of the alif (الألف) and the nun (النون), and he is the one who is perfect (الكامل) in knowledge (العلم) and action (العمل). And al-Qummi (said): That is, Jesus (عيسى) did not say to the people: "I created you and كُونُوا عِباداً لِي مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَلكِنْ ** (Be my worshippers instead of Allah's. But [he would say]:)" Rather, He said to them: ** كُونُوا رَبَّانِيِّينَ (Be devoted to the Lord), meaning: scholars (علماء). بِما كُنْتُمْ تُعَلِّمُونَ الْكِتابَ وَبِما كُنْتُمْ تَدْرُسُونَ (because of your teaching the Book and your studying it.) [This means] due to your being teachers of the Book and students (دارسين) of it, for the benefit of teaching (التعليم) and learning (التعلّم) is the knowledge of truth (الحق) and goodness (الخير) for belief (الاعتقاد) and action (العمل). And it was recited with takhfif (التخفيف) [i.e., تَعْلَمُونَ instead of تُعَلِّمُونَ], meaning: due to your being knowledgeable (عالمين). In al-Uyun, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "Do not raise me above my rightful status, for Allah, the Exalted, took me as a servant (عبداً) before He took me as a Prophet (نبياً)." Then he recited this verse. And from the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين): "Two types of people will perish concerning me, and I bear no sin: an excessive lover (محبّ مفرط) and an excessive hater (مبغض مفرط). And I am innocent before Allah, the Exalted, of those who exaggerate concerning us and raise us above our limit, just as Jesus son of Mary (عيسى بن مريم) is innocent of the Christians (النصارى)."

(٨٠) وَلا يَأْمُرَكُمْ (80) Nor would He bid you And it was read with the rāʾ having a fatḥah (وَلَا يَأْمُرَكُمْ). ** أَنْ تَتَّخِذُوا الْمَلائِكَةَ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ أَرْباباً أَيَأْمُرُكُمْ بِالْكُفْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ (80) Nor would He bid you to take the angels and the prophets as lords. Would He bid you to commit infidelity after you have submitted [to Him]? Al-Qummi said: There was a group of people who worshipped the angels, and a group of Christians claimed that Jesus was a lord (رَبّ), and the Jews said Uzayr was the son of Allah. So Allah said: ** وَلا يَأْمُرَكُمْ (to the end of) the verse.

(81) ** وَإِذْ أَخَذَ اللهُ مِيثاقَ النَّبِيِّينَ لَما آتَيْتُكُمْ (81) And when Allah took a pledge from the prophets: "Inasmuch as I have given you And it was read with kasrah on the lām and ātainākum (آتيناكم) مِنْ كِتابٍ وَحِكْمَةٍ ثُمَّ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مُصَدِّقٌ لِما مَعَكُمْ لَتُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهِ وَلَتَنْصُرُنَّهُ a part of the Book and wisdom, should a messenger come to you, confirming what is with you, you shall believe in him and you shall help him."**

In al-Jawāmi' and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): "And when Allah took the covenant from the nations (أُمَم) of the prophets, [it was] that every nation should affirm its prophet and act upon what came to them through him. But they did not fulfill it, and they abandoned many of their laws (شَرَائِع) and distorted many of them."

And in al-'Ayyāshī, narrated from Imam al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): [A narration] with its meaning expanded, and He said: "Thus did Allah reveal it," meaning, He omitted [the word أُمَم] from it.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah the Almighty took the covenant from the prophets before our Prophet that they should inform their nations (أُمَم) of His advent and His description (نَعْت), give them glad tidings of Him, and command them to affirm Him."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him) that He said: "Allah did not send any prophet, starting from Adam and those after him, except that He took a covenant from him: 'If Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is sent while he is alive, he shall surely believe in Him and surely help Him.' And He commanded him to take the covenant regarding this from his people."

And in al-Qummī and al-'Ayyāshī, narrated from Imam al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): "Allah did not send a prophet, from the time of Adam onwards, except that he will return to the world and help the Commander of the Faithful. This is His saying: لَتُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهِ (you shall believe in him), meaning the Messenger of Allah, ** وَلَتَنْصُرُنَّهُ** (and you shall help him), meaning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them both)."

And in Kitāb al-Wāḥidah, narrated from Imam al-Bāqir (peace be upon him), He said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty is One (أَحَد), Single (وَاحِد). He became unique in His Oneness (وَحْدَانِيَّة), the Exalted. Then He spoke a word (كَلِمَة) and it became a light (نُور). Then He created Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from that light, and He created me and my progeny (ذُرِّيَّة). Then He spoke a word and it became a spirit (رُوح). So Allah settled it in that light and settled it in our bodies. Thus, we are the Spirit of Allah and His Words (كَلِمَات). Through us, He veiled Himself from His creation. We remained in a green shade (ظُلَّة خَضْرَاء), where there was no sun, nor moon, nor night, nor day, nor an eye blinking, worshipping Him, sanctifying Him, and glorifying Him. That was before He created His creation. And He took the covenant from the prophets regarding faith in us and helping us. That is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: (وَإِذْ أَخَذَ اللهُ مِيثاقَ النَّبِيِّينَ لَما آتَيْتُكُمْ مِنْ كِتابٍ وَحِكْمَةٍ ثُمَّ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مُصَدِّقٌ لِما مَعَكُمْ لَتُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهِ وَلَتَنْصُرُنَّهُ) (And when Allah took a pledge from the prophets: "Inasmuch as I have given you a part of the Book and wisdom, should a messenger come to you, confirming what is with you, you shall believe in him and you shall help him.") meaning: ** لَتُؤْمِنُنَ (you shall believe) in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and ** وَلَتَنْصُرُنَّهُ (and you shall help him) [means] His successor (وَصِيّ). And you shall all help Him. And indeed, Allah took my covenant along with the covenant of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding our mutual help for one another. I have already helped Muhammad, struggled (جَاهَدْتُ) before Him, killed His enemy, and fulfilled for Allah what He took from me of the covenant (مِيثَاق), the pledge (عَهْد), and the help (نُصْرَة) for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. None of the prophets of Allah and His messengers helped me, because Allah took them back to Him. But they will soon help me, and what is between its East and its West will belong to me. And Allah will surely resurrect them alive, from Adam to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, every sent prophet, striking with the sword before me the heads (هَام) of the dead and the living, and the two weighty things (الثَّقَلَيْن) altogether. O wonder! And how can I not wonder at the dead whom Allah resurrects alive, responding group after group (زُمْرَة زُمْرَة) with the Talbiyah (التَّلْبِيَة): 'Here I am, here I am, O Caller of Allah (يَا دَاعِيَ اللهِ)!' They have already appeared in the streets (سِكَك) of Kufa, having drawn their swords upon their shoulders, striking with them the heads of the disbelievers (الْكَفَرَة) and their tyrants (جَبَابِرَتِهِم), and their followers from the tyrants of the former and the latter generations, until Allah fulfills what He promised them in His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: (وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ كَمَا اسْتَخْلَفَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ دِينَهُمُ الَّذِي ارْتَضى لَهُمْ وَلَيُبَدِّلَنَّهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ خَوْفِهِمْ أَمْناً يَعْبُدُونَنِي لا يُشْرِكُونَ بِي شَيْئاً) (Allah has promised those of you who have faith and do righteous deeds that He will surely make them successors in the land, just as He made those who were before them successors, and He will surely establish for them their religion which He has approved for them, and He will surely change their state to security after their fear. They worship Me, not associating anything with Me.) meaning: they will worship Me securely, not fearing anyone in My worship; they will have no dissimulation (تَقِيَّة) with them. And indeed, for me is the return (الْكَرَّة) after the return, and the Raj'ah (الرَّجْعَة) after the Raj'ah. I am the master of the returns (الرَّجَعَات) and the comebacks (الْكَرَّات), and the master of the assaults (الصَّوْلَات), the vengeance (النَّقَمَات), and the wondrous dominions (الدَّوْلَات الْعَجِيبَات). And I am a horn of iron (قَرْن مِنْ حَدِيد)." The narration (الْحَدِيث) in its entirety.

قالَ أَأَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَخَذْتُمْ عَلى ذلِكُمْ إِصْرِي He said, "Do you affirm and accept My covenant concerning this?" meaning My covenant (عَهْدِي): قالُوا أَقْرَرْنا قالَ فَاشْهَدُوا وَأَنَا مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ They said, "We affirm." He said, "Then be witnesses, and I will be with you among the witnesses."

In al-Qummī, narrated from Imam al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): He said to them concerning the atoms (الذَّرّ): ** أَأَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَخَذْتُمْ عَلى ذلِكُمْ إِصْرِي (Do you affirm and accept My covenant concerning this?), meaning My covenant (عَهْدِي). ** قالُوا أَقْرَرْنا قالَ (They said, "We affirm." He said) — Allah said to the angels — ** فَاشْهَدُوا** (Then be witnesses).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): ** قالَ أَأَقْرَرْتُمْ (He said, "Do you affirm?") and seal the covenant concerning this upon your nations (أُمَم). ** قالُوا (They said) — meaning the prophets and their nations said — ** أَقْرَرْنا ("We affirm") what You commanded us to affirm. ** قالَ (He said) — Allah said — ** فَاشْهَدُوا (Then be witnesses) concerning that upon your nations, ** وَأَنَا مَعَكُمْ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ (and I will be with you among the witnesses) against you and against your nations.

(1) Al-Qummī [narrated] this verse along with the verse in Sūrat al-Aḥzāb: ** وَإِذْ أَخَذْنا مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِيثاقَهُمْ وَمِنْكَ وَمِنْ نُوحٍ ** (And when We took a covenant from the prophets, and from you, and from Noah) and the verse in Sūrat al-A'rāf: ** وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِنْ بَنِي آدَمَ مِنْ ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ (When your Lord took from the children of Adam, from their loins, their descendants). And these three verses have been written in three Sūrahs. (From him, may his secret be sanctified (قُدِّسَ سِرُّه)).

(٨٢) فَمَنْ تَوَلَّى بَعْدَ ذلِكَ (82) So whoever turns away after that— [Meaning:] the covenant and the confirmation— ** فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ it is they who are the transgressors. [Meaning:] the rebellious ones among the disbelievers. (٨٣) أَفَغَيْرَ دِينِ اللهِ يَبْغُونَ (83) Do they seek a religion other than Allah’s, And it was recited with the tā’ (ت). ** وَلَهُ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ طَوْعاً وَكَرْهاً. while to Him have submitted those in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly,

In al-Tawhid and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is their affirmation of the Oneness (توحيد) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): Its meaning is that some peoples were compelled to accept Islam, and some peoples came willingly. He said: "Unwillingly (كرهاً)" means out of fear of the sword.

I say: Perhaps the intent is that this will occur during the time of the Qā’im (peace be upon him), just as al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): that it was revealed concerning the Qā’im (peace be upon him).

And in a narration: He recited it and then said: "When the Qā’im (peace and blessings be upon him) rises, there will remain no land except that the testimony that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah will be proclaimed therein."

وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ and to Him they will be brought back? And it was recited with the tā’ (ت).

(٨٤) قُلْ آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَما أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْنا وَما أُنْزِلَ عَلى إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْماعِيلَ وَإِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْباطِ وَما أُوتِيَ مُوسى وَعِيسى وَالنَّبِيُّونَ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ (84) Say, 'We have faith in Allah, and in what has been sent down to us, and what was sent down to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes, and what Moses and Jesus were given, and what the prophets were given from their Lord.' He commanded the Messenger to inform concerning himself and his followers regarding faith: We do not differentiate between any one of them by affirmation and denial وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ (and to Him we submit), meaning: submissive, sincere in His worship.

(٨٥) وَمَنْ يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ الْإِسْلامِ دِيناً (85) Should anyone desire a religion other than Islam, meaning, other than monotheism (التوحيد) and submission (الانقياد) to the command of Allah the Almighty. فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ وَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ it shall never be accepted from him, and he will be among the losers in the Hereafter. by his invalidating the sound disposition (الفطرة السليمة) upon which people were created.

(٨٦) كَيْفَ يَهْدِي اللهُ قَوْماً كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمانِهِمْ وَشَهِدُوا أَنَّ الرَّسُولَ حَقٌ (86) How shall Allah guide a people who disbelieved after their belief, and [after] they had testified that the Apostle is true وَجاءَهُمُ الْبَيِّناتُ and manifest proofs had come to them? [This is] a declaration of improbability (استبعاد) that Allah should guide them, for the one who deviates (الحائد) from the truth after it has become clear to him is immersed (منهمك) in misguidance (الضلال), far removed from guidance (الرشاد). ** وَشَهِدُوا is grammatically conjoined (عطف) to the verbal meaning (معنى الفعل) contained within ** إِيمانِهِمْ (their belief), or it is a circumstantial qualifier (حال) with an implied (بإضمار) قد (قد). ** وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ**. And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

(٨٧) أُولئِكَ جَزاؤُهُمْ أَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةَ اللهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ. (87) Their requital is that the curse of Allah and the angels and all mankind shall be upon them. (٨٨) خالِدِينَ فِيها لا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذابُ وَلا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ. (88) They shall remain in it [forever]; the punishment shall not be lightened for them, nor will they be granted any respite. (٨٩) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ (89) Excepting those who repent after that and reform, for Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. He accepts their repentance, All-merciful (رَحِيمٌ), He bestows His favor upon them. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The verses were revealed concerning a man from the الأنصار named al-Harith ibn Suwayd ibn al-Samit. He had killed al-Muhadhdhar ibn Ziyad al-Balawi treacherously (غدراً), fled, apostatized from Islam, and joined Mecca. He regretted it, so he sent word to his people: "Ask the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم if there is any repentance for me." They asked, and the verses were revealed. A man from his people carried them to him and said: "I certainly know that you are truthful, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is more truthful than you, and Allah the Almighty is the most truthful of the three." He returned to al-Madinah, repented, and his Islam became good.

(٩٠) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِيمانِهِمْ ثُمَّ ازْدادُوا كُفْراً (90) Indeed those who disbelieve after their faith, and then increase in disbelief,

Like the Jews who disbelieved in Jesus (عيسى) and the Gospel (الإنجيل) after believing in Moses (موسى) and the Torah (التوراة), then they increased in disbelief by [disbelieving in] Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Quran (القرآن). Or they disbelieved in Muhammad after they had believed in him before his mission (مبعثه), then ازْدادُوا كُفْراً (they increased in disbelief) through persistence (الإصرار), obstinacy (العناد), slandering him (الطعن فيه), hindering [others] from faith (الصد عن الايمان), and breaking the covenant (نقض الميثاق). And like a people who apostatized (ارتدوا) and joined Mecca (مكّة), then they increased in disbelief by their saying: "We await the calamities of fate (ريب المنون) for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم," or "We will return to him and act hypocritically (ننافقه) by feigning repentance (إظهار التوبة)." لَنْ تُقْبَلَ تَوْبَتُهُمْ their repentance shall never be accepted,

because their repentance is not based on sincerity (الإخلاص), or because it only occurs at the time of despair (اليأس) and witnessing death (معاينة الموت). وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الضَّالُّونَ and it is they who are the astray.

[meaning] those who are firmly established upon misguidance (الضلال).

(٩١) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَماتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَنْ يُقْبَلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِمْ مِلْءُ الْأَرْضِ ذَهَباً (Indeed, those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers, the earth full of gold shall not be accepted from any of them, even if he offered it as a ransom.)

That which fills the earth with gold, وَلَوِ افْتَدى بِهِ for himself from the punishment. It is said that its estimation (تقديره) is: the earth full of gold shall not be accepted from any of them as a ransom, even if he offered as ransom the earth full of gold. And it is possible that the intended meaning (المراد) is: shall not be accepted from any of them his spending (إنفاقه) in the way of Allah, [even if it were] the earth full of gold in this world (في الدنيا), even if it were in the manner of ransom (الافتداء) from the punishment of the Hereafter, without expecting any other reward (ثواب آخر). ** أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ وَما لَهُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ**. (Those are the ones for whom there is a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers.)

(٩٢) لَنْ تَنالُوا الْبِرَّ (92) (You will never attain piety) You will not reach its reality, nor will you become truly pious (أبرار), ** حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ (until you spend out of what you hold dear.) from wealth, status (الْجَاه), life-blood (الْمُهْجَة), and other things, in obedience to Allah.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** لَنْ تَنالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا ما تُحِبُّونَ** (You will never attain piety until you spend what you hold dear). He said: "Read it thus."

And in al-Majma': Ali (blessings and peace of Allah be upon him) bought a garment, and it pleased him, so he gave it away in charity. He said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم say: 'Whoever prefers others over himself (آثَرَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ), Allah will prefer him with Paradise on the Day of Resurrection. And whoever loves something and dedicates it for Allah, Allah will say on the Day of Resurrection: The servants used to recompense one another with kindness (بِالْمَعْرُوف) amongst themselves, but today I shall recompense you with Paradise.'"

And narrated from al-Husayn ibn Ali and al-Sadiq (blessings of Allah be upon them): They both used to give sugar (السُّكَّر) in charity, and they would say: "It is the dearest of things to us, and Allah the Almighty has said: لَنْ تَنالُوا الْبِرَّ حَتَّى تُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ (You will never attain piety until you spend out of what you hold dear.)" And whatever thing you spend, whether beloved or otherwise, ** فَإِنَّ اللهَ بِهِ عَلِيمٌ** (then Allah is indeed aware of it.) and He will recompense you according to it.

(٩٣) كُلُّ الطَّعامِ أي الطعومات ** كانَ حِلًّا لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ كان أكلها حلالاً لهم والحل مصدر نعت به ** إِلَّا ما حَرَّمَ إِسْرائِيلُ يعقوب ** عَلى نَفْسِهِ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تُنَزَّلَ التَّوْراةُ**. (93) All food was lawful for the Children of Israel, except what Israel forbade himself before the Torah was sent down.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Israel (إسرائيل) used to suffer from pain in the flank (وجع الخاصرة) whenever he ate camel meat, so he forbade camel meat upon himself. And that was ** قَبْلِ أَنْ تُنَزَّلَ التَّوْراةُ** (before the Torah was sent down). When the Torah was sent down, He did not forbid it, and he did not eat it.

I say: It means Moses (موسى) did not forbid it, and he (Israel/Jacob) did not eat it. Or, the Torah did not forbid it, and it was not made to be eaten (ولم يؤكّلْه), meaning it was neglected and not encouraged to be eaten (derived from التأكيل).

And al-Qummi (narrates): Jacob (يعقوب) used to suffer from sciatica (عرق النساء), so he forbade camel meat upon himself. The Jews then said that the camel was forbidden in the Torah. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to them: قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِالتَّوْراةِ فَاتْلُوها إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (Say: 'Bring the Torah and recite it, should you be truthful'). Israel (إسرائيل) only forbade this upon himself and did not forbid it upon the people.

The summary of the meaning is that all foods (المطاعم) remained lawful for the Children of Israel before the sending down of the Torah. The prohibition of what was forbidden to them later, due to their injustice (ظلمهم) and transgression (بغيهم), did not include anything forbidden before that time, except for the food that Israel (إسرائيل) forbade upon himself. This is a refutation of the Jews, as they sought to clear themselves of what the Quran stated regarding the prohibition of good things (الطيّبات) upon them due to their transgression and injustice, as mentioned in His saying: ** ذلِكَ جَزَيْناهُمْ بِبَغْيِهِمْ** (That is how We requited them for their transgression).

And His saying: ** فَبِظُلْمٍ مِنَ الَّذِينَ هادُوا حَرَّمْنا عَلَيْهِمْ طَيِّباتٍ أُحِلَّتْ لَهُمْ** (So, for the wrong-doing of the Jews, We forbade them good things which were lawful for them).

They said: "We are not the first upon whom things were forbidden. They were already forbidden to Noah (نوح) and Abraham (إبراهيم) and those among the Children of Israel who came after them, until the prohibition reached us." So Allah gave the lie to them: ** قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِالتَّوْراةِ فَاتْلُوها إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (Say: 'Bring the Torah and recite it, should you be truthful'). He commanded that they be refuted using their own Book and that they be silenced by what is contained therein, until it becomes clear that it was a recent prohibition (تحريم حادث) due to their injustice and transgression, and not an ancient prohibition (تحريم قديم) as they claimed. They were unable to produce the Torah, so they were confounded (فبهتوا).

(٩٤) فَمَنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ الْكَذِبَ (94) So whoever fabricates a lie against Allah by claiming (بزعمه) that that was forbidden (محرماً) upon the Prophets (الأنبياء) and upon the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل) before the sending down (انزال) of the Torah (التوراة), مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ after that, after the proof (الحجّة) became binding upon them, فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ they are the wrongdoers. to their souls (لأنفسهم) because of their arrogance (لمكابرتهم) toward the truth (الحقّ) after its clarity (وضوحه).

(٩٥) قُلْ صَدَقَ اللهُ (95) Say, 'Allah has spoken the truth.' This is an allusion to their falsehood, meaning it is established that Allah is truthful in what He revealed, and you are the liars. فَاتَّبِعُوا مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ حَنِيفاً So follow the creed of Abraham, a ḥanīf. And it is the creed of Islam which Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and those who believed with him are upon. Then He, glory be to Him, acquitted Abraham from what the Jews and the polytheists used to attribute to him regarding his being upon their religion, so He said: وَما كانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ. and he was not one of the polytheists.

(٩٦) إِنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِلنَّاسِ ** so that it may be a place of worship (مُتَعَبَّدًا) for them لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ ** the House (البيت) which is in Bakka, and it is the Ka'bah. (96) Indeed the first house to be set up for mankind is the one at Bakka,

In al-Kafi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them), and in al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When Allah Almighty desired to create the earth, He commanded the winds, and they struck the surface of the water until it became waves, then it foamed and became a single foam (زَبَدًا وَاحِدًا). Then He gathered it in the place of the House (البيت), then He made it a mountain of foam, then He spread (دَحَى) the earth from beneath it. And this is the saying of Allah Almighty: إِنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِلنَّاسِ لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ مُبارَكاً (96) Indeed the first house to be set up for mankind is the one at Bakka, blessed,

And it is added in al-Faqih: The first spot (بُقْعَة) created on earth was the Ka'bah, then the earth was extended (مُدَّت) from it.

And therein (فيه): Allah chose something from everything; He chose the location of the Ka'bah from the earth.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Makkah was only named Bakka because people crowd (يَبُكُّونَ) therein, meaning they jostle (يَزْدَحِمُونَ).

And in another narration: [It is due] to the weeping (بُكَاءِ) of people around it and within it. And it is said: Because it crushes (تَبُكُّ) the necks of the tyrants (الْجَبَابِرَة), meaning it breaks them (تَدُقُّهَا).

And from him (peace be upon him): The location of the House (البيت) is Bakka, and the town (القرية) is Makkah.
