And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Makkah was only named Bakka because men and women crowd (يَبُكُّ) therein, and a woman may pray in front of you, to your right, to your left, and with you, and there is no harm in that, because it is only disliked (يُكْرَهُ) in other lands (سَائِرِ الْبُلْدَانِ).

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The names of Makkah are five: Umm al-Qura (أُمُّ الْقُرَى), Makkah (مَكَّة), Bakka (بَكَّة), and al-Bassasah (الْبَسَّاسَة) (1) — if they committed injustice therein, She expelled them (بَسَّتْهُمْ), meaning She drove them out (أَخْرَجَتْهُمْ) and destroyed them (أَهْلَكَتْهُمْ); and Umm Ruhum (أُمُّ رُحُم) — they were shown mercy (رُحِمُوا) if they adhered to Her. And the like of this is found in al-Faqih as a mursal (unattributed) narration.

And therein (فيه), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent it down for Adam from Paradise, and it was a white pearl (دُرَّة بَيْضَاء). Then Allah raised it to the heaven, and its foundation (أُسُّهُ) remained, and it is directly above this House (البيت). Seventy thousand angels enter it every day, never to return to it again. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded Abraham (إبراهيم) and Ishmael (إسماعيل) to build the House upon the foundations (الْقَوَاعِد).

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The location of the Ka'bah was a white mound (رَبْوَة) of earth, shining like the light of the sun and the moon, until the two sons of Adam killed one another, whereupon it turned black. When Adam descended, Allah Almighty raised the entire earth for him until he saw it, then He said: "This is all for you." He said: "O Lord, what is this bright white land?" He said: "It is My Sanctuary (حَرَمِي) on My earth, and I have made it incumbent upon you to circumambulate (تَطُوفَ) it seven hundred times every day."

In al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: It was found inscribed on a stone (حَجَر): "I am Allah, the Lord of Bakka. I created Her the day I created the heavens and the earth, and the day I created the sun and the moon. And I surrounded Her with seven angels, a blessed surrounding (حَفًّا مُبَارَكًا) for Her people, in water and milk. Her provision comes to Her from three paths: from above Her, from below Her, and the third path (الثَّنِيَّة) after it." مُبارَكاً [meaning] abundant in goodness and benefit for whoever performs the Hajj (حَجَّهُ) or the Umrah (اعْتَمَرَهُ), or performs i'tikaf (اعْتَكَفَ) near it, or circumambulates around it, or intends toward it, in terms of multiplying the reward (الثَّوَاب), expiating sins (تَكْفِيرِ الذُّنُوب), removing poverty (نَفْيِ الْفَقْر), increasing provision (كَثْرَةِ الرِّزْق), وَهُدىً لِلْعالَمِينَ because it is their Qiblah (قِبْلَتَهُمْ) and their place of worship (مُتَعَبَّدَهُمْ). (96) Indeed the first house to be set up for mankind is the one at Bakka, blessed, and a guidance for all the nations.

footnote (١) And in a narration in *al-Kafi*: It was named Bakka because it crushes (*تَبُكُّ*) the necks of the transgressors (*الْبَاغِينَ*) if they transgress therein. And *al-Ayyashi* narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was named Bakka because people jostle (*يَبُكُّ*) one another with their hands. And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Bakka is the location of the House (*البيت*), and Makkah is everything encompassed by the Sanctuary (*الْحَرَم*). And *al-Bass* (*الْبَسّ*) with the single dot (*بِالْمُوَحَّدَة*) means destruction (*الطَّمّ*), and with the *Nun* (*بِالنُّون*) means expulsion (*الطَّرْد*), and it is narrated with both. And *al-Ruhum* (*الرُّحُم*) with the *dammah* (*بِالضَّمّ*) means mercy (*الرَّحْمَة*). Allah Almighty said: **وَأَقْرَبَ رُحْماً** (and closer in mercy). And sometimes it is vocalized (*يُحَرَّك*) (from him, may Allah have mercy on him).

(97) فِيهِ آياتٌ بَيِّناتٌ (1) (In it are manifest signs). Such as His subjugation of those tyrants who sought to harm it, like the Companions of the Elephant (أصحاب الفيل), and other such instances. مَقامُ إِبْراهِيمَ (the standing place of Abraham). Meaning, among those signs is the standing place of Abraham.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what these manifest signs (الآيات البيّنات) were. He said: "The standing place of Abraham (مَقامُ إِبْراهِيمَ), where he stood upon the stone and his feet left their mark upon it, and the Black Stone (الحجر الأسود), and the dwelling place of Ishmael (منزل إسماعيل)."

I say: As for the standing place (المقام) being a sign (آية), it is due to what has been mentioned, and because of its elevation by Abraham (peace be upon him) until it became taller than the mountains, as its mention will come in Surat al-Hajj, if Allah wills. As for the Black Stone (الحجر الأسود) being a sign, it is due to the wonders that appeared from it for the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Successors (الأوصياء), since it was a jewel that Allah placed with Adam in Paradise (الجنّة), and since it was an angel from the great angels (عظماء الملائكة) to whom Allah entrusted the Covenant (الميثاق) and deposited it with Him. It will come on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) having a speaking tongue and two eyes, recognized by creation, bearing witness for the one who fulfilled his commitment to it with fulfillment, and for the one who rendered the Covenant to it with rendering, and against the one who denied it with denial, and other such matters, as has been narrated in the reports from the Pure Imams (الأئمة الأطهار). And also due to what appeared to a group regarding its speaking to some of the Infallibles (المعصومين), such as al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), when his uncle Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah disputed with him concerning the matter of the Imamate (الإمامة), as mentioned in the narrations, and due to its lack of obedience to anyone other than the Infallible (المعصوم) when placing it in its position, as has been experienced more than once. As for the dwelling place of Ishmael (منزل إسماعيل) being a sign, it is because he was brought down there without water, yet water gushed forth for him. The standing place (المقام) was specifically mentioned in the Qur'an, while the mention of others was omitted, because it is the most manifest of His signs to people today. It was said: The reason for this mark (الأثر) is that when the structure of the Ka'bah was raised, he stood upon this stone so that he could lift the stones, and his feet sank into it. And it was said: When he came as a visitor from Syria (الشّام) to Mecca (مكّة), Ishmael's wife said to him, "Dismount so that we may wash your head." But he did not dismount. So she brought him this stone and placed it on his right side, and he placed his foot upon it until she washed that side of his head. Then she moved it to his left side until she washed the other side, and the mark of his two feet remained upon it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The location of the standing place (المقام) that Abraham (peace be upon him) had placed was near the wall of the House (جدار البيت). It remained there until the people of the Age of Ignorance (أهل الجاهلية) moved it to the place where it is today. When the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) conquered Mecca, he returned it to the location where Abraham (peace be upon him) had placed it. It remained there until 'Umar ibn al-Khattab assumed authority. He asked the people, "Who among you knows the place where the standing place (المقام) was?" A man said, "I measured its distance with a woven strap (نسع) (1), and I have it with me." He said, "Bring it to me." So he brought it to him, and he measured it and then returned it to that location. وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ كانَ آمِناً (and whoever enters it shall be safe).

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said to Abu Hanifah: "Inform me about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ كانَ آمِناً (and whoever enters it shall be safe). Where on earth is that?" He replied: "The Ka'bah." He said: "Do you know that when al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf placed the catapult (المنجنيق) upon Ibn al-Zubayr in the Ka'bah and killed him, was he safe therein?" He said: So he remained silent. He (Imam al-Sadiq) asked him for the answer, and He said: "Whoever pledges allegiance to our Qa'im (قائمنا), enters with Him, wipes his hand upon His hand, and enters into the covenant of His companions كانَ آمِناً (shall be safe)."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): مَنْ دَخَلَهُ (whoever enters it) while being aware of our right (حقنا) as He is aware of it, he departs from his sins and is spared the worries of this world and the Hereafter.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "Whoever intends this House (البيت) knowing that it is the House with which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded, and recognizes us, the People of the House (أهل البيت), with the true recognition of us, كانَ آمِناً (shall be safe) in this world and the Hereafter."

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That مَنْ دَخَلَهُ (whoever enters it) knowing all that Allah has made obligatory upon him كانَ آمِناً (shall be safe) in the Hereafter from the everlasting punishment.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the supplications for entering the House (البيت): "O Allah, You said: وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ كانَ آمِناً (and whoever enters it shall be safe), so grant me safety from the punishment of the Fire."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "Whoever among the people enters the Sanctuary (الحرم) seeking refuge in it, he is safe therein from the wrath of Allah. وَمَنْ دَخَلَهُ (and whoever enters it) from the wild beasts and birds shall be safe from being agitated or harmed until it leaves the Sanctuary."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "If a servant commits a crime (جناية) outside the Sanctuary (الحرم) and then flees to the Sanctuary, it is not permissible for anyone to seize him within the Sanctuary. However, he is prevented from the marketplace (السوق); he is not sold to, nor fed, nor given drink, nor spoken to. If this is done, he is likely to leave and be apprehended. But if he commits a crime within the Sanctuary, the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) is carried out upon him within the Sanctuary."

And it is added in al-Kafi: "Because he did not respect the sanctity of the Sanctuary (الحرم)."

And in a narration: If a thief steals outside Mecca (مكّة) or commits a crime against himself and flees to the Sanctuary (الحرم), he is not apprehended as long as he is in the Sanctuary until he leaves it. However, he is prevented from the marketplace (السوق); he is not sold to, nor sat with, until he leaves it and is apprehended. But if he commits that offense within the Sanctuary, he is apprehended therein.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Samā'ah asked Him about a man who owed him money and had been absent from him for a time, and he saw him circumambulating the Ka'bah. "Should I demand my money from him?" He said: "No. Do not greet him, nor frighten him, until he leaves the Sanctuary (الحرم)."

And narrated from Him: Whoever is buried in the Sanctuary (الحرم) is safe from the Greatest Terror (الفزع الأكبر), whether they are righteous (برّ) or wicked (فاجر) among the people.

In al-Faqih: Whoever dies in one of the Two Sanctuaries (الحرمين) will be resurrected by Allah among those who are safe. And whoever dies between the Two Sanctuaries, no record (ديوان) will be spread out for him. And whoever is buried in the Sanctuary (الحرم) is safe from the Greatest Terror (الفزع الأكبر). وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ (Pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by all mankind). And it was read with the kasrah on the ha' (حِجُّ), meaning the intention to perform the specific rites (المناسك المخصوصة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He meant by it the Hajj (الحجّ) and the Umrah (العمرة) together, because both are obligatory for مَنِ اسْتَطاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلاً (those who can afford the journey to it).

Al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "Health in his body and capability in his wealth."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked, "What is the way (السبيل)?" He said: "That he possesses what he can perform Hajj with." I said: "If someone is offered what he can perform Hajj with, but he feels ashamed of that, is he one of those who can afford the journey (يستطيع اليه سبيلاً)?" He said: "Yes. Why should he be ashamed? Even if he performs Hajj upon a donkey (حمار) that is mutilated and tailless (أجدع ابتر). If he is able to walk part of the way and ride part of the way, then let him perform Hajj." And in a narration: "He should go out and walk if he does not have [a mount]." It was said: "He cannot walk." He said: "He should walk and ride." It was said: "He cannot do that." He said: "He should serve the people (يخدم القوم) and go out with them." And in a narration: He (peace be upon him) was asked about this verse, and He said: "Whoever is healthy in his body, free in his path (مخلى سربه), and possesses provisions (زاد) and a mount (راحلة), he is one of those who can afford Hajj." Or He said: "One of those who possesses wealth (مال)." And in a narration: He (peace be upon him) was asked about this verse, and He said: "What do the people say?" It was said: "Provisions and a mount." He said: "Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) was asked about this and He said: 'The people would perish then! If the one who has provisions and a mount, only enough to sustain his dependents and make him independent of people, must go to them and ask them for it, then they have surely perished.' He was asked: 'And what is the way (السبيل)?' He said: 'It is abundance in wealth, such that he performs Hajj with a portion and keeps a portion to sustain his dependents. Did not Allah make the Zakat (الزكاة) obligatory only upon the one who owns two hundred dirhams?'"

I say: The meaning of the narration is: If the one who only possesses enough to sustain his dependents is obligated to spend that on provisions and a mount, and then goes to people asking them for the sustenance of his dependents, then the people would perish then. The variation in the narrations should be understood as relating to the differences among people regarding the aspects of affordability (الاستطاعة), the degrees of reliance [on Allah] (التوكل), and the levels of strength and weakness, for man is a witness against himself. وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعالَمِينَ (and whoever disbelieves, Allah is indeed self-sufficient, independent of the creatures). It was said that the word "disbelieved" (كفر) was placed in the position of "did not perform Hajj" to emphasize its obligation and to sternly warn the one who abandons it.

In al-Faqih and in the testament of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) to Ali (peace be upon him): "O Ali, the one who abandons Hajj while being able is a disbeliever (كافر). Allah the Almighty said: وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلاً وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعالَمِينَ (Pilgrimage to the House is a duty owed to Allah by all mankind—those who can afford the journey to it. And whoever disbelieves, Allah is indeed self-sufficient, independent of the creatures). O Ali, whoever postpones Hajj until he dies, Allah will resurrect him on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) as a Jew or a Christian."

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever dies and has not performed the obligatory Hajj (حجة الإسلام), and was not prevented from it by a pressing need (حاجة تجحف به), or an illness in which he cannot endure Hajj, or a ruler (سلطان) who prevents him, let him die a Jew or a Christian."

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: وَمَنْ كَفَرَ (and whoever disbelieves), He said: "Meaning, whoever abandons [it]."

And narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him), when his brother Ali asked Him: "Whoever among us does not perform Hajj has disbelieved?" He said: "No. But whoever says, 'This is not so,' has disbelieved."

I say: That is because disbelief (الكفر) relates to belief (الإعتقاد), not merely action. Thus, the Almighty's saying: وَمَنْ كَفَرَ (and whoever disbelieves) means: whoever does not believe in its obligation, or does not care about abandoning it, for lack of care reverts to lack of belief.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: It is disbelief in (1) blessings (كفر النعم). And He said: Meaning, whoever abandons [it]. And it was narrated: When the beginning of the verse was revealed, the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) gathered the followers of the religions (أرباب الملل) and addressed them, saying: "Allah has prescribed Hajj upon you, so perform Hajj." One nation believed in it, and five nations disbelieved. Then وَمَنْ كَفَرَ (and whoever disbelieves) was revealed. It was said: The command of Hajj was emphasized in this verse through several aspects indicating its obligation: by using the form of a statement (صيغة الخبر), by presenting it in the nominal form (صورة الاسمية), by stating it in a manner that conveys that it is a mandatory right (حقّ واجب) owed to Allah the Almighty upon the necks of mankind, by generalizing the ruling first and then specifying it (which is like clarification after ambiguity), by repeating it (which is a reiteration of the intent), and by naming the abandonment of Hajj as disbelief (كفراً) because it is the action of the disbelievers (الكفرة). And that self-sufficiency (الاستغناء) in this context indicates hatred (المقت) and abandonment (الخذلان). And His saying عَنِ الْعالَمِينَ (independent of the creatures) is a substitute for it, due to the exaggeration of generalization contained therein, the indication of His self-sufficiency from it by proof (البرهان), and the notification of the greatness of His wrath, because it is a difficult obligation (تكليف شاقّ) that combines the breaking of the ego (كسر النفس), the tiring of the body, the spending of wealth, the stripping away of desires (التجرد عن الشهوات), and turning towards Allah the Almighty.

(1) Narrated from Ibn Abbas: He read āyatun bayyinah (a manifest sign) مَقامُ إِبْراهِيمَ (the standing place of Abraham), thus making مَقامُ إِبْراهِيمَ alone the sign, and he said that the mark of his feet in the standing place is a manifest sign. This is according to al-Majma'. And it was said that all the sacred sites (المشاعر) are آياتٌ بَيِّناتٌ (manifest signs) due to the crowding of people upon them and their veneration of them. And it is recounted that the circumambulation (الطواف) of the House never ceases, and due to the deviation of birds from flying directly over the House, and the mixing of hunted animals in the Sanctuary (الحرم) with predatory beasts, and their familiarity with people, and the disappearance of the pebbles (الجمار) despite the multitude of throwers—for if they were not lifted, stones like mountains would accumulate there—and other such reasons. (From him [the author]).

(1) Al-Nis' (النسع) with a kasrah: A wide woven strap used to tie saddles. (From him, may his secret be sanctified).

(1) Because obeying the command of Allah is gratitude for His blessing, and abandoning what is commanded is disbelief in His blessing (كفر لنعمته). (Majma')

(٩٨) قُلْ يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لِمَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ (98) Say, 'O People of the Book! Why do you disbelieve in the signs of Allah, meaning, by His audible (السمعية) and rational (العقليّة) signs which indicate the truthfulness of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding what He claims, such as the obligation of Hajj and other matters. And the specification of the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) with the address is evidence that their disbelief is more heinous (أقبح), and that even if they claim that they are believers in the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإنجيل), they are disbelievers in both of them. وَاللهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلى ما تَعْمَلُونَ and Allah is witness to what you do?' while He is a Witness, fully aware (مطّلع) of your deeds, so He will recompense you for them. Neither alteration (التحريف) nor concealment (الاستستار) will benefit you (1).

(99) قُلْ يا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ لِمَ تَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ مَنْ آمَنَ (99) Say, 'O People of the Book! Why do you bar from the way of Allah those who have faith?'

He repeated the address and the interrogation, exaggerating the censure and negating any excuse for them, and indicating that each one of the two matters is inherently repulsive and independently sufficient to bring about punishment. And the way of Allah (سَبِيلِ اللهِ) is His true religion, which is commanded to be followed, and that is Islam (الإِسلام). It was said that they used to tempt the believers and incite (يُحَرِّشُونَ) (2) discord among them, even going so far as to approach the Aws and the Khazraj and remind them of the enmity and warfare that existed between them during al-Jahiliyyah (الجاهلية), so that they might return to the like of it, and they sought means to bar them from it. ** تَبْغُونَها عِوَجاً seeking to make it crooked? seeking crookedness for it, by confusing the people and making them imagine that there is a deviation (عِوَجاً) from the truth in it, such as by denying abrogation (النسخ) and altering the description of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and similar actions; or by inciting discord among the believers so that their unity (كَلِمَتُهُمْ) might be broken and the affair of their religion (دِينِهِمْ) might be disrupted, وَأَنْتُمْ شُهَداءُ while you are witnesses that it is the way of Allah, the Exalted, and barring others from it is misguidance (ضَلَال) and leading astray (إِضْلَال). Or (it means) you are trustworthy (عُدُول) among the people of your religion, who trust your statements and seek your testimony in legal matters (القضايا). ** وَمَا اللهُ ** بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ And Allah is not unaware of what you do. This is a threat/warning to them. And since the reprehensible act in the first Ayah was their disbelief (كُفْرُهُمْ), which they openly declared, He concluded it with His saying: And Allah is witness. And since in this Ayah (the reprehensible act) was their barring the believers from Islam (الإِسلام), which they used to conceal and seek means (يَحْتَالُونَ) to achieve, He said: And Allah is not unaware of what you do.**

(١٠٠) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنْ تُطِيعُوا فَرِيقاً مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ يَرُدُّوكُمْ بَعْدَ إِيمانِكُمْ كافِرِينَ (100) O you who have faith! If you obey a faction of those who were given the Book, they will turn you back as faithless after your faith.

It is said that [this verse] was revealed concerning a group (نفر) from the Aws and the Khazraj who were sitting and conversing. Sās ibn Qays the Jew passed by them, and their harmony (تألّفهم) and gathering enraged him. So, he commanded a young man from the Jews to sit with them and remind them of the Day of Bu'āth (يوم بغاث) (١) and recite to them some of what was said concerning it. The victory on that day had belonged to the Aws. He did so, and the people disputed, boasted, and became angry. They said: "Weapons! Weapons!" And a great multitude (خلق عظيم) gathered from the two tribes. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his Companions went towards them. He said: "Do you call upon the ways of Jāhiliyyah (الجاهلية) while I am among you, after Allah honored you with Islam, and thereby cut off from you the matter of Jāhiliyyah (الجاهلية), and brought harmony between you?" They realized that it was an instigation (نزغة) (٢) from Satan and a plot (كيد) from their enemy. So, they cast down their weapons, sought forgiveness, embraced one another, and departed with the Messenger. Allah addressed them Himself, after commanding the Messenger to address the People of the Book, only to demonstrate the majesty of their status (جلالة قدرهم) and to indicate that they are the ones most deserving that Allah should address them and speak to them.

وَكَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ (١٠١) وَأَنْتُمْ تُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ آياتُ اللهِ وَفِيكُمْ رَسُولُهُ (101) How can you disbelieve, while Allah’s signs are recited to you and His Apostle is among you? [This is] a denial and astonishment at their disbelief while the causes inviting to faith and turning away from disbelief have gathered for them. وَمَنْ يَعْتَصِمْ بِاللهِ And whoever takes hold of Allah And whoever holds fast to His religion or seeks refuge in Him in the totality of his affairs ** فَقَدْ هُدِيَ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ** is certainly guided to a straight path. Then he has been guided inevitably.

يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ حَقَّ تُقاتِهِ (102) (O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah with the wariness due to Him) (meaning) the wariness due to Him (حَقَّ تُقاتِهِ) and what is obligatory from it, which is exhausting one's utmost effort in fulfilling the obligations (الواجب) and avoiding the forbidden things (المحارم).

In al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, it is narrated: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about this verse. He said: "He is obeyed and not disobeyed; He is remembered and not forgotten; and He is thanked and not denied."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it (the verse) and he said: "It is abrogated (منسوخة)." It was asked: "And what abrogated it?" He said: "The saying of Allah: فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ وَلا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ (16) (So be wary of Allah, as much as you can, and do not die except as Muslims.) And do not be in any state other than the state of Islam (الإِسلام) when death overtakes you."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** وَأَنْتُمْ (wa antum) is read as musallimūna (مُسَلِّمُونَ) with tashdid (بالتشديد), and its meaning is: submitting (مستسلمون) to what the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought, and obedient (منقادون) to it.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him), that He said to one of His companions: "How do you read this verse: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ حَقَّ تُقاتِهِ وَلا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنْتُمْ what?" He replied: مُسْلِمُونَ (muslimūna). He (Imam al-Kazim) said: "Glory be to Allah (سبحان الله)! He imposes faith upon them and calls them believers (مؤمنين), then He asks them for Islam (الإسلام), while faith (الإيمان) is above Islam?" He (the companion) said: "This is how it is read in the recitation (قراءة) of Zayd." He (Imam al-Kazim) said: "It is only in the recitation (قراءة) of Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him), and it is the revelation (التنزيل) that Gabriel brought down to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: ** إِلَّا وَأَنْتُمْ musallimūna (مُسَلِّمُونَ) to the Messenger of Allah, and then to the Imam (الإِمام) after him."

(١٠٣) وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللهِ (And hold fast to the cord of Allah) It was said: [It refers] to His religion, Islam, or to His Book, due to His saying: "The Quran is the strong cord (حبل الله المتين) of Allah." He used the metaphor of the cord (الحبل) for it, and holding fast (الاعتصام) to it is trusted, in that adherence to it is the cause of salvation from destruction (الردى), just as holding fast to a trusted cord is the cause of safety from falling (التردّي). And al-Qummi [said]: The cord (الحبل) is al-Tawhid (monotheism) and al-Wilayah (guardianship/authority). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) are the strong cord (حَبْل اللهِ المتين) of Allah, which He commanded [us] to hold fast to, for He said: ** وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللهِ جَمِيعاً وَلا تَفَرَّقُوا (And hold fast to the cord of Allah, all of you, and do not scatter) And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) is the strong cord (حَبْل اللهِ المتين) of Allah. And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sajjad, he said: The Imam from among us is not except one who is infallible (معصوماً). And infallibility (العصمة) is not in the outward appearance of creation (ظاهر الخلقة) such that he is known by it. Therefore, he is not except one who is designated (منصوصاً). So it was said to him: "O son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, what is the meaning of the infallible (المعصوم)?" He replied: "He is the one who holds fast (المعتصم) to the cord of Allah (حبل الله), and the cord of Allah is the Quran, and the Quran guides to the Imam." And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ** إِنَّ هذَا الْقُرْآنَ يَهْدِي لِلَّتِي هِيَ أَقْوَمُ. (Indeed this Quran guides to what is most upright.) I say: And the ultimate meaning of all [these interpretations] is one, which is explained by the saying of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Two extended cords, one end of which is in the Hand of Allah and the other end is in your hands, and they shall never separate." جَمِيعاً (all of you), meaning: being united upon it. ** وَلا تَفَرَّقُوا (and do not scatter), meaning: do not separate from the truth by causing disagreement among yourselves. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, knew that they would separate after their Prophet and disagree, so He forbade them from separation, just as He forbade those who were before them. Thus, He commanded them to unite upon the Wilayah (guardianship) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and not to separate. وَاذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ كُنْتُمْ أَعْداءً (And remember Allah’s blessing upon you when you were enemies) in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية), fighting one another. ** فَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِكُمْ (and He brought your hearts together) through Islam. فَأَصْبَحْتُمْ بِنِعْمَتِهِ إِخْواناً (so you became brothers by His blessing), loving one another, united upon brotherhood in Allah, the Exalted. It was said that the Aws and the Khazraj were two brothers from two parents, but enmity arose between their descendants, and the wars lasted for one hundred and twenty years, until Allah, the Exalted, extinguished them through Islam and brought them together through His Messenger. وَكُنْتُمْ عَلى شَفا حُفْرَةٍ مِنَ النَّارِ (and you were on the brink of a pit of the Fire), meaning: overlooking the fall into the Fire of Hell due to your disbelief, for if death had overtaken you in that state, you would have fallen into the Fire. ** فَأَنْقَذَكُمْ مِنْها. (then He rescued you from it.) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: ** فَأَنْقَذَكُمْ مِنْها (then He rescued you from it) by Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. By Allah, thus did Gabriel descend with it upon Muhammad. كَذلِكَ (Thus), meaning: like that clarification, يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ آياتِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ** (does Allah clarify His signs for you, so that you may be guided), intending your steadfastness upon guidance and your increase therein.

(104) وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ some of you أُمَّةٌ In al-Majma': Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) read it as (أئمة) [leaders]. يَدْعُونَ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ. (104) And let there be a nation among you who call to good, and enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about enjoining the right (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر): Is it obligatory upon the entire nation (الأمة)? He said: "No." It was asked: "And why?" He said: "It is only upon the strong, the obeyed, the one who knows the right (المعروف) from the wrong (المنكر), not upon the weak (الضَّعَفَة) who do not find a way (سبيلاً) to distinguish which from which—meaning, the truth (الحق) from the falsehood (الباطل). The proof for this is the Book of Allah, the Almighty, His saying: ** وَلْتَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أُمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ. This is specific (خاصّ) and not general (عام), just as Allah, the Almighty, said: وَمِنْ قَوْمِ مُوسى أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ (And among the people of Moses is a nation who guide by the truth and dispense justice by it). He did not say upon the nation of Moses (أمّة موسى) nor upon every people, while they were, at that time, different nations (أُمم مختلفة). And the nation (الأمة) is one or more, just as Allah, the Glorified, said: ** إِنَّ إِبْراهِيمَ كانَ أُمَّةً قانِتاً لِلَّهِ (Indeed, Abraham was a nation, obedient to Allah), meaning: obedient to Allah. And there is no blame (حرج) upon the one who knows this during this truce (الهدنة) if he has no power, no number, and no obedience." And he was asked about the Hadith that came from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, the best Jihad is a word of justice before a tyrannical ruler (إمام جائر)." What is its meaning? He said: "This is contingent upon him enjoining him [the ruler] after knowing him, and provided that [the ruler] accepts it from him; otherwise, no."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Only a believer (مؤمن) enjoins the right (يأمر بالمعروف) and forbids the wrong (ينهى عن المنكر) so that he may be admonished, or an ignorant person (جاهل) so that he may learn. As for the one with a sword (صاحب سيف) or a whip (سوط), then no."

And al-Qummi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: He said: "This is for the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) and those who follow them: ** يَدْعُونَ إِلَى الْخَيْرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ**."

And in Nahj al-Balaghah, he said: "And forbid the wrong (وانهوا عن المنكر) and desist from it (وتناهوا عنه), for you were only commanded to forbid after desisting." And he said: "May Allah curse those who enjoin the right (الآمرين بالمعروف) but abandon it, and those who forbid the wrong (النّاهين عن المنكر) but practice it." وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ (and it is they who are the felicitous)—those who are specified with the perfection of felicity (كمال الفلاح) and are most deserving of it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Enjoining the right (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر) are two creations (خلقان) of Allah, the Almighty. Whoever supports them, Allah grants him might (أعزه الله), and whoever abandons them, Allah abandons him (خذله الله)."

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He said: "People will continue to be in goodness as long as they enjoin the right (أمروا بالمعروف), forbid the wrong (نهوا عن المنكر), and cooperate in righteousness (البر). If they fail to do that, blessings (البركات) will be removed from them, some of them will be given authority over others, and they will have no helper (ناصر) on earth or in the heaven."

And in both [books], narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "There will be in the latter times a people among whom are followed hypocritical people (مراؤون) who recite the Quran (يتقرءون) (1) and practice asceticism (يتنسكون), being new, foolish ones (حدثاء سفهاء). They do not deem obligatory the enjoining of the right (أمراً بمعروف) nor the forbidding of the wrong (نهياً عن منكر) except when they are safe from harm. They seek dispensations (الرّخص) (1) and excuses (المعاذير) for themselves. They follow the slips (زلّات) of the scholars and the corruption of their knowledge. They turn to prayer (الصلاة) and fasting (الصيام) and that which does not burden them (يكلمهم) (2) in soul or wealth. But if prayer were to harm the rest of what they do with their wealth and bodies, they would abandon it, just as they abandoned the highest and most honorable of the obligatory duties (الفرائض): indeed, enjoining the right (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر) is a great obligatory duty (فريضة عظيمة) by which the obligatory duties are established. At that point, the wrath of Allah is completed upon them, and He encompasses them with His punishment, thereby destroying the righteous (الأبرار) in the abode of the wicked (الفجّار) and the young (الصغار) in the abode of the old (الكبار). Indeed, enjoining the right (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر) is the path (سبيل) of the Prophets and the methodology (منهاج) of the righteous. It is a great obligatory duty by which the obligatory duties are established, paths (المذاهب) are secured, earnings (المكاسب) become lawful, injustices (المظالم) are returned, the earth is cultivated (تعمر الأرض), justice is exacted from enemies (ينتصف من الأعداء), and affairs become upright. So, denounce [the wrong] with your hearts, speak out with your tongues, and strike their foreheads with them [your words]. And do not fear the blame of any blamer in the cause of Allah. If they are admonished or return to the truth (الحق), then there is no way against them: ** إِنَّمَا السَّبِيلُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَظْلِمُونَ النَّاسَ وَيَبْغُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (The way is only against those who wrong the people and commit aggression on the earth unjustly. For such there is a painful punishment). At that point, strive against them with your bodies and hate them with your hearts, not seeking authority (سلطاناً), nor desiring wealth, nor wishing for victory through oppression (الظلم), until they return to the command of Allah and proceed upon His obedience." Abu Ja’far (peace be upon him) said: "And Allah commanded the Prophet Shu’ayb (peace be upon him): 'I shall punish a hundred and forty thousand of your people: forty thousand of their wicked ones (شرارهم) and sixty thousand of their righteous ones (خيارهم).' He said: 'O Lord, these are the wicked, but what about the righteous?' So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him that they compromised with the people of disobedience (أهل المعاصي) and did not become angry for My anger."

footnote (1) It is read as: they worship (*تعبد*) and practice asceticism (*تنسك*) from *al-Nusk* (vocalized with a *dammah*, *fathah*, or *kasrah* on the *nūn*, or with two *dammahs*), meaning worship and every right belonging to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And *ḥudathāʾ* (*حدثاء*) is the plural of *ḥadīth* (*حديث*), like *sufahāʾ* (*سفهاء*), meaning new. The intended meaning is that their method is a recent, newly invented method, not the method of their predecessors, or that their nature is the nature of one who is young in age, not the nature of the elderly.

(105) وَلا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ تَفَرَّقُوا وَاخْتَلَفُوا (And do not be like those who became divided and differed) like the Jews and the Christians, who differed concerning monotheism (التوحيد), transcendence (التنزيه), and the states of the Hereafter. مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَهُمُ الْبَيِّناتُ (after the manifest proofs had come to them) the signs (الآيات) and arguments (الحجج) clarifying the truth, which necessitate agreement upon it. وَأُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ (and for them is a great punishment) a threat (وعيد) for those who became divided, and a warning (تهديد) against resembling them.

(106) يَوْمَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهٌ وَتَسْوَدُّ وُجُوهٌ (106) on the day when [some] faces will turn white and [some] faces will turn black.

[These are] two allusions to the manifestation of the radiance of joy and the gloom of fear on that Day. And it was said: The people of truth are marked by the whiteness of the face and the scroll (الصحيفة), the brightness of the skin, and the light running before them and on their right; and the people of falsehood are marked by the opposites of that. فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ اسْوَدَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ أَكَفَرْتُمْ بَعْدَ إِيمانِكُمْ As for those whose faces have turned black, [they will be asked]: 'Did you disbelieve after your faith?'

[This is understood] with the intention of the saying, meaning, it will be said to them: أَكَفَرْتُمْ (Did you disbelieve?). And the hamzah is for reprimand and astonishment at their condition.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): They are the people of innovations (البدع), desires (الأهواء), and false opinions (الآراء الباطلة) from this Ummah.

And narrated from the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family), he said: "By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, some of those who accompanied me will be driven away from the Pond (الحوض). Until when I see them being snatched away from me, I will surely say: 'My companions! My companions!' It will be said to me: 'You do not know what they innovated after you. They reverted on their heels, turning backward (القهقهرى).'" Al-Tha'labi mentioned this in his Tafsir. فَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ So taste the punishment

[This is] a command of humiliation. بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ because of what you used to disbelieve.

because of your disbelief.

(١٠٧) وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ ابْيَضَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فَفِي رَحْمَتِ اللهِ (107) But as for those whose faces are whitened, they will be in Allah’s mercy.

meaning Paradise (الجنة) or the everlasting reward (الثواب المخلد). He expressed this using "mercy" (الرحمة) as an alert that the believer, even if he spends his entire life immersed in obedience to Allah, will not enter Paradise except through His mercy and grace (فضله). It was said that the proper order (حقّ الترتيب) would have been to mention them first, but the intention was for the beginning and the end of the discourse to be the adornment (حلية) of the believers and their reward (ثوابهم). هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ They will remain in it forever. He presented it in the manner of a new beginning (الاستيناف) for emphasis, as if it were asked, "How will they be in it?" So He said: هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ (They will remain in it forever).

And al-Qummi narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: When this Ayah was revealed: يَوْمَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهٌ وَتَسْوَدُّ وُجُوهٌ (The day when [some] faces will turn white and [some] faces will turn black), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "My Ummah will come to me on the Day of Resurrection under five banners (رايات). One banner is that of the calf (عجل) of this Ummah. I will ask them: 'What did you do with the Two Weighty Things (الثقلين) after me?' They will say: 'As for the Greater (الأكبر), we distorted it (حرفناه) and cast it behind our backs. And as for the Lesser (الأصغر), we showed enmity towards it (عاديناه), hated it (أبغضناه), and wronged it (ظلمناه).' So I will say: 'Return to the Fire, thirsty (ظماء), parched (مظمئين), with your faces blackened (مسودّة وجوهكم).'

"Then a banner will come to me with the Pharaoh (فرعون) of this Ummah. I will say to them: 'What did you do with the Two Weighty Things after me?' They will say: 'As for the Greater, we distorted it, tore it apart (مزّقناه), and opposed it (خالفناه). And as for the Lesser, we showed enmity towards it and fought it (قاتلناه).' So I will say: 'Return to the Fire, thirsty, parched, with your faces blackened.'

"Then a banner will come to me with the Samiri (سامري) of this Ummah. I will say: 'What did you do with the Two Weighty Things after me?' They will say: 'As for the Greater, we disobeyed it (عصيناه) and abandoned it (تركناه). And as for the Lesser, we failed to support it (خذلناه) and neglected it (ضيّعناه).' So I will say: 'Return to the Fire, thirsty, parched, with your faces blackened.'

"Then a banner will come to me with Dhul-Thudayyah (ذي الثدية), along with the first and the last of the Khawarij (الخوارج). I will ask them: 'What did you do with the Two Weighty Things after me?' They will say: 'As for the Greater, we tore it apart and disavowed ourselves from it (برئنا منه). And as for the Lesser, we fought and killed (قاتلنا وقتلنا).' So I will say: 'Return to the Fire, thirsty, parched, with your faces blackened.'

"Then a banner will come to me—the banner of the Imam of the Pious (إمام المتّقين), the Master of the Successors (سيّد الوصيين), the Leader of the Bright-faced (قائد الغر المحجلين), and the Successor (وصيّ) of the Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds (ربّ العالمين). I will say to them: 'What did you do with the Two Weighty Things after me?' They will say: 'As for the Greater, we followed it (اتبعناه) and obeyed it (أطعناه). And as for the Lesser, we loved him (أحببناه), befriended him (واليناه), and supported him (نصرناه) until our blood was spilled for his sake (أهرقت فيه دماؤنا).' So I will say: 'Return to Paradise, fully quenched (رواء), satisfied (مرويّين), with your faces whitened (مبيضة وجوهكم).' Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited: يَوْمَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهٌ (The day when [some] faces will turn white) until His saying: خالِدُونَ (They will remain in it forever)."

(١٠٨) تِلْكَ آياتُ اللهِ (108) These are the signs of Allah Which have been revealed concerning His promise and His threat. ** نَتْلُوها عَلَيْكَ بِالْحَقِ** We recite them to you truthfully. Clothed in truth, there is no doubt in them. ** وَمَا اللهُ يُرِيدُ ظُلْماً لِلْعالَمِينَ ** And Allah does not desire any wrong for the creatures. Since injustice is impossible from Him, because the perpetrator of injustice is either ignorant of its ugliness or in need of performing it, and Allah is exalted far above ignorance and need.

(١٠٩) وَلِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth,) as ownership (مَلِكاً), dominion (مُلكاً), and creation (خلقاً). وَإِلَى اللهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ (and to Allah all matters are returned.) so He will recompense everyone according to what He promised and what He warned. (١١٠) كُنْتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ (You are the best nation) The verb كُنْتُمْ (you were/are) here encompasses all times, not being restricted to the past, similar to His saying, وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً (and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful). أُخْرِجَتْ (meaning: manifested) لِلنَّاسِ تَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ (brought forth for mankind: you enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong,) This is a new clause (استيناف) clarifying their being the best nation, or it is a second predicate (خبر ثان) for كُنْتُمْ. وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ (and you have faith in Allah.) This includes faith in everything that must be believed in, because faith in Him is only valid and recognized if faith is achieved in everything He commanded to be believed in. He only delayed mentioning it, though it ought to have been mentioned first, because the intention behind mentioning it was to indicate that they enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong out of faith in Allah, affirmation of Him, and manifestation of His religion.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That [a person] recited to him: كُنْتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ (You are the best nation). So he (peace be upon him) said: "The best nation are those who kill the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and al-Hasan and al-Husayn, the two sons of Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon them)?" The reciter said: "May I be made your ransom, how was it revealed?" He (peace be upon him) said: "It was revealed: You are the best Imams (أئمة) brought forth for mankind. Do you not see Allah's praise for them: تَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ?"

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that he said: In the recitation of Ali (قراءة عليّ): كُنْتُمْ خَيْرَ Imams (أئمة) أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ. He said: "They are the Family of Muhammad (آل محمد)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): This verse was only revealed to Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) specifically concerning him and the Successors (الأوصياء). So He said: You are the best Imams (أئمة) أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ تَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ . By Allah, thus did Gabriel (جبرئيل) descend with it, and He did not mean by it anyone except Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) and his Successors (peace be upon them).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "It means the nation (الأمة) for whom the supplication (دعوة) of Abraham (إبراهيم) was made incumbent. They are the nation in whom, from whom, and to whom Allah sent [the Messenger]. They are the Middle Nation (الأمة الوسطى), and they are خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ."

In al-Manaqib, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): You are the best nation (أمة) with the alif (بالألف). Gabriel (جبرائيل) descended with it, and He did not mean by it anyone except Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), Ali, and the Successors (الأوصياء) from his progeny (peace be upon them).

وَلَوْ آمَنَ أَهْلُ الْكِتابِ لَكانَ خَيْراً لَهُمْ مِنْهُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ (And had the People of the Book believed, it would have been better for them. Among them are the faithful,) such as Abdullah ibn Salam (عبد الله بن سلام) and his companions, وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ (but most of them are the transgressors,) meaning those who are rebellious (المتمردون) in disbelief (الكفر).

(111) لَنْ يَضُرُّوكُمْ إِلَّا أَذىً (They will not harm you except for a slight annoyance) a slight harm, such as stabbing (طعن) and threatening (تهديد). ** وَإِنْ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ يُوَلُّوكُمُ الْأَدْبَارَ (And if they fight you, they will turn their backs on you) and they will be defeated and will not harm you through killing or captivity. ** ثُمَّ لَا يُنْصَرُونَ (Then they will not be helped) Then there will not be anyone who helps them against you or repels your might (بأسكم) from them. And the matter was thus.

(١١٢) ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الذِّلَّةُ The humiliation (الذِّلَّة) is encompassing them, like the enclosure of a tent (البيت) pitched over its inhabitants. And the humiliation (الذِّلَّة) is the squandering (هدر) of life, wealth, and family, or the humiliation of clinging to falsehood (الباطل) and the poll-tax (الجزية). ** أَيْنَ ما ثُقِفُوا means: they were found (وجدوا). ** إِلَّا بِحَبْلٍ مِنَ اللهِ وَحَبْلٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ. Al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: "The covenant (الحبل) from Allah is the Book of Allah (كتاب الله), and the covenant (الحبل) from the people (الناس) is Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon him)." وَباؤُ بِغَضَبٍ مِنَ اللهِ means: they returned with it, having deserved it (مستوجبين له). وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَسْكَنَةُ ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ الْأَنْبِياءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ ذلِكَ بِما عَصَوْا وَكانُوا يَعْتَدُونَ. (Humiliation has been stamped upon them wherever they are found, except by a covenant with Allah or a treaty with the people. They have incurred Allah’s wrath, and wretchedness has been stamped upon them. That is because they used to defy the signs of Allah and kill the prophets unjustly. That is because they were disobedient and used to commit transgression.)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, they did not kill them with their own hands, nor did they strike them with their swords, but they heard their narrations (أحاديثهم) and spread them (1), so they were seized for them and killed. Thus, it became killing, transgression (اعتداءً), and disobedience (معصية)." It was said: The restriction "unjustly" (بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ), despite the fact that it is so in reality (في نفس الأمر), is meant to indicate that it was not considered rightful according to their own belief (اعتقادهم) either.

(١١٣) لَيْسُوا means the People of the Book ** سَواءً (equal) in their religion. لَيْسُوا سَواءً مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ أُمَّةٌ قائِمَةٌ (113) They are not equal. Among the People of the Book is an upright community upon the truth, and they are those among them who embraced Islam. يَتْلُونَ آياتِ اللهِ آناءَ اللَّيْلِ وَهُمْ يَسْجُدُونَ (reciting the signs of Allah in the watches of the night, and they prostrate themselves) meaning, they recite them during their night vigil (تَهَجُّد). يَتْلُونَ آياتِ اللهِ آناءَ اللَّيْلِ وَهُمْ يَسْجُدُونَ** (reciting the signs of Allah in the watches of the night, and they prostrate themselves.)

(114) يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَيُسارِعُونَ فِي الْخَيْراتِ (114) They believe in Allah and the Last Day, and enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong, and they are quick to [perform] good deeds.

He described them with characteristics that are not found among the Jews, for they are deviating from the truth (الحق), not worshipping during the night, associating partners with Allah (مشركون بالله), heretical concerning His attributes (ملحدون في صفاته), describing the Last Day (اليوم الآخر) contrary to its true description, being hypocritical in enjoining good (الاحتساب), and lagging behind in good deeds (الخيرات). وَأُولئِكَ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ. (114) ... and these are among the righteous.

(115) وَما يَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَلَنْ يُكْفَرُوهُ (And whatever good they do, they will not be denied it.) its reward will not be lost nor diminished. And it was read with the yā’ (بالياء) in both (verbs). That was named denial (كفراناً) just as the full payment of the reward was named gratitude (شكراً). In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Verily, the believer is denied (مكفّر), and that is because his good deed (معروفه) ascends to Allah, so it does not spread among the people. And the disbeliever is thanked (مشكور), and that is because his good deed is for the people, so it spreads among the people and does not ascend to the heaven." وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالْمُتَّقِينَ (and Allah knows best the Godwary.) This is good news (بشارة) for them and an indication that piety (التقوى) is the origin (مبدأ) of goodness (الخير) and righteous action (حسن العمل).

(١١٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوالُهُمْ وَلا أَوْلادُهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً وَأُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ. (116) Indeed, neither their wealth nor their children will avail those who are faithless anything against Allah, and it is they who shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it forever.

footnote (1) The narration (*الحديث*) is said to have spread (*ذاع*) with a spreading (*ذيعاً*) if it became widespread (*انتشر*) and apparent (*ظهر*). And if someone else spread it (*أذاعه غيره*), he divulged it (*أفشاه*) and made it apparent (*أظهره*). From this is the narration (*الحديث*): "Whoever divulges (*أذاع*) our narration (*حديثنا*) against us, Allah will strip Him of faith (*سلبه الله الايمان*)," meaning, whoever divulged it (*أفشاه*) and made it apparent (*أظهره*) to the enemy (*للعدو*) (*مجمع*).

(117) مَثَلُ ما يُنْفِقُونَ فِي هذِهِ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا كَمَثَلِ رِيحٍ فِيها صِرٌّ (The parable of what they spend in this worldly life is like the parable of a wind containing frost) — severe cold (برد شديد) — which struck the harvest of a people who wronged themselves through disbelief (الكفر) and disobedience (المعصية), and destroyed it as a punishment (عقوبة) for them. He likened what they spent, in its wastefulness (ضياعه), to the harvest of disbelievers (كفّار) which was struck by severe cold (برد شديد) resulting from the wrath (سخط) of Allah, so it utterly destroyed it (فاستأصلته), and no benefit (منفعة) remained for them in it, neither in this world (الدنيا) nor in the Hereafter (الآخرة). ** وَما ظَلَمَهُمُ اللهُ (Allah did not wrong them) — meaning, the spenders, by the wastefulness (ضياع) of their expenditures (نفقاتهم). ** وَلكِنْ أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (but they wrong themselves) — because they did not spend it in a manner that would be recognized (يعتدّ بها).

(118) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَّخِذُوا بِطانَةً (118) O you who have faith! Do not take as confidants It means a close intimate (وليجة) (1), who is the one to whom a man entrusts his secrets, relying on him. He is likened to the lining (بطانة) of a garment, just as he is likened to the inner garment (الشعار). مِنْ دُونِكُمْ from outside your own ranks. From other than the Muslims. لا يَأْلُونَكُمْ خَبالاً They will spare no effort to corrupt you; They do not fall short in causing corruption for you. وَدُّوا ما عَنِتُّمْ they desire what distresses you. They wish for your distress (عنتكم), which is severe harm. قَدْ بَدَتِ الْبَغْضاءُ مِنْ أَفْواهِهِمْ Hatred has already shown in their mouths, Meaning, from their speech, because they cannot restrain themselves due to the excessiveness of their hatred. وَما تُخْفِي صُدُورُهُمْ أَكْبَرُ and what their breasts conceal is greater. Than what has appeared. قَدْ بَيَّنَّا لَكُمُ الْآياتِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ. We have certainly made the signs clear for you, should you apply reason.

(١١٩) ها أَنْتُمْ أُولاءِ (119) ‘Ah! You are the ones (You are) the ones who err in befriending the disbelievers (الكفّار). تُحِبُّونَهُمْ وَلا يُحِبُّونَكُمْ وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْكِتابِ who love them, though they do not love you, and you believe in the Book (You believe) in the genus (جنس) of the Book كُلِّهِ in its entirety. (Meaning) your Book, their Book, and others besides them. The meaning is that they do not love you, while you, in fact, believe in their Book as well. So, what is wrong with you that you love them, while they do not believe in your Book? In this is a reproach, indicating that they are inwardly firmer than you regarding your own cause (حقكم). وَإِذا لَقُوكُمْ قالُوا آمَنَّا When they meet you, they say, ‘We believe,’ out of hypocrisy (نفاقاً) and deception (تغريراً). وَإِذا خَلَوْا عَضُّوا عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَنامِلَ مِنَ الْغَيْظِ but when they are alone, they bite their fingertips at you out of rage. out of regret (تأسّفاً) and sorrow (تحسّراً) when they see your harmony (ائتلافكم) and the unity of your word (اجتماع كلمتكم), and they find no way to satisfy their malice (التشفي). قُلْ مُوتُوا بِغَيْظِكُمْ Say, ‘Die in your rage!’ (This is) a supplication against them for the persistence of rage until they die. إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ Indeed Allah knows best what is in the breasts. whether good or evil. Thus, He knows their rage (غيظهم) and their resentment (حنقهم), and the most hidden things they conceal. And this (last sentence) is either part of the preceding statement (القول) or a new independent sentence (مستأنف).

(١٢٠) إِنْ تَمْسَسْكُمْ حَسَنَةٌ تَسُؤْهُمْ وَإِنْ تُصِبْكُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ يَفْرَحُوا بِها وَإِنْ تَصْبِرُوا وَتَتَّقُوا لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْئاً إِنَّ اللهَ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ** (120) If a good fortune befalls you, it grieves them, and if an ill befalls you, they rejoice at it. But if you are patient and wary, their cunning will not harm you in any way. Indeed Allah comprehends what they do.

إِنْ تَمْسَسْكُمْ حَسَنَةٌ (meaning) a blessing (نعمة) such as harmony (ألفة) or victory over the enemies, it grieves them (تَسُؤْهُمْ). وَإِنْ تُصِبْكُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ (meaning) an affliction (محنة) such as separation (فرقة) or an enemy striking one of you, they rejoice at it (يَفْرَحُوا بِها). This is a clarification of the extremity of their enmity. وَإِنْ تَصْبِرُوا regarding their enmity وَتَتَّقُوا befriending them (موالاتهم) and mixing with them (مخالطتهم) ** لا يَضُرُّكُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْئاً because Allah has promised preservation (الحفظ) to the patient and the wary. And it was recited with the kasrah on the ḍād and the jazm on the rāʾ. إِنَّ اللهَ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ**.

footnote (١) The *walījah* (*وليجة*) of a man: his inner circle (*بطانته*), his intimates (*دخلاؤه*), his close associates (*خاصته*), and that which he takes as his reliance (*معتمداً عليه*). The *walījah* (*الوليجة*) is everything you introduce into something else when it is not originally part of it. And a man who is among a people but is not one of them is a *walījah* (*وليجة*) among them (*al-Majma'* - *مجمع*).

(١٢١) وَإِذْ غَدَوْتَ And remember وَإِذْ غَدَوْتَ مِنْ أَهْلِكَ تُبَوِّئُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مَقَاعِدَ لِلْقِتَالِ وَاللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (121) when you went out early from your family to assign the faithful to their positions for battle, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing.

— meaning, you prepared for them positions (مَقاعِدَ) for fighting (لِلْقِتالِ), [which are] stations and places for it — وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ (and Allah is all-hearing) of your sayings عَلِيمٌ (all-knowing) of your intentions.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: The reason for the revelation of this verse is that Quraysh departed from Mecca intending to wage war against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so the Messenger of Allah went out seeking a place for fighting.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Qummi, from him (peace be upon him), He said: The reason for the Battle of Uhud (غزوة أُحد) was that when Quraysh returned from Badr to Mecca, they had suffered what they suffered in terms of killing and captivity, for seventy of them were killed and seventy were taken captive. Abu Sufyan said: "O assembly of Quraysh, do not let your women weep over your slain, for when a tear flows, it removes the grief and the enmity toward Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." So, when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم went out to battle on the Day of Uhud, they permitted their women to weep and wail. They departed from Mecca with three thousand horsemen and two thousand foot soldiers, and they brought the women out with them. When this reached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He gathered His companions and urged them toward Jihad. Abdullah ibn Ubayy said: "O Messenger of Allah, do not leave Medina until we fight in its alleys, so that the weak man, the woman, the slave, and the bondswoman may fight at the mouths of the streets (السكك) (1) and on the rooftops. No people have ever sought us out while we were in our fortresses and homes and prevailed over us, and we have never gone out against an enemy except that they gained victory over us." Then Sa'd ibn Mu'adh and others from the Aws stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah, no one among the Arabs dared to covet us while we were polytheists worshipping idols, so how can they prevail over us while You are among us? No, [we must go out] until we march out to them and fight them. Whoever among us is killed will be a martyr (شهيد), and whoever among us survives will be a striver (مجاهد) in the way of Allah." So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم accepted his opinion and went out with a group of His companions, assigning positions for the fighting, just as the Glorious One said: وَإِذْ غَدَوْتَ مِنْ أَهْلِكَ (when you went out early from your family) and the rest of the verse.

Abdullah ibn Ubayy and a group of the Khazraj who followed his opinion stayed behind. Quraysh arrived at Uhud, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had arranged His companions (1), who numbered seven hundred men. He placed Abdullah ibn Jubayr with fifty archers at the mouth of the mountain pass (الشعب) (2), fearing that their ambush might come from that place. He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Abdullah ibn Jubayr and his companions: "If you see that we have routed them until we have driven them into Mecca, do not abandon this place. And if you see that they have routed us until they have driven us into Medina, do not abandon [it], and hold fast to your positions." Abu Sufyan placed Khalid ibn al-Walid in ambush with two hundred horsemen and told him: "If you see that we have become intermingled, then charge them from this mountain pass (الشعب) so that you will be behind them." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم arranged His companions and handed the banner (الراية) to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). The Ansar charged the polytheists of Quraysh, and they suffered a terrible defeat. The companions of the Messenger of Allah fell upon their main body (سوادهم). Khalid ibn al-Walid, with two hundred horsemen, descended (2) upon Abdullah ibn Jubayr, but they met them with arrows, so he retreated. The companions of Abdullah ibn Jubayr looked at the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم plundering the main body of the people, and they said to Abdullah ibn Jubayr: "Our companions have gained spoils, and we remain without spoils." Abdullah said to them: "Fear Allah! The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded us not to abandon [this place]." But they did not accept his words and began to slip away, man by man, until they vacated their positions. Abdullah ibn Jubayr remained with twelve men. The banner of Quraysh was with Talhah ibn Abi Talhah al-Abdari from Banu Abd al-Dar, and Ali (peace be upon him) killed him. Abu Sa'id ibn Abi Talhah took the banner, and Ali killed him. The banner fell, and Musafih ibn Talhah took it, and Ali killed him, until nine men from Banu Abd al-Dar were killed, and their standard (لواؤهم) passed to a black slave of theirs called Sawwab. Ali reached him and cut off his hand. The slave took the banner with his left hand, so Ali struck his left hand and cut it off. The slave then embraced (1) it to his chest with his two stumps (بالجذماوين). Then he turned to Abu Sufyan and said: "Have I made excuses (هل أعذرت) regarding Banu Abd al-Dar?" Ali (peace be upon him) struck him on the head and killed him. The standard fell, and Amrah bint Alqamah al-Kinaniyyah took it and raised it. Khalid ibn al-Walid descended (2) upon Abdullah ibn Jubayr, whose companions had fled, and he remained with a small group, so Khalid killed them at the mouth of the mountain pass (الشعب). Then he came upon the Muslims from their rear. Quraysh, in their defeat, saw the banner raised, so they rallied around it. The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم suffered a great defeat and began to climb the mountains and flee in every direction. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw the defeat, He uncovered the helmet (البيضة) from His head and said: "To me! I am the Messenger of Allah! To me! Where are you fleeing from Allah and from His Messenger?" It is said that Hind bint Utbah was in the middle of the army, and whenever a man from Quraysh fled, she handed him a kohl stick (ميل) and a kohl container (مكحلة) and said: "You are but a woman, so apply kohl with this." Hamzah ibn Abd al-Muttalib used to charge the people, and when they saw him, they fled, and no one stood firm against him. Hind had given a pledge to Wahshi: "If you kill Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, or Ali, or Hamzah (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), I will give you such and such." Wahshi was an Abyssinian slave belonging to Jubayr ibn Mut'im. Wahshi said: "As for Muhammad, I cannot reach him. As for Ali, I saw him cautious and frequently looking around, so there is no hope of getting him." So, he lay in wait for Hamzah. He said: "I saw him cutting (يهذ) (3) people down fiercely. He passed by me and stepped on the bank (جرف) (4) of a river and fell. I took my spear (حربة), shook (5) it, and threw it at him. It struck his flank and came out of his groin (الثنية) (6). He fell. I came to him, slit his belly, took his liver, and brought it to Hind. I said: 'This is the liver of Hamzah.' She took it in her mouth and chewed (7) it, but Allah made it in her mouth like the al-Da'idah (الداعضة), which is the bone of the kneecap. So, she spat it out and threw it away." The Messenger of Allah said: "Allah sent an angel who carried it and returned it to its place." He said: Then she came to him and cut off his genitals (مذاكيره), cut off his ears, and cut off his hand and his leg. No one remained with the Messenger of Allah except Abu Dujanah Simak ibn Kharashah and Ali. Whenever a group charged the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, Ali met them and repelled them from Him until his sword broke. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم gave him His sword, Dhul-Fiqar. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم withdrew (1) to a side of Uhud and stood there, and the fighting was from one direction. Ali continued to fight them until he sustained seventy wounds on his face, head, hands, belly, and legs. He said: Then Gabriel said: "This is indeed true sacrifice (المواساة), O Muhammad!" He [the Prophet] said to him: "He is from Me, and I am from him." Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: The Messenger of Allah looked at Gabriel between the heaven and the earth on a throne of gold, saying: "There is no sword except Dhul-Fiqar, and no youth (فتى) except Ali."

It is narrated: The reason for their defeat was the call of Iblis among them that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had been killed, while the Prophet was in the midst of the crowd, and they could not see Him.

(1) السكك: The alleys.

(1) عبأ (past tense of يُعَبِّئُ): He prepared the provisions and the matter, like (مَنَعَ), meaning He arranged it; and the army, meaning He equipped it, like (عَبَّأْتُهُ) (تَعْبِئَةً) and (تَعْبِيئاً) in both cases (ق).

(2) الشعب: The path in the mountain, the watercourse in the bottom of the land, and what opens up between two mountains (ق).

(1) عانقت the woman and اعتنقتها: It is the embracing and clinging. الجذماء is the feminine of الأجذم, which is the severed thing, and what is described here is the hand. It means the slave pressed the banner to his chest with his two severed hands.

(2) حَطَطْتُ the man and other things (حَطّاً) from the category of (قَتَلَ): I brought him down from above to below. From this is فَانْحَطَّ the man while he was standing in his prayer (مجمع).

(3) الهذ: Swiftness in cutting (م).

(4) الجرف of everything: Its edge (ق).

(5) هَزَزْتُ the thing (هَزّاً) so it اهْتَزَّ: Meaning I moved it, so it moved (م).

(6) الثنية (with ḍamma): The groin (ق).

(7) اللوك: The turning of a thing in the mouth. لاكه يلوكه لوكاً and لَكَتُّ the thing in my mouth, meaning I chewed it (م).

(١٢٢) إِذْ هَمَّتْ طائِفَتانِ مِنْكُمْ (122) When two groups among you resolved Al-Qummi said: He means 'Abd Allah ibn Ubayy and his companions and his people. And in al-Majma', narrated from them (peace be upon them both): They are Banu Salimah and Banu Harithah, two clans (حيان) from the Ansar. And it was said: They are Banu Salimah from the Khazraj and Banu al-Harithah from the Aws, and they were the two wings of the army. ** أَنْ تَفْشَلا to lose heart That they become cowardly and weak. وَاللهُ وَلِيُّهُما and Allah was their guardian Their Helper. وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ** and in Allah let the faithful put their trust. So let them rely upon Him for sufficiency.

(١٢٣) وَلَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللهُ بِبَدْرٍ (123) And certainly Allah helped you at Badr, (This is) a reminder of some of what reliance (upon Him) benefited them. And Badr is a water source between Mecca and Medina that belonged to a man named Badr, so it was named after him. وَأَنْتُمْ أَذِلَّةٌ while you were abased.

Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "They were not abased while the Messenger of Allah was among them. Rather, it was revealed as: وَأَنْتُمْ ضُعَفَاءُ (while you were weak)."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): Abu Basir recited the Ayah in his presence, and he (the Imam) said: "Stop! It was not revealed by Allah like this. Rather, it was revealed as: وَأَنْتُمْ قَلِيلٌ (while you were few)."

And in a narration: "Allah never abased His Messenger at all. Rather, it was revealed as: وَأَنْتُمْ قَلِيلٌ (while you were few)."

And in more than one of the infallible (المعصومية) narrations: Their number was three hundred and thirteen. فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ in steadfastness لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ what He bestowed upon you.

(So be wary of Allah that you may be grateful.)

(١٢٤) إِذْ تَقُولُ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَلَنْ يَكْفِيَكُمْ أَنْ يُمِدَّكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِثَلاثَةِ آلافٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ مُنْزَلِينَ (124) When you said to the faithful, 'Is it not enough for you that your Lord should aid you with three thousand angels sent down?' And it was recited with the zāy (الزاي) intensified (مشدّدة). (١٢٥) بَلى إِنْ تَصْبِرُوا وَتَتَّقُوا وَيَأْتُوكُمْ meaning, the polytheists (المشركون) مِنْ فَوْرِهِمْ هذا from this very hour (من ساعتهم هذه) يُمْدِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِخَمْسَةِ آلافٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ at the moment of their coming, without delay (بلا تراخ) مُسَوِّمِينَ marked (معلّمين), derived from al-taswīm (التسويم), meaning the manifestation of the sign (سيماء) of a thing. And it was recited with the wāw (الواو) having a kasrah (بكسر الواو). (125) Yes, if you are patient and Godwary, and they come at you suddenly, your Lord will aid you with five thousand angels marked [for battle].

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The angels wore white, flowing turbans (العمائم البيض المرسلة) on the Day of Badr.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The angels who aided Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the Day of Badr have not ascended since, nor will they ascend until they aid the Master of this Affair (صاحب هذا الأمر), and they are five thousand.

(١٢٦) وَما جَعَلَهُ اللهُ (126) And Allah did not appoint it And He did not appoint your reinforcement by the angels إِلَّا بُشْرى لَكُمْ except as a good news for you وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُكُمْ بِهِ and so that your hearts may be at rest thereby. and so that they may be tranquil through it, away from fear. وَمَا النَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ And victory is not except from Allah, not from preparation (العُدة) or equipment (العِدة). And in this is an alert (تنبيه) that there is no need for reinforcement (مدد), but rather He reinforced them and promised them as a glad tiding for them and to strengthen their hearts, because the general populace (العامّة) looks more towards the means (الأسباب), and as an encouragement (حثاً) that they should not care about those who lagged behind them. الْعَزِيزِ the All-mighty, Who is not overcome in His decrees (أقضيته). الْحَكِيمِ the All-wise. Who grants victory and abandons according to the requirement of wisdom (الحكمة) and benefit (المصلحة).

(١٢٧) لِيَقْطَعَ طَرَفاً مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (127) that He may cut off a flank of the faithless He granted you victory so that a portion of them might be diminished by the killing of some and the capture of others. And this is what occurred on the Day of Badr: the killing of seventy and the capture of seventy of their chiefs (صناديدهم), as the indication to it has passed, and the complete story will come in Sūrat al-Anfāl, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله). أَوْ يَكْبِتَهُمْ or abase them, and al-kabt (الكبت) is intense rage (شدة غيض) or a weakness (وهن) that falls upon the heart, or it is for variation (للتنويع). فَيَنْقَلِبُوا خائِبِينَ so they are defeated, having their hopes cut off (منقطعي الآمال).

(١٢٨) لَيْسَ لَكَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ (128) You have no say in the matter, is an interjection. أَوْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ or He may relent toward them, if they embrace Islam. أَوْ يُعَذِّبَهُمْ or He may punish them, if they persist (in disbelief). فَإِنَّهُمْ ظالِمُونَ for they are wrongdoers. they have certainly deserved the punishment due to their wrongdoing (ظلم).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He recited أَن تَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ تُعَذِّبَهُمْ (that You may relent toward them or You may punish them), using the tā’ (التاء) in both (verbs).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): When لَيْسَ لَكَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ (You have no say in the matter) was recited in His presence, He said: "Yes, by Allah, He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) certainly has a say in the matter, and a say, and a say! It is not as you have understood. But I will inform you: When Allah the Almighty informed His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He should proclaim the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali, the Prophet reflected upon the enmity of his people toward him regarding what Allah had favored him with over them in all his characteristics, and their envy of him for it. He felt constrained by this. So, Allah informed him that He (the Prophet) had no say in this matter (هذا الأمر); rather, the matter concerning it belongs to Allah: that He should make Ali (peace be upon him) His successor (وصيه) and the holder of authority (ولي الأمر) after Him. This is what Allah meant. And how could He (the Prophet) have no say in the matter when Allah has delegated to Him (the authority) to make what He declares lawful (أحل) lawful (حلال), and what He declares unlawful (حرم) unlawful (حرام), as stated in His saying: ما آتاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَما نَهاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا (What the Messenger gives you, take it, and what he forbids you, refrain from it)?"

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was eager that Ali (peace be upon him) should be in authority over the people after Him. But what Allah willed was contrary to what He (the Prophet) desired. So, He (Allah) said to Him: لَيْسَ لَكَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ (You have no say in the matter), O Muhammad, concerning Ali. The matter concerning Ali and others belongs to Me. Did I not reveal to you, O Muhammad, in what I revealed of My Book to you: الم أَحَسِبَ النَّاسُ أَنْ يُتْرَكُوا أَنْ يَقُولُوا آمَنَّا وَهُمْ لا يُفْتَنُونَ (Alif, Lām, Mīm. Do the people suppose that they will be let off with just saying, 'We believe,' and will not be tested?)—the āyāt? He (the Imam) said: So, the Messenger of Allah delegated the matter to Him (Allah).

I say: The meaning of His saying that Ali should be in authority over the people after Him is that He should also be His successor (خليفة) over them outwardly (في الظاهر), without anyone repelling Him.

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He recited لَيْسَ لَكَ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ أَن يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ يُعَذِّبَهُمْ (You have no say in the matter, that He may relent toward them or He may punish them).

And the general public (العامّة) narrated: That Utbah ibn Abi Waqqas wounded Him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the Day of Uhud and broke His incisor tooth (رباعيته). He began wiping the blood from His face, saying: "How can a people prosper who have stained the face of their Prophet with blood?" So, (the āyah) was revealed, informing Him that many of them would eventually embrace faith.

(١٢٩) وَلِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (129) To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. (He owns them) as creation and dominion, so the entire command belongs to Him. ** يَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ**. He forgives whomever He wishes and punishes whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

In al-Majma', it is said that Allah only left the matter of punishment (التعذيب) and forgiveness (المغفرة) ambiguous so that the accountable person (المكلف) stands between fear (الخوف) and hope (الرجاء). And the saying of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) points to this: "If the hope (رجاء) and fear (خوف) of the believer were weighed, they would be equal."

(١٣٠) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَأْكُلُوا الرِّبَوا أَضْعافاً مُضاعَفَةً ** (130) O you who have faith! Do not consume usury, multiplying it manifold. It was said that the man among them would practice usury until a term, then increase it until another, until he consumed the debtor's wealth with a small amount (of the original loan).
