And it was read (مُضَعَّفَة) with the doubling (تشديد) of the letter ع (al-ʿayn). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (And be wary of Allah) regarding what you have been forbidden. ** لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ ** that you may be felicitous. (This indicates) hope, so it is not a definitive ruling (حكم).

(١٣١) وَاتَّقُوا النَّارَ الَّتِي أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكافِرِينَ (131) And beware of the Fire which has been prepared for the faithless, by avoiding the likeness of their deeds. (١٣٢) وَأَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَالرَّسُولَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (132) And obey Allah and the Apostle so that you may be granted His mercy. by obeying both of them. The use of لعلّ (perhaps) and عسى (it may be) in such instances is evidence of the difficulty (عزة) of attaining it. (١٣٣) وَسارِعُوا (133) And hasten And [some] read سارعوا (hasten) without the waw (و) [at the beginning], and [it means] rush (بادروا) إِلى مَغْفِرَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ toward the causes of forgiveness. In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): [Hasten] toward performing the obligatory duties (الفرائض) وَجَنَّةٍ عَرْضُهَا السَّماواتُ وَالْأَرْضُ. and toward a paradise whose breadth is the heavens and the earth. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It is] when they are placed like this—and He spread His hands, one with the other. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked, "If Paradise's breadth is the heavens and the earth, then where is the Fire?" He replied: "Glory be to Allah (سبحان الله)! When the day comes, where is the night?" The author of al-Majma' said: This is a counter-argument (معارضة) that dismisses the question, because the One Who is able to take the night wherever He wills is able to create the Fire wherever He wills. I say : And the secret (السر) in this is that one of the two abodes (الدارين) for every human being only becomes the place of the other as a substitute for it, just as in the case of night and day. prepared for the Godwary. (أُعِدَّتْ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ). In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful صلوات الله عليه: "For you will not attain it except through piety (التقوى)."

(١٣٤) الَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ فِي السَّرَّاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ (those who spend in ease and hardship) in the two states of ease (الرخاء) and hardship (الشدّة). This means they spend in all their circumstances, whatever is made easy for them, whether little or much. وَالْكاظِمِينَ الْغَيْظَ (and those who restrain their anger) those who hold it back (الممسكين عليه), refraining from executing it (الكافين عن إمضائه).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever restrains anger, even though he was able to execute it, Allah will fill his heart on the Day of Resurrection with contentment (رضاً)." وَالْعافِينَ عَنِ النَّاسِ (and those who excuse [the faults of] the people) regarding it (the anger/fault).

Narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Hold fast to forgiveness (العفو), for forgiveness does not increase a servant except in honor (عزاً). So forgive one another, and Allah will grant you honor." ** وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ**. (and Allah loves the virtuous.)

In al-Majma', it is narrated: A slave-girl belonging to Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon him) began pouring water over him so that he might prepare for prayer. The ewer (الإبريق) fell from her hand and injured him. He raised his head toward her. The slave-girl said to him: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: ** وَالْكاظِمِينَ الْغَيْظَ (and those who restrain their anger)." He said to her: "I have restrained my anger." She said: ** وَالْعافِينَ عَنِ النَّاسِ (and those who excuse [the faults of] the people). He said: "May Allah forgive you." She said: وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ (and Allah loves the virtuous). He said: "Go, for you are free for the sake of Allah."

وَالَّذِينَ إِذا فَعَلُوا فاحِشَةً أَوْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ ذَكَرُوا اللَّهَ فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا لِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَمَن يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَلَمْ يُصِرُّوا عَلَىٰ مَا فَعَلُوا وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (135) (and those who, when they commit an indecency or wrong themselves, remember Allah and plead for forgiveness for their sins—and who forgives sins except Allah?—and do not persist in what they have done, while they know)

وَالَّذِينَ إِذا فَعَلُوا فاحِشَةً (and those who, when they commit an indecency) meaning, an evil deed (سيئة) reaching the extreme in ugliness, like fornication (الزنا). ** أَوْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (or wrong themselves) by committing a sin (ذنباً) greater than fornication (الزنا). ذَكَرُوا اللهَ (remember Allah) meaning, they recalled His threat (وعيده) or His tremendous right (حقه العظيم). فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا لِذُنُوبِهِمْ (and plead for forgiveness for their sins) through remorse (الندم) and repentance (التوبة). وَمَنْ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا اللهُ (and who forgives sins except Allah?) This is an interrogative (استفهام) meaning negation (النفي), inserted between the coordinated clauses (بين المعطوفين). The intent behind it is to describe Him with the vastness of mercy (سعة الرحمة) and the universality of forgiveness (عموم المغفرة), and to encourage seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار), and to promise the acceptance of repentance (قبول التوبة). ** وَلَمْ يُصِرُّوا عَلى ما فَعَلُوا (and do not persist in what they have done) meaning, they did not remain (لم يقيموا) upon their sins (ذنوبهم) without seeking forgiveness (غير مستغفرين).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "Persistence (الإِصرار) is that one commits a sin (الذنب) and does not seek Allah's forgiveness (يستغفر الله) nor does he speak to himself about repentance (توبة). That is persistence (الإِصرار)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "By Allah, no servant escapes a sin (ذنب) except through persistence (إلا بإصرار), and no servant escapes a sin (ذنب) except through confession (إلّا بالإِقرار)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "There is no minor sin (صغيرة) with persistence (الإصرار), and no major sin (كبيرة) with seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار)." And it was narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "He who seeks forgiveness (استغفر) has not persisted (ما أصر), even if He returns [to the sin] seventy times in a day." وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (while they know) meaning, they did not persist (لم يصروا) in their ugly deed (قبيح فِعلهم) while being aware (عالمين) of it.

أُولئِكَ جَزاؤُهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَجَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعامِلِينَ (136) (The requital of such is forgiveness from their Lord, and gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever. How excellent is the reward of the workers!) Forgiveness and gardens.

In al-Majalis, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When this verse was revealed, Iblis ascended a mountain and screamed at the top of his voice, calling his عفاريت (demons). They gathered before him and said: "O our master, why have you called us?" He said: "This verse has been revealed, so who is responsible for it?" A عفريت (1) from the devils stood up and said: "I am responsible for it, by doing such and such." Iblis said: "You are not responsible for it." Another stood up and said the same thing. Iblis said: "You are not responsible for it." Then الوسواس الخناس (the Whispering Sneaker) said: "I am responsible for it." Iblis asked: "With what?" He replied: "I promise them and give them false hopes until they commit the sin, and when they commit the sin, I make them forget الاستغفار (seeking forgiveness)." Iblis said: "You are responsible for it," and entrusted him with it until the Day of Resurrection.

And narrated from Abd al-Rahman ibn Ghanm al-Dawsi (2), he said: Mu'adh entered and said: "O Messenger of Allah, there is a young man at the door, fresh of body, pure of complexion, handsome of appearance, weeping over his youth like a bereaved mother over her child, wishing to enter upon you." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Let the young man enter upon me, O Mu'adh." So he brought him in. He greeted him with السّلام (peace), and He returned it. Then He said: "What makes you weep, O young man?" He said: "How can I not weep when I have committed sins such that if Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, were to seize me for just some of them, He would admit me to the Fire of Hell, and I do not see but that He will seize me for them and never forgive me." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Have you associated anything with Allah?" He said: "I seek refuge with Allah from associating anything with my Lord." He said: "Have you killed a soul that Allah has forbidden?" He said: "No." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Allah will forgive your sins, even if they were like the towering mountains (الجبال الرواسي)." The young man said: "But they are greater than the towering mountains." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Allah will forgive your sins, even if they were like the seven earths, their seas, their sands, their trees, and the creatures within them." The young man said: "But they are greater than the seven earths, their seas, their sands, their trees, and the creatures within them." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Allah will forgive your sins, even if your sins were like the heavens and their stars, and like the Throne (العرش) and the Footstool (الكرسيّ)." He said: "But they are greater than that." The narrator said: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked as if He were angry, then He said: "Woe to you, O young man! Are your sins greater, or is your Lord?" The young man rushed out, saying: "Glory be to my Lord! Nothing is greater than my Lord! My Lord is greater, O Prophet of Allah, than every great thing!" The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Does anyone forgive the great sin except the Great Lord?" The young man said: "No, by Allah, O Messenger of Allah." Then the young man fell silent. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "Woe to you, O young man! Will you not inform me of a single sin of yours?" He said: "Yes, I will inform you. I used to dig up graves for seven years, taking out the dead and stripping the shrouds. Then a young woman, one of the daughters of the أنصار (Helpers), died. When she was carried to her grave, buried, and her family departed from her, and night fell upon them, I came to her grave, dug it up, then took her out and stripped off the shrouds that were upon her, leaving her naked on the edge of her grave. I turned to leave, but Satan came to me and began to beautify her for me, saying: 'Do you not see her belly and her whiteness? Do you not see her thighs (وركيها)?' (1) He did not cease saying this to me until I returned to her, and I could not control myself until I had intercourse with her and left her in her place. Suddenly, I heard a voice from behind me saying: 'O young man, woe to you from the Judge (ديان) on the Day of Judgment, the Day He will make me and you stand, just as you left me naked among the armies of the dead, removed me from my grave, stripped me of my shrouds, and left me to stand naked (جنبة) for my reckoning, due to your youth, from the Fire! I do not think I will ever smell the scent of Paradise, O Messenger of Allah. What do you advise for me?" The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Get away from me, O wicked one (فاسق)! I fear that I might be burned by your fire! How close you are to the Fire!" Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم continued to speak and point to him until he moved far away from before Him and left. He went to Medina, took provisions from it, then went to one of its mountains and devoted himself to worship there. He wore a coarse garment (مسحاً) (1) and tied both his hands to his neck, and cried out: "O Lord! This is Your servant بهلول (2), bound before You! O Lord! You are the One Who knows me, and the transgression You know has occurred from me. My Master, O Lord! I have become one of the remorseful, and I came to Your Prophet repentant, but He drove me away and increased my fear. So I ask You by Your Name, Your Majesty, and the greatness of Your Sovereignty, that You do not disappoint my hope, my Master, nor invalidate my supplication, nor make me despair of Your mercy." He continued saying this for forty days and nights, and the beasts of prey and the wild animals wept for his weeping. When forty days and nights were completed for him, he raised his hands to the sky and said: "O Allah, what have You done regarding my need? If You have answered my supplication and forgiven my sin, then reveal it to Your Prophet. But if You have not answered my supplication and have not forgiven my sin, and You intend to punish me, then hasten a fire to burn me or a punishment in this world to destroy me, and save me from the disgrace of the Day of Resurrection." Then Allah the Almighty revealed to His Prophet:

وَالَّذِينَ إِذا فَعَلُوا فاحِشَةً (And those who, when they commit an indecency) meaning fornication (الزنا)

أَوْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (or wrong themselves) meaning by committing a sin greater than fornication (الزنا), which is digging up graves and taking the shrouds

ذَكَرُوا اللهَ فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا لِذُنُوبِهِمْ (remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins) He says: they feared Allah and hastened repentance

وَمَنْ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا اللهُ (and who forgives sins except Allah?) Allah the Almighty says: "My servant came to you, O Muhammad, repentant, but you drove him away. Where will he go, and to whom will he turn, and whom will he ask to forgive his sin other than Me?" Then the Almighty said:

وَلَمْ يُصِرُّوا عَلى ما فَعَلُوا وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (and do not persist in what they have done, while they know.) He says: they did not persist in fornication (الزنا), digging up graves, and taking the shrouds

أُولئِكَ جَزاؤُهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَجَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعامِلِينَ (The requital of such is forgiveness from their Lord, and gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever. How excellent is the reward of the workers!) When this verse was revealed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He went out reciting it and smiling. He said to His companions: "Who can guide me to this repentant young man?" Mu'adh said: "O Messenger of Allah, we have been informed that he is in such and such a place." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم went with His companions until they reached that mountain. They ascended it, searching for the young man. They found the young man standing between two rocks, his hands bound to his neck, his face blackened, and the lashes of his eyes fallen out from weeping. He was saying: "My Master, You have perfected my creation and perfected my form. Would that I knew what You intend for me! Will You burn me in the Fire, or will You settle me near You? O Allah, You have multiplied Your kindness toward me and bestowed favors upon me. Would that I knew what my final outcome will be! Will You escort me (1) to Paradise, or will You drive me to the Fire? O Allah, my sin is greater than the heavens and the earth, and greater than Your vast Footstool (كرسيك الواسع) and Your Great Throne (عرشك العظيم). Would that I knew whether You will forgive my sin or disgrace me with it on the Day of Resurrection." He continued saying things like this, weeping and throwing dust upon his head. The beasts of prey had surrounded him, and the birds were lined up above him, weeping for his weeping. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم approached him, released his hands from his neck, shook the dust from his head, and said: "O بهلول! Rejoice, O بهلول! For you are the freed one of Allah from the Fire." Then He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to His companions: "Thus should you remedy sins, just as بهلول remedied them." Then He recited to him what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had revealed concerning him and gave him the glad tidings of Paradise.

(١٣٧) قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ سُنَنٌ (137) There have been [similar] ways [of dealing with nations] before you. [It means] events which Allah the Almighty established concerning the denying nations. فَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانْظُروا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ (So travel over the land and observe how was the fate of the deniers.) so that you may take heed by what you see of the traces of their destruction.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, regarding the Almighty's saying: ** فَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانْظُروا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ** (So travel over the land and observe how was the fate of the deniers.) He (peace be upon him) said: He meant by that: Look into the Quran and know how was the fate of those who were before you and what He informed you about.

(١٣٨) هذا بَيانٌ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدىً وَمَوْعِظَةٌ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (١٣٨) (138) This is a declaration for mankind, and a guidance and advice for the Godwary. هذا أي القرآن بَيانٌ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدىً وَمَوْعِظَةٌ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ خاصّة.

This, meaning the Quran, is a declaration for mankind, and a guidance and advice for the Godwary (لِلْمُتَّقِينَ) specifically (خاصّة).

(١٣٩) وَلا تَهِنُوا وَلا تَحْزَنُوا وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (١٣٩) (139) Do not falter or grieve; you shall be the superior ones if you are faithful. وَلا تَهِنُوا تضعفوا عن الجهاد بما أصابكم يوم أُحد وَلا تَحْزَنُوا على من قتل منكم تسلية لهم عما أصابهم وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ وحالكم أنكم أعلى منهم شأناً فإنكم على الحق وقتالكم لله وقتلاكم في الجنة وانهم على الباطل وقتالهم للشيطان وقتلاهم في النار وانكم أصبتم منهم يوم بدر أكثر ممّا أصابوا منكم ليوم أو أنكم منصورون في العاقبة غالبون إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ان صح إيمانكم.

Do not falter, meaning: do not weaken (تضعفوا) from Jihad (الجهاد) due to what afflicted you on the Day of Uhud, or grieve over those among you who were killed, as a consolation (تسلية) for them regarding what afflicted them. you shall be the superior ones, and your state is that you are higher in status (شأناً) than them, for you are upon the truth, and your fighting is for Allah, and your slain are in Paradise (الجنة). While they are upon falsehood (الباطل), and their fighting is for Satan (الشيطان), and their slain are in the Fire (النار). And you afflicted them on the Day of Badr more than they afflicted you on that day, or (it means) that you shall be victorious (منصورون) in the end (العاقبة), prevailing (غالبون). if you are faithful, if your faith (إيمانكم) is sound (صح).

إِنْ يَمْسَسْكُمْ قَرْحٌ (140) If a wound has afflicted you, (قَرْحٌ) is read with fath (الفتح) and ḍamm (الضم) as two linguistic variants (لغتان). It is said that with fath (بالفتح) it means the wounds (الجراح), and with ḍamm (بالضم) it means their pain (ألمها). He read with both of them wherever it occurred.

فَقَدْ مَسَّ الْقَوْمَ قَرْحٌ مِثْلُهُ a similar wound has certainly afflicted the people [of the enemy]. Meaning: if they have afflicted you, then you have certainly afflicted them.

وَتِلْكَ الْأَيَّامُ And these days (meaning: the times of victory and dominance)

نُداوِلُها بَيْنَ النَّاسِ We alternate them among mankind, We turn them among them, granting dominance (نديل) to these sometimes and to those other times, just as it was said: "A day against us and a day for us, a day we are saddened and a day we are gladdened."

وَلِيَعْلَمَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا and so that Allah may ascertain those who have faith, We alternate them so that such and such occurs, and among the benefits (المصالح) is that those who are firm in faith (الثابتون على الإيمان) are distinguished from those who are on the edge (على حرف). And Allah knows that when people witness it, just as He knows it from before and from after.

وَيَتَّخِذَ مِنْكُمْ شُهَداءَ and take martyrs from among you, and honor some people among you with martyrdom (الشهادة).

وَاللهُ لا يُحِبُّ الظَّالِمِينَ and Allah does not love the wrongdoers. This is an interjection (اعتراض) containing a warning that He does not truly grant them victory, but rather grants them dominance sometimes as a gradual enticement (استدراجاً لهم) for them and a trial (ابتلاء) for the believers.

footnote (١) **The Almighty's saying:** **فَأَقْبَلُوا إِلَيْهِ يَزِفُّونَ** meaning: they hastened (*يسرعون*). It is said: "The man came *يَزِفُّ*" (*yaziff*) from the category (*باب*) of *ḍaraba* (*ضرب*). The *زفيف* (*zafīf*) of the ostrich is the beginning of its running and the end of its walking. "M."

(١٤١) وَلِيُمَحِّصَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (141) and that Allah may purge the faithful so that He may purify them and cleanse them of sins, if the dominance (الدولة) is for them. ** وَيَمْحَقَ الْكافِرِينَ and annihilate the faithless. and destroy them, if [the dominance] is against them. And al-mahq (المحق) is the gradual decrease of a thing, little by little. (١٤٢) أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ (142) Do you suppose Rather, did you suppose? Meaning: Do not suppose. ** أَنْ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ اللهُ الَّذِينَ جاهَدُوا مِنْكُمْ وَيَعْلَمَ الصَّابِرِينَ that you will enter paradise before Allah has ascertained those of you who wage jihad and ascertained the steadfast? while those who wage jihad (يجاهد) among you have not yet waged jihad, and those who are patient have not yet shown patience. Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse. He said: "Indeed, Allah is more knowledgeable of what He is creating before He creates it, and they were particles (ذر), and He knew who would wage jihad (يجاهد) and who would not wage jihad, just as He causes His creation to die before He causes them to die, and He has not shown them their death while they are alive."

(١٤٣) وَلَقَدْ كُنْتُمْ تَمَنَّوْنَ الْمَوْتَ ** (143) Certainly you used to long for death meaning martyrdom (بالشهادة) ** مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَلْقَوْهُ ** before you met it, meaning before you witnessed it (تشاهدوه) and knew its severity (شدته) ** فَقَدْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ وَأَنْتُمْ تَنْظُرُونَ Now you have certainly seen it, while you looked on witnessing it (معاينين له) when those of your brothers who were killed were slain near you (دونكم).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: When Allah Almighty informed the believers of what He had done for their martyrs (شهدائهم) on the Day of Badr concerning their stations (منازلهم) in Paradise, they desired that. So they said: "O Allah, show us a battle (قتالاً) in which we may be martyred (نستشهد فيه)." Then Allah showed them that on the Day of Uhud, but they did not stand firm (يثبتوا) except those among them whom Allah willed. That is His saying: وَلَقَدْ كُنْتُمْ تَمَنَّوْنَ الْمَوْتَ (Certainly you used to long for death) [and the rest of] the verse.

(١٤٤) وَما مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ (144) And Muhammad is but an apostle; [other] apostles have passed before him. So he will pass away, just as they passed away, by death or by being killed. أَفَإِنْ ماتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انْقَلَبْتُمْ عَلى أَعْقابِكُمْ Will you turn back on your heels if he dies or is killed? This is a denunciation of their apostasy (ارتدادهم) and their turning back on their heels from the religion due to his passing away by death or being killed, despite their knowledge that the apostles before him had passed away and their religion remained, adhered to by their followers.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about someone who was killed: "Did he die?" He said: "No. Death (الموت) is death, and killing (القتل) is killing." It was said: "No one is killed except that he has already died." He said: "The saying of Allah is truer than your saying, and He differentiated between the two in the Quran. He said: أَفَإِنْ ماتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ (Will he die or be killed?) And He said: لَئِنْ مُتُّمْ أَوْ قُتِلْتُمْ لَإِلَى اللهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (If you die or are killed, you will surely be mustered toward Allah.) It is not as you said. Death is death, and killing is killing." It was said: "But Allah says: كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ (Every soul shall taste death.)" He said: "Whoever is killed has not tasted death." Then he said: "He must return until he tastes death." Another narration concerning this meaning will come at the end of this Surah, if Allah wills.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When the people fled from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the Day of Uhud, he turned towards them, saying: "I am Muhammad! I am the Messenger of Allah! I have not been killed, nor have I died!" So, so-and-so and so-and-so turned to look at him and said: "Now he is mocking us too, even though we have been defeated!" Only Ali (peace be upon him) and Simak ibn Kharashah Abu Dujanah (may Allah have mercy on him) remained with him. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called him and said: "O Abu Dujanah, depart, and you are absolved (في حل) of your oath of allegiance (بيعتك). As for Ali (peace be upon him), he is me, and I am him." So, [Abu Dujanah] turned, sat before the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and wept. He said: "No, by Allah!" He raised his head to the sky and said: "No, by Allah! I will not absolve myself of my oath of allegiance! I have pledged allegiance to you, so to whom should I depart, O Messenger of Allah? To a wife who will die, or a child who will die, or a house that will be ruined, or wealth that will perish, while the appointed time (أجل) has drawn near?" The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم felt compassion for him. He continued fighting until he was severely wounded. He was fighting in one direction, and Ali (peace be upon him) was fighting in another. When he fell, Ali (peace be upon him) carried him and brought him to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, placing him beside Him. He said: "O Messenger of Allah, have I fulfilled my oath of allegiance?" He said: "Yes." And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم spoke well of him. The people were attacking the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from the right flank (الميمنة), and Ali would repel them. When he repelled them, the left flank (الميسرة) would approach the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. This continued until his sword broke into three pieces. He came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, threw it before Him, and said: "This is my sword; it has broken." On that day, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم gave him Dhū al-Fiqār. When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saw his legs twitching (اختلاج ساقيه) from the intensity of the fighting, He raised His head to the sky, weeping, and said: "O Lord, You promised me that You would make Your religion prevail, and if You will, You will not fail me." Ali (peace be upon him) approached the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, I hear a loud rumbling (دويّاً شديداً), and I hear the sound of Aqdm Hayzūm (أقدم حيزوم), and I do not intend to strike anyone except that he falls dead before I strike him." He said: "That is Gabriel, Michael, and Israfil among the angels." Then Gabriel (peace be upon him) came and stood beside the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, this is indeed true support (المواساة)." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, Ali is from me, and I am from him." Gabriel (peace be upon him) said: "And I am from both of you." Then the people fled. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Ali: "O Ali, proceed with your sword until you confront them. If you see them riding the young she-camels (القلاص) and leading the horses, they intend to go to Mecca. But if you see them riding the horses and leading the young she-camels (القلاص) (1), they intend to go to Medina." Ali (peace be upon him) went to them, and they were riding the young she-camels (القلاص). Abu Sufyan said to Ali (peace be upon him): "What do you want? We are going to Mecca now, so return to your companion." Gabriel followed them, and whenever they heard the sound of his horse's hoof, they hastened their journey. He continued to follow them, and whenever they stopped, they would say: "Here is Muhammad's army صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم approaching." Abu Sufyan entered Mecca and informed them of the news. The shepherds and woodcutters came and entered Mecca and said: "We saw Muhammad's army; whenever Abu Sufyan departed, they would encamp, led by a horseman on a reddish-yellow horse, seeking their tracks." So, the people of Mecca turned to Abu Sufyan, reproaching him. Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم departed, with the banner (الراية) carried by Ali (peace be upon him), who was before Him. When he raised the banner from the pass (العقبة) behind the people, Ali (peace be upon him) called out: "O people, this is Muhammad! He has not died, nor has He been killed!" The one who had said, "Now he is mocking us, even though we have been defeated," said: "This is Ali, and the banner is in his hand." Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم suddenly came upon (2) them, and the women of the Ansar were in their courtyards and at the doors of their houses. The men came out, seeking refuge with Him and repenting to Him. The women of the Ansar had scratched their faces, let down their hair, cut (3) their forelocks, torn their collars, and ripped their bellies for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. When He saw them, He spoke well to them and commanded them to cover themselves and enter their homes. He said: "Indeed, Allah promised me that He would make His religion prevail over all other religions." And Allah revealed to Muhammad: وَما مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ (And Muhammad is but an apostle; [other] apostles have passed before him) (the Ayah). وَمَنْ يَنْقَلِبْ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَنْ يَضُرَّ اللهَ شَيْئاً And whoever turns back on his heels will not harm Allah in the least, but rather harms himself. وَسَيَجْزِي اللهُ الشَّاكِرِينَ and Allah will reward the grateful like the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and those who follow his example.

In al-Ihtijaj, in the Sermon of Ghadir: "O assembly of people, I warn you that I am the Messenger of Allah to you; apostles have passed before me. If I die or am killed, انْقَلَبْتُمْ عَلى أَعْقابِكُمْ وَمَنْ يَنْقَلِبْ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَنْ يَضُرَّ اللهَ شَيْئاً وَسَيَجْزِي اللهُ الشَّاكِرِينَ (will you turn back on your heels? And whoever turns back on his heels will not harm Allah in the least, and Allah will reward the grateful.) Behold, Ali is the one described by patience (الصبر) and gratitude (الشكر), and then after me, my children from his loins."

In al-Kafi, in the Sermon of al-Wasilah by the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "...until when Allah called His Prophet and raised Him to Himself, what followed was nothing but a flicker of a heartbeat or a faint flash (1) of lightning, until they turned back on their heels (رجعوا على الأعقاب), retreated on their backs (انتكصوا على الأدبار), sought vengeance (طلبوا بالأوتار), displayed the battalions (الكتائب), blocked the door (ردموا الباب), plundered the homes (فلوا الديار), altered the traces of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, turned away from His rulings, distanced themselves from His lights, and replaced His successor (مستخلفه) with a substitute whom they adopted, and they were wrongdoers (ظالمين). They claimed that the one they chose from the family of Abu Quhafah was more worthy of the station of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم than the one whom the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم chose for His station, and that the emigrants (مهاجر) from the family of Abu Quhafah were better than the emigrants (مهاجري) from the Ansar."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: "The people were apostates (أهل ردة) after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, except for three." It was asked: "And who are the three?" He said: "Al-Miqdad, Abu Dharr, and Salman al-Farisi, may Allah have mercy on them." Then, a few people recognized the truth shortly thereafter. He said: "These are the ones around whom the millstone turned, and they refused to pledge allegiance until they brought the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) under duress, and he pledged allegiance." And that is the meaning of Allah's saying: وَما مُحَمَّدٌ (And Muhammad is but an apostle) (the Ayah).

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Do you know whether the Prophet died or was killed? Indeed, Allah says: أَفَإِنْ ماتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انْقَلَبْتُمْ عَلى أَعْقابِكُمْ (Will you turn back on your heels if he dies or is killed?)" Then he said: "Indeed, the two women gave Him drink before His death," meaning the two wives.

(1) Al-Qalūṣ (القلوص) is a young she-camel and a long-legged she-camel, specific to females. Its plural is qalā’iṣ (قلائص) and quluṣ (قلص), which is the plural of qilāṣ (قلاص) (ق).

(2) Hajama ‘alayhi hujūman (هجم عليه هجوماً) means he reached him suddenly or entered without permission (ق).

(3) Al-Jazz (الجزء) is cutting, and al-kharm (الخرم) is tearing (ق).

(1) Wamaḍa al-barq yamḍu wamḍan wamīḍan (ومض البرق يمض ومضاً وميضاً) means the lightning flashed lightly (ق).

(١٤٥) وَما كانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَنْ تَمُوتَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ (No soul may die except by Allah’s leave) except by His Will (بمشيته) or by His permission (باذنه) to the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) to seize its soul. It will not delay for an hour by holding back (بالاحجام) from fighting, nor will it hasten forward by advancing (بالإقدام) upon it. And in this is an incitement and encouragement to fight. The death is written ** كِتاباً مُؤَجَّلاً (as a fixed term) fixed (مؤقتاً), which neither delays nor advances. ** وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوابَ الدُّنْيا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْها ** (Whoever desires the reward of the world, We shall give him of it) This is an allusion to those whom the spoils (الغنائم) distracted on the Day of Uhud. ** وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوابَ الْآخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِ مِنْها (and whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter, We shall give him of it) meaning, of its reward (ثوابها). وَسَنَجْزِي الشَّاكِرِينَ (and soon We shall reward the grateful) those who were grateful for Allah's favor (نعمة الله) and were not distracted from Jihad (الجهاد) by anything.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Sixty wounds afflicted Ali (peace be upon him) on the Day of Uhud, and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded Umm Sulaym and Umm Atiyyah to treat him. They said: "We do not treat one place on him except that another place bursts open, and we fear for him." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Muslims entered to visit him, and he was one single wound (١). He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم began wiping him with His hand and saying: "Indeed, a man who encountered this for the sake of Allah has performed excellently and is excused." The wound (القرح) that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم wiped would heal. Then Ali (peace be upon him) said: "All praise belongs to Allah, for I did not flee nor turn my back." So Allah thanked him for that in two places in the Quran, which are His saying: وَسَيَجْزِي اللهُ الشَّاكِرِينَ وَسَنَجْزِي الشَّاكِرِينَ. (and Allah will reward the grateful and soon We shall reward the grateful).

(١٤٦) وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ نَبِيٍ (How many a prophet) And how many a prophet. And it was recited كائن (ككاعن). ** قَاتَلَ مَعَهُ (fought alongside him) waged war. And it was recited قُتِلَ with a ḍammah on the qāf. ** رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ (many godly men) It was said: meaning scholars (علماء), jurists (فقهاء), patient ones (صبّر). And it was said: the multitude (المجموع).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The ربيون are ten thousand.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ نَبِيٍّ قُتِلَ مَعَهُ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ (How many a prophet was killed alongside him by many godly men). He said: "Thousands and thousands." Then he said: "Yes, by Allah, they are killed." فَما وَهَنُوا لِما أَصابَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (Yet they did not falter because of what befell them in the way of Allah). So they did not slacken, nor was their resolve broken by the killing of those among them who were killed. ** وَما ضَعُفُوا (nor did they weaken) in religion, nor against the enemy. ** وَمَا اسْتَكانُوا (nor did they abase themselves) Nor did they submit to the enemy. And this is an allusion to what befell them when the rumor (الإرجاف) (2) of his killing صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم spread.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah, the Glorified, clarified that even if the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had been killed, as was rumored (أُرجف) on the Day of Uhud, that would not necessitate that they weaken or falter, just as those who were with the prophets did not falter when their prophets were killed. وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الصَّابِرِينَ (And Allah loves the patient.) So He grants them victory in the end (العاقبة) and magnifies their status.

The Almighty's saying: (١٤٧) وَما كانَ قَوْلَهُمْ despite their steadfastness and strength in religion and their being godly scholars (ربانيين), إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنا ذُنُوبَنا وَإِسْرافَنا فِي أَمْرِنا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدامَنا وَانْصُرْنا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكافِرِينَ (147) And their saying was only that they said, 'Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our excesses in our affairs, and make our feet steady, and help us against the faithless lot.'

They attributed the sins (الذنوب) and the excesses (الإسراف) to themselves, humbling their souls, and attributing what afflicted them to the evil of their deeds. And they sought forgiveness for them. Then they requested steadfastness in the places of war (مواطن الحرب) and victory over the enemy, so that it may be based on humility (خضوع) and purity (طهارة), and thus be closer to the response (الإِجابة).

(١٤٨) فَآتاهُمُ اللهُ ثَوابَ الدُّنْيا وَحُسْنَ ثَوابِ الْآخِرَةِ (So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the good reward of the Hereafter.)

So Allah gave them, because of seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار) and resorting (اللجأ) to Allah, victory and spoils (الغنيمة) and good renown (حسن الذكر) in this world (الدنيا), and Paradise (الجنة) and bliss (النعيم) in the Hereafter (الآخرة). And He specified the reward of the Hereafter with "goodness" (الحسن), indicating its merit and that it is truly considered (لمعتد به) by Allah.

** وَاللهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ** (and Allah loves the virtuous.)

in their sayings and their deeds.

(١٤٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنْ تُطِيعُوا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَرُدُّوكُمْ عَلى أَعْقابِكُمْ فَتَنْقَلِبُوا خاسِرِينَ. (O you who have faith! If you obey the faithless, they will turn you back on your heels, and you will turn back as losers.)

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the hypocrites (المنافقين) when they said to the believers on the Day of Uhud during the defeat: "Return to your brothers and return to their religion."

(١٥٠) بَلِ اللهُ مَوْلاكُمْ (150) Nay, Allah is your Master your Helper (ناصركم). And it was recited with the nasb (بالنصب) (accusative case) meaning: Nay, rather obey Allah. وَهُوَ خَيْرُ النَّاصِرِينَ and He is the best of helpers. So, be content with Him instead of the guardianship (ولاية) and help (نصر) of anyone else.

(١٥١) سَنُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ (151) We will cast terror into the hearts of the faithless And it was recited with two dammahs (بضمتين) (i.e., الرُّعُب). It is said that this is the fear (الخوف) that was cast into their hearts on the Day of Uhud, until they abandoned fighting and retreated without any [apparent] reason. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I was helped by terror [that reaches] the distance of a month's journey." بِما أَشْرَكُوا بِاللهِ because of their ascribing partners to Allah due to their associating partners with Him ما لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ سُلْطاناً for which He has sent down no authority. meaning, deities (آلهة) for whose association [in worship] there is no proof (حجة) sent down from Allah upon them. And the intent is the negation of both the proof (الحجة) and its sending down (نزولها) entirely. ** وَمَأْواهُمُ النَّارُ وَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الظَّالِمِينَ Their refuge shall be the Fire, and evil is the lodging of the wrongdoers. meaning, their lodging (مثواهم). The explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (الضمير) for emphasis (للتغليظ) and justification (والتعليل).

(١٥٢) وَلَقَدْ صَدَقَكُمُ اللهُ وَعْدَهُ (152) And certainly Allah fulfilled His promise to you (His promise) to you of victory, conditioned upon piety (التقوى) and patience (الصبر). And it was so, until the archers (الرماة) disobeyed. For when the polytheists (المشركين) advanced, the archers began to shower them (1) and the rest struck them with the sword until they were routed, and the Muslims were upon their tracks.

إِذْ تَحُسُّونَهُمْ بِإِذْنِهِ when you were killing them by His leave. Meaning: you were killing them by the leave of Allah, meaning killing them by way of eradication (الاستيصال). Its origin (أصله) is al-ihsas (الاحساس), from ahassahu (أحسه) when He nullified his sense (حسه).

حَتَّى إِذا فَشِلْتُمْ until, when you flagged You became cowardly (جبنتم) and your resolve weakened by inclining towards the spoils (الغنيمة). وَتَنازَعْتُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ (and disputed about the matter) meaning the disagreement of the archers when the polytheists were routed. Some of them said: "What is our position here?" And others said: "We shall not disobey the command of the Messenger." So their commander remained in his place with a small group, and the rest departed for plunder (النهب).

وَعَصَيْتُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما أَراكُمْ ما تُحِبُّونَ and disputed about the matter and disobeyed after He had shown you what you loved (What you loved) of victory (الظفر), spoils (الغنيمة), and the enemy's rout. The answer (جواب) to idha (إذا) is omitted, and it is: He tested you (امتحنكم).

مِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الدُّنْيا Among you are those who desire the world, And they are those who abandoned the post to seize the spoils (الغنيمة). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): Meaning the companions of Abdullah ibn Jubayr (عبد الله بن جبير) who abandoned their posts and went for the spoils (الغنيمة). وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرِيدُ الْآخِرَةَ (and among you are those who desire the Hereafter) And they are those who remained steadfast, preserving the command of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): Meaning Abdullah ibn Jubayr (عبد الله بن جبير) and his companions who remained until they were killed. ثُمَّ صَرَفَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ (Then He turned you away from them) He restrained you from them when they overcame you. لِيَبْتَلِيَكُمْ (so that He might test you) regarding the calamities (المصائب) and test your steadfastness upon faith (الإِيمان) during them. وَلَقَدْ عَفا عَنْكُمْ (And certainly He has excused you) as a favor (تفضلاً) and because He knew of your regret (ندمكم) over the disobedience (المخالفة). وَاللهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (and Allah is gracious to the faithful) He bestows favor upon them through forgiveness (العفو) and other things in all circumstances, whether victory (2) is granted to them or against them, since the trial (الابتلاء) is also a mercy (رحمة).

(١٥٣) إِذْ تُصْعِدُونَ (when you were fleeing) It is related to صَرَفَكُمْ (He turned you away). And al-is'ad (الإِصعاد) is the going forth and the moving far away (الإبعاد) in the land.

وَلا تَلْوُونَ عَلى أَحَدٍ (not looking askance at anyone) No one stopped for anyone, nor did he wait for him.

وَالرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ (while the Apostle was calling you) He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was saying to the servants of Allah: "O servants of Allah, return! I am the Messenger of Allah. To where are you fleeing, away from Allah and away from His Messenger?" And in a narration: "Whoever turns back (يَكُرّ) will have Paradise."

فِي أُخْراكُمْ (in your rear) In your rear guard (ساقتكم) and your other group (جماعتكم الأخرى).

فَأَثابَكُمْ غَمًّا بِغَمٍ (He requited you with grief upon grief) So Allah recompensed you for your killing and your disobedience with grief connected to grief. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for the first grief, it was the defeat and the killing, and the other grief was the appearance (اشراف) of Khalid ibn al-Walid over them.

لِكَيْلا تَحْزَنُوا عَلى ما فاتَكُمْ (so that you might not grieve for what you missed) of the spoils (الغنيمة).

وَلا (nor) nor upon ما أَصابَكُمْ (for what befell you) of the killing of your brothers.

وَاللهُ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ (and Allah is well aware of what you do)

(1) Al-rashq (الرشق) with fathah (الفتح) followed by sukūn (السكون) is the throwing (الرمي). And rashaqa-hu yarshuqu-hu (رشقه يرشقه) from the pattern of qatala (قتل), rashqan (رشقا), means when he threw arrows (السهام) at him. And al-rishq (الرشق) with kasrah (الكسر) is the number of throws (الرمي) upon which they agree (يتفقان). [Majma' (مجمع)].

(2) Dālat al-ayyām (دالت الأيّام): meaning, they revolved (دارت). And Allah yudāwilu-hā (يداولها) among the people, meaning He causes them to revolve (يديرها). And tadāwalat-hu al-aydī (تداولته الأيدي): means this hand took it once and that hand took it once. Until he said: It is said udīla lanā ‘alā a‘dā’inā (أديل لنا على أعدائنا), meaning we were granted victory (نصرنا) over them. [Majma' (مجمع)].

(١٥٤) ثُمَّ أَنْزَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِن بَعْدِ الْغَمِ (154) Then He sent down to you, after the distress, يعني الهزيمة أَمَنَةً نُعاساً meaning the defeat, a security, a slumber, a security until slumber overtook you. يَغْشى طائِفَةً مِنْكُمْ overtaking a faction of you. And it was recited with the tā’ (تغشى), and they are the true believers. It is narrated that slumber overtook them in the battle lines (المصاف) until the sword would fall upon the hand of one of them, then fall down, and he would pick it up.

وَطائِفَةٌ هم المنافقون قَدْ أَهَمَّتْهُمْ أَنْفُسُهُمْ And a faction (طائِفَةٌ)—they are the hypocrites—whose own souls had preoccupied them. Their souls cast them into worries, since what concerned them most was their own selves and seeking their deliverance. يَظُنُّونَ بِاللهِ غَيْرَ الْحَقِ thinking ill of Allah, the thought of ignorance. They thought that the affair of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was destined to vanish and that He would not grant him victory. ظَنَّ الْجاهِلِيَّةِ The thought of the people of the era of ignorance (الملّة الجاهلية), meaning the disbelievers. يَقُولُونَ هَلْ لَنا مِنَ الْأَمْرِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ They say, 'Do we have any share in the matter?' Any share whatsoever of victory and triumph, as He promised us, or any choice in managing and directing our own affairs? They say that by way of denial (الإنكار). قُلْ إِنَّ الْأَمْرَ كُلَّهُ لِلَّهِ Say, 'Indeed the matter belongs wholly to Allah.' The true dominance belongs to Allah, the Exalted, and His allies, فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللهِ هُمُ الْغالِبُونَ ** or the victory, the martyrdom, and the decree all belong to Allah. He does whatever He wills, and يَحْكُمُ ما يُرِيدُ He decrees whatever He desires. And kullahu (كُلَّهُ) was recited with the nominative case (الرفع). يُخْفُونَ فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ ما لا يُبْدُونَ لَكَ They conceal in their hearts what they do not disclose to you. They show that they are seeking guidance (مسترشدون) and asking for victory, but they conceal denial (الإِنكار) and rejection (التكذيب). يَقُولُونَ They say within their souls, and when some of them are alone with others, لَوْ كانَ لَنا مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ 'Had we had a share in the matter, Had the victory been ours and the choice belonged to us, we would not have been killed here. We would not have left Medina, but rather we would have stayed there, as was the opinion of Ibn Ubayy and others. Thus, we would not have been defeated, nor would those among us who were killed in this battle have died. قُلْ لَوْ كُنْتُمْ فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ لَبَرَزَ الَّذِينَ كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَتْلُ Say, 'Even if you had been in your homes, those for whom killing had been decreed would have gone forth those whom Allah had decreed to be killed and whose fate was written in the Preserved Tablet (اللوح المحفوظ) would have gone forth إِلى مَضاجِعِهِمْ to their resting places.' to their places of death (مصارعهم). Residence in Medina would not have benefited them, and no one would have escaped the killing, because what Allah has decreed of affairs and planned in His prior judgment (سابق قضائه) has no repeller, for there is no reversing His decree (قضائه) and no preventing His judgment (حكمه). وَلِيَبْتَلِيَ اللهُ ما فِي صُدُورِكُمْ And so that Allah may test what is in your breasts, and so that Allah may examine what is in your breasts and reveal their secrets (سرائرها) of sincerity (الإِخلاص) and hypocrisy (النفاق), He did what He did. وَلِيُمَحِّصَ ما فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ and purge what is in your hearts. and so that He may uncover it and distinguish it. وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ And Allah knows best what is in the breasts. He is Knower of their hidden matters (خفياتها) before their manifestation. And in this is a promise and a threat, and an alert that He is independent of the testing (الابتلاء), and He only did that to train the believers and reveal the state of the hypocrites.

(١٥٥) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا مِنْكُمْ يَوْمَ الْتَقَى الْجَمْعانِ (155) Indeed, those of you who turned their backs on the day when the two hosts met— They were defeated on the Day of Uhud, and the two hosts (الجمعان) were the host of the Muslims and the host of the polytheists (المشركين).

إِنَّمَا اسْتَزَلَّهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ it was only Satan who made them slip He carried them toward the slip (الزلة).

بِبَعْضِ ما كَسَبُوا because of some of what they had earned. [This was] due to their disobedience to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by abandoning their post (المركز), being greedy for the spoils (الغنيمة), and other such things. Thus, they were prevented from receiving support (التأييد) and strength of heart (قوة القلب).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: They are the people of al-Aqabah (أصحاب العقبة).

وَلَقَدْ عَفَا اللهُ عَنْهُمْ Certainly Allah has excused them. Due to their repentance (توبتهم) and their seeking forgiveness (اعتذارهم).

إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, of sins (الذنوب).

حَلِيمٌ all-forbearing. He does not hasten the punishment of the sinner (المذنب) so that he may repent.

(١٥٦) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (156) O you who have faith! Do not be like the faithless meaning the hypocrites (المنافقين). وَقالُوا لِإِخْوانِهِمْ—for their sake and concerning them— إِذا ضَرَبُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ when they journeyed therein and died أَوْ كانُوا غُزًّى or were combatants فَقُتِلُوا لَوْ كانُوا عِنْدَنا ما ماتُوا وَما قُتِلُوا لِيَجْعَلَ اللهُ ذلِكَ حَسْرَةً فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ and said to their brethren when they traveled in the land or were combatants: "Had they been with us, they would not have died or been killed." so that Allah may make that a regret in their hearts. The lām (اللام) here denotes the consequence (العاقبة), similar to its use in لِيَكُونَ لَهُمْ عَدُوًّا وَحَزَناً that he may be an enemy and a grief for them. وَاللهُ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ And Allah gives life and brings death. This is a refutation of their saying, meaning: He is the One Who gives life (المحيي) and brings death (المميت), not residence (الإقامة) or travel (السفر). For He, the Exalted, may give life to the traveler (المسافر) and the combatant (الغازي), and bring death to the resident (المقيم) and the sedentary (القاعد). وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ And Allah is watchful of what you do. This is a threat (تهديد) to the believers against resembling them, and according to the reading with the yā’ (الياء) [i.e., يَعْمَلُونَ], it is a warning (وعيد) to those who disbelieved.

(١٥٧) وَلَئِنْ قُتِلْتُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَوْ مُتُّمْ (157) And if you are killed in the way of Allah, or die,

in His way. And it was read with a kasrah on the mīm.

لَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَحْمَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ surely forgiveness from Allah and mercy are better than what they amass.

And it was read with the tā’. The answer to the oath (جواب القسم) was put in the place of the consequence (الجزاء). And the meaning is that travel and raiding (الغزاة) are not among the things that bring death or advance the appointed time (الأجل). And if that occurs in the way of Allah, then what you attain of forgiveness and mercy through death is better than what you amass of the world and what is in it and its benefits, had you not died or had you not been killed.

(١٥٨) **وَلَئِنْ مُتُّمْ أَوْ قُتِلْتُمْ لَإِلَى اللهِ تُحْشَرُونَ ** (And if you die or are slain, surely to Allah you will be mustered) upon whatever manner it occurs, ** لَإِلَى اللهِ تُحْشَرُونَ ** (surely to Allah you will be mustered) in all circumstances.

In al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: That the path (سبيل) of Allah is Ali and his progeny. Whoever is slain in his wilayah (ولاية) is slain in the path of Allah (سبيل الله), and whoever dies in his wilayah (ولاية) dies in the path of Allah (سبيل الله). And a narrative concerning the difference between death (الموت) and being slain (القتل) has already preceded during the interpretation of His saying: ** أَفَإِنْ ماتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انْقَلَبْتُمْ ** from this Surah.

(159) فَبِما رَحْمَةٍ مِنَ اللهِ لِنْتَ لَهُمْ (It is by a mercy of Allah that you are gentle to them) The mā (ما) is augmentative (مزيدة) for emphasis (للتأكيد). His gentleness reached the point that He grieved for them after they had disobeyed Him. وَلَوْ كُنْتَ فَظًّا (And had you been harsh) ill-mannered, coarse ** غَلِيظَ الْقَلْبِ (hard-hearted) hardened ** لَانْفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ (they would have scattered from around you) they would have dispersed from you and would not have found comfort in you. ** فَاعْفُ عَنْهُمْ ** (So excuse them) regarding what pertains specifically to you ** وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ ** (and plead for forgiveness for them) regarding what pertains to Allah ** وَشاوِرْهُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ (and consult them in the matter) regarding the matter of war and other things concerning which consultation is proper, in order to seek support (استظهاراً) through their opinion, to soothe their souls (تطييباً لنفوسهم), and to establish the tradition (سنة) of consultation for the nation (للامة).

Narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "There is no loneliness more desolate than conceit (العجب), and no support (مظاهرة) more reliable than consultation (المشاورة)."

In Nahj al-Balagha: "Whoever acts independently based on his own opinion perishes, and whoever consults men shares in their intellects (عقولها)." And therein: "Consultation (الاستشارة) is the essence (عين) of guidance, and he who deems his own opinion sufficient has risked danger (1)."

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding ** وَشاوِرْهُمْ** (and consult them): "Consult in your affair those who fear Allah."

And al-Ayyashi narrated: Al-Jawad (peace be upon him) wrote to Ali ibn Mahziyar: "Ask so-and-so to advise (2) me and to choose what is best for himself, for he knows what is permissible (يجوز) in his land and how to deal with the rulers (السلاطين). Indeed, consultation (المشاورة) is blessed." Allah the Almighty said to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in His decisive Book, and He recited this Ayah.

He said: ** وَشاوِرْهُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ (and consult them in the matter) means seeking guidance (الاستخارة). ** فَإِذا عَزَمْتَ (Then, when you have resolved) means: when you have settled your soul upon something after the consultation (الشورى), ** فَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ (put your trust in Allah) in carrying out your affair (إمضاء أمرك) upon what is most beneficial (أصلح) for you, for none knows it besides Him. The general populace (العامّة) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding ** فَإِذا عَزَمْتَ (Then, when you have resolved) with a ḍammah on the tā’ (بضم التاء), meaning: when He has resolved for you, granted you success (وفقتك), and guided you (أرشدتك). إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَوَكِّلِينَ (Indeed Allah loves those who rely on Him) so He grants them victory and guides them to righteousness (الصلاح).

(١٦٠) إِنْ يَنْصُرْكُمُ اللهُ (160) If Allah helps you, just as He helped you on the Day of Badr, ** فَلا غالِبَ لَكُمْ no one will overcome you. So no one will defeat you. وَإِنْ يَخْذُلْكُمْ ** But if He forsakes you, just as He forsook you on the Day of Uhud, ** فَمَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يَنْصُرُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ who is it that can help you after Him? There is no helper for you after Allah, if you disregard Him, and after His forsaking. ** وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** And in Allah let the faithful put their trust. So let them single Him out for reliance (التوكل) because they have believed in Him and know that there is no helper besides Him.

footnote (١) من المخاطرة وهي ارتكاب ما فيه خطر «م». (1) From al-mukhatarah (المخاطرة), which is undertaking that which involves danger (خطر) [m].

footnote (٢) لعل المراد من قوله «ع» **يشير علي :** أي سله يظهر لي ما عنده من مصلحتي في أمر كذا ويتخير لنفسه أي يتخير لي تخيراً كتخيره لنفسه كما هو شأن الأخ المحب المحبوب الذي يخشى الله تعالى. (2) Perhaps the intent of his saying (peace be upon him): He advises me (يشير علي): meaning, ask Him, and He will reveal to me what He possesses of benefit (مصلحتي) for me in such-and-such an affair, and He chooses for Himself (ويتخير لنفسه), meaning, He chooses for me a choice (تخيراً) like His choice for Himself, as is the manner of the loving, beloved brother who fears Allah, the Exalted.

(١٦١) وَما كانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ يَغُلَ (It is not for a prophet to act unfaithfully) And it is not proper for a prophet to act unfaithfully (أن يخون) regarding the spoils of war (في الغنائم), for prophethood (النبوّة) contradicts treachery (الخيانة). Al-Ghulūl (الغلول) is taking something from the spoils of war (المغنم) secretly (في خفية). And it was read with a ḍammah on the yā’ and a fatḥah on the ghayn (يُغَلَّ), meaning: he is attributed to treachery (ينسب إلى الخيانة).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): It was revealed concerning the Battle of Badr (حرب بدر). The reason for its revelation was that among the spoils of war (الغنيمة) they acquired on the Day of Badr was a red velvet cloak (قطيفة حمراء), and it was lost. A man from the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Why do we not see the cloak? I do not think otherwise except that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took it." So, Allah revealed this verse concerning that. Then a man came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "Indeed, so-and-so acted unfaithfully (غل) regarding a cloak, so dig for it there." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that that place be dug up, and the cloak was brought out.

In al-Majalis, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Indeed, the contentment (رضاء) of people cannot be possessed (لا يملك), and their tongues cannot be restrained (لا تضبط). Did they not attribute to him on the Day of Badr that He took a red velvet cloak for Himself from the spoils of war, until Allah made Him aware of the cloak (أظهره الله على القطيفة) and acquitted His Prophet (وبرَّأ نبيه) of treachery, and revealed in His Book: ** وَما كانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ يَغُلَ** (It is not for a prophet to act unfaithfully) [the rest of] the verse."

وَمَنْ يَغْلُلْ يَأْتِ بِما غَلَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ (Whoever acts unfaithfully shall bring what he has acted unfaithfully with on the Day of Resurrection) He will carry it upon his neck (يحمله على عنقه).

And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: "And whoever acts unfaithfully (غل) regarding something will see it on the Day of Resurrection in the Fire, then he will be tasked (يكلف) to enter into it and bring it out from the Fire."

ثُمَّ تُوَفَّى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ما كَسَبَتْ (Then every soul shall be paid in full for what it has earned) It will be given the reward (جزاء) for what it earned completely (وافياً). He generalized the ruling (عمم الحكم) so that it may serve as a proof (كالبرهان) for the intended meaning (على المقصود).

وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ (and they will not be wronged) The reward (ثواب) of their obedient ones will not be diminished, nor will the punishment (عقاب) of their disobedient ones be increased.

(١٦٢) أَفَمَنِ اتَّبَعَ رِضْوانَ اللهِ (Is someone who follows Allah’s pleasure) through obedience (بالطاعة) كَمَنْ باءَ (like someone who incurs) meaning: returned (رجع) ** بِسَخَطٍ مِنَ اللهِ (Allah’s wrath) through disobedience (بالمعصية) ** وَمَأْواهُ جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ. (and his refuge is hell, and it is an evil destination?)

(١٦٣) هُمْ دَرَجاتٌ عِنْدَ اللهِ (They are ranks with Allah) It was said: [They are] possessors of ranks (ذو درجات), or they were likened to ranks (بالدرجات) due to the difference (التفاوت) between them.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Those who followed Allah’s pleasure (الذين اتبعوا رضوان الله) are the Imams (الأئمة). And they are, by Allah, ranks (درجات) with Allah for the believers. And through their wilayah (بولايتهم) and their recognition (ومعرفتهم) of us, Allah multiplies their deeds for them, and Allah raises the highest ranks (الدرجات العلى) for them."

And al-Ayyashi added: "And those who incurred Allah’s wrath (والذين باؤوا بسخط من الله) are those who denied the right of Ali and the right of the Imams from us, the People of the House (أهل البيت), may the blessings of Allah be upon them (صلوات الله عليهم). Thus, they incurred Allah’s wrath (بسخط من الله) for that."

And narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace and blessings be upon him): "The rank (الدرجة) is what is between the heaven and the earth." وَاللهُ بَصِيرٌ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ (and Allah is watchful of what they do) so He will recompense them according to it.

(١٦٤) لَقَدْ مَنَّ اللهُ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (164) Allah has certainly favored the faithful Allah bestowed favor upon them إِذْ بَعَثَ فِيهِمْ رَسُولاً مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ when He sent among them a Messenger from themselves an Arab like them, so that they might understand His speech easily يَتْلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ آياتِهِ who recites to them His signs meaning the Quran, after they were ignorant people who had not heard the revelation (الوحي) وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ and purifies them He cleanses them from evil beliefs (العقائد), morals (الأخلاق), and deeds (الأعمال) وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ and teaches them the Book and the Wisdom the Quran and the Sunnah (السنة) وَإِنْ كانُوا ** and though they were And indeed they were مِنْ قَبْلُ before before His sending (the Messenger) لَفِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ certainly in manifest error. apparent (ظاهراً).

(165) أَوَلَمَّا أَصابَتْكُمْ مُصِيبَةٌ قَدْ أَصَبْتُمْ مِثْلَيْها (When a disaster struck you, though you had inflicted twice as much [on the enemy]) The hamzah (الهمزة) is for reproach (التقريع) and confirmation (التقرير).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Muslims had afflicted one hundred and forty men at Badr: they killed seventy men and captured seventy. When the Day of Uhud came, seventy men were afflicted among the Muslims, and they grieved because of that. So, the verse was revealed: قُلْتُمْ أَنَّى هذا (You said, 'Whence is this?') —meaning, from where did this affliction come to us, when Allah had promised us victory? قُلْ هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ أَنْفُسِكُمْ (Say, 'It is from yourselves') —due to your choice of ransom (الفداء) on the Day of Badr. Similarly, it is narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him) in al-Majma'.

Al-Qummi said: The ruling concerning the captives on the Day of Badr was execution. So, the Ansar stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah, grant them to us and do not kill them until we ransom them." Then Gabriel descended and said that Allah had permitted them the ransom (الفداء): that they should take [money] from these people and release them, on the condition that a number of them equal to the number from whom they take ransom (فداء) would be martyred in the following year. The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) informed them of this condition. They said: "We are pleased with it. This year we will take the ransom (الفداء) from these people and strengthen ourselves with it, and in the following year, a number of us equal to those from whom we take the ransom (الفداء) will be killed, and we will enter Paradise." So, they took the ransom (الفداء) from them and released them. When the Day of Uhud came, seventy of the companions of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) were killed. They said: "O Messenger of Allah, what is this that has afflicted us, when you used to promise us victory?" So, Allah revealed ** أَوَلَمَّا أَصابَتْكُمْ (When a disaster struck you...)—the Ayah—هُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ أَنْفُسِكُمْ (It is from yourselves) —meaning: due to what you stipulated on the Day of Badr. The complete story of Badr will come in Surat al-Anfal, if Allah the Almighty wills.

إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (Indeed Allah has power over all things.) Thus, He has power over granting victory and withholding it, and over afflicting [the enemy] through you, and afflicting you through [the enemy].

(١٦٦) وَمَا أَصَابَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْتَقَى الْجَمْعَانِ فَبِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (166) (166) What befell you on the day when the two hosts met was by Allah’s leave, and so that He may ascertain the faithful, The phrase يَوْمَ الْتَقَى الْجَمْعانِ means the Day of Uhud. فَبِإِذْنِ اللهِ (was by Allah’s leave) means it occurred by His decree, allowing (تخلية) (1) the disbelievers [to prevail temporarily]. وَلِيَعْلَمَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (and so that He may ascertain the faithful).

(١٦٧) وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ نَافَقُوا وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا قَاتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَوِ ادْفَعُوا قَالُوا لَوْ نَعْلَمُ قِتَالًا لَاتَّبَعْنَاكُمْ هُمْ لِلْكُفْرِ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَقْرَبُ مِنْهُمْ لِلْإِيمَانِ يَقُولُونَ بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ مَا لَيْسَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَكْتُمُونَ (167) (167) And so that He may ascertain the hypocrites. When they were told, ‘Come, fight in the way of Allah, or defend [yourselves],’ they said, ‘Had we known there would be a fight, we would have followed you.’ That day they were nearer to disbelief than to faith. They say with their mouths what is not in their hearts, and Allah knows best what they conceal. وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ نافَقُوا (And so that He may ascertain the hypocrites). And so that the two parties may be distinguished by the manifestation of the faith of the former and the disbelief of the latter. وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ (When they were told)—meaning, to the hypocrites—تَعالَوْا قاتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَوِ ادْفَعُوا (Come, fight in the way of Allah, or defend [yourselves])—meaning, defend yourselves, your souls, and your wealth, or [defend] by increasing the numbers (بتكثير السواد). قالُوا لَوْ نَعْلَمُ قِتالاً لَاتَّبَعْناكُمْ (they said, ‘Had we known there would be a fight, we would have followed you.’). They said this deceitfully (دغلا) (2) and mockingly, claiming that what they were doing was not fighting, but rather casting their souls into destruction (التهلكة). هُمْ لِلْكُفْرِ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَقْرَبُ مِنْهُمْ لِلْإِيمانِ (That day they were nearer to disbelief than to faith), as is evident from this speech of theirs. يَقُولُونَ بِأَفْواهِهِمْ ما لَيْسَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ (They say with their mouths what is not in their hearts); they manifest the opposite of what they conceal. وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَكْتُمُونَ (and Allah knows best what they conceal) of hypocrisy (النفاق) and what some of them discuss privately with others, for He knows it in detail (مفصلاً) with an essential knowledge (بعلم واجب), whereas you only know it generally (مجملاً) through signs (بأمارات).

In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in his discourse: "And whoever has weak certainty (يقينه) clings to means (الأسباب), permits (رخص) (3) that for himself, and follows customs and the sayings of people without reality (بغير حقيقته). And the striving in the affairs of the world, and gathering it, and withholding it—he affirms with his tongue that there is no preventer and no giver except Allah, and that the servant attains nothing except what He has provided and apportioned for him, and that effort does not increase provision (الرزق). Yet, he denies this with his action and his heart." Allah the Almighty said: يَقُولُونَ بِأَفْواهِهِمْ ما لَيْسَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما يَكْتُمُونَ (They say with their mouths what is not in their hearts, and Allah knows best what they conceal.)

(168) الَّذِينَ قالُوا (Those who said) This is another description for them. لِإِخْوانِهِمْ (to their brethren) means for their sake and concerning them, referring to those among them who were killed on the Day of Uhud ** وَقَعَدُوا ** (while they stayed behind) is a circumstantial clause, meaning while they were staying behind from the fighting لَوْ أَطاعُونا (Had they obeyed us) in staying behind ** ما قُتِلُوا (they would not have been killed) just as we were not killed ** قُلْ فَادْرَؤُا (Say: Then avert) meaning, repel ** عَنْ أَنْفُسُكُمُ الْمَوْتَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ** (from yourselves death, if you are truthful). (If you are truthful) that you are able to repel killing and its causes from whomever it has been decreed upon, then that is more fitting for you. And the meaning is that staying behind is not beneficial, for the causes of death are numerous. Just as fighting can be a cause of destruction and staying behind a cause of salvation, the matter may sometimes be the reverse.

(١٦٩) ** وَلا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَمْواتاً (Do not suppose that those who are slain in the way of Allah are dead) And it was recited with tashdid (بالتشديد). It was revealed concerning the martyrs of Badr and Uhud together, as stated in al-Majma' from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). And it includes everyone who is killed in any path (سبيل) among the paths (سبل) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), whether his killing was through the lesser jihad (الجهاد الأصغر) and the sacrifice of the soul (بذل النفس) seeking the pleasure of Allah, or through the greater jihad (الجهاد الأكبر) and the breaking of the soul (كسر النفس) and the suppression of desire (قمع الهوى) through spiritual discipline (الرياضة). ** بَلْ أَحْياءٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ (Rather they are living, with their Lord) Possessing proximity to Him. ** يُرْزَقُونَ** (provided with sustenance) From Paradise.

(١٧٠) ** فَرِحِينَ بِما آتاهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ (170) [They are] joyous because of what Allah has given them out of His grace And that is the honor of martyrdom (شرف الشهادة), the attainment of eternal life (الحياة الابدية), proximity to Allah the Almighty (القرب من الله تعالى), and the enjoyment of the bliss of Paradise. ** وَيَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَلْحَقُوا بِهِمْ مِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ and they rejoice for those who have not yet joined them from behind them from their believing brethren (إخوانهم المؤمنين) whom they left behind and who have not yet attained their ranks (درجاتهم) after them. ** أَلَّا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ that they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. Meaning, they rejoice that they are safe (آمنون); there is no fear upon them, nor will they grieve.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: A man came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "I am desirous (راغب), eager (نشيط) (1) for al-Jihad." He [the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم] said: "A striver (مُجاهد) in the way of Allah: If you are killed, you will be alive with Allah, receiving sustenance (ترزق). If you die, your reward (أجرك) has fallen upon Allah. And if you return, you will have departed from sins (الذنوب) unto Allah." This is the exegesis (تفسير) of the verse: وَلا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَمْواتاً (And do not suppose that those who are slain in the way of Allah are dead) and the rest of the Ayah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "By Allah, they are our followers (شيعتنا) when their souls (أرواحهم) have reached Paradise (الجنّة) and they have received honor (الكرامة) from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ). They knew and were certain (استيقنوا) that they were upon the truth (الحق) and upon the religion of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (دين الله عزّ وجلّ). So they rejoiced for those of their believing brethren (إخوانهم من خلفهم من المؤمنين) who have not yet joined them from behind them."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "They narrate that the souls (أرواح) of the believers are in the crops (حواصل) of green birds around the Throne (العرش)." He said: "No. The believer is more honored (أكرم) by Allah than that He should place his soul in the crops (حواصل) (1) of a bird. Rather, [they are] in bodies (أبدان) like their [earthly] bodies." And a similar narration concerning this meaning has already passed in another hadith in Surat al-Baqarah regarding the Almighty's saying: ** وَلا تَقُولُوا لِمَنْ يُقْتَلُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَمْواتٌ** (And do not call those who are slain in the way of Allah 'dead').

(1) نَشِط (nashita) نشاطاً (nashāṭan) with al-fath (بالفتح), so he is ناشط (nāshiṭ) and نشيط (nashīṭ): his soul is willing for work and other things, like تنّشط (tannashshaṭa) (ق).

(١٧١) يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِنِعْمَةٍ مِنَ اللهِ ** (They rejoice in a blessing from Allah) which is their security as a reward for their deeds, وَفَضْلٍ (and grace) which is the increase upon it, like the Almighty's saying: ** لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنى وَزِيادَةٌ (For those who are virtuous is the best reward and more) and the indefiniteness of the two (words) is for magnification (للتعظيم). وَأَنَّ اللهَ لا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (and that Allah does not waste the reward of the faithful) (The phrase) is part of that which is rejoiced in, with the kasrah (of the hamzah of أَنَّ), and the hamzah was also recited with al-istīnaf (as a new beginning (الاستيناف)).

(١٧٢) الَّذِينَ اسْتَجابُوا لِلَّهِ وَالرَّسُولِ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما أَصابَهُمُ الْقَرْحُ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا مِنْهُمْ وَاتَّقَوْا أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ. (172) (Those who responded to Allah and the Apostle after they had been afflicted by the wound—for those of them who are virtuous and Godwary there is a great reward.)

Al-Qummi narrated: When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered al-Madinah after the Battle of Uhud, Gabriel descended upon him and said: "O Muhammad, Allah commands you to go out in pursuit of the people (القوم), and none shall go out with you except those who have wounds."

So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded a caller to announce: "O company of the Muhajirun and the Ansar! Whoever has a wound, let him go out with it, and whoever does not have a wound, let him stay." So they came forward, bandaging their wounds and treating them, and they went out despite the pain and injuries they suffered.

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم reached Hamra’ al-Asad, and Quraysh had encamped at al-Rawha’, ‘Ikrimah ibn Abi Jahl, al-Harith ibn Hisham, ‘Amr ibn al-‘As, and Khalid ibn al-Walid said: "We should return and raid (نغير) al-Madinah. We have killed their nobles (سراتهم) and their chief (كبشهم)," meaning Hamzah.

Then a man who had left al-Madinah met them, and they asked him for news. He said: "I left Muhammad and his companions at Hamra’ al-Asad, seeking you with great earnestness."

So Abu Sufyan said: "This is misfortune (النكد) and transgression (البغي). We have already triumphed over the people, and we have transgressed. By Allah, no people who transgressed have ever succeeded."

Then Nu‘aym ibn Mas‘ud al-Ashja‘i met them. Abu Sufyan asked: "Where are you going?" He replied: "To al-Madinah, to procure (لأمتار) food for my family."

He said: "Would you pass by Hamra’ al-Asad and meet the companions of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and inform them that our allies and supporters have joined us, having come to us from al-Ahabish (الأحابيش), so that they might turn back from us? For you, I have ten young she-camels (قلائص) which I will fill with dates and raisins." He replied: "Yes."

So, on the morrow of that day, he arrived at Hamra’ al-Asad and said to the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Where are you going?" They said: "To Quraysh." He said: "Go back! Quraysh have gathered their allies and those who had lagged behind them. I believe that the vanguard of their cavalry will appear before you this very hour."

So they said: حَسْبُنَا اللهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ (Sufficient for us is Allah, and [He is] the best trustee.) "We do not care."

Then Gabriel descended upon the Messenger of Allah and said: "Return, O Muhammad, for Allah has struck terror (أرعب) into Quraysh, and they are passing by, not turning back for anything." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned to al-Madinah, and Allah revealed: الَّذِينَ اسْتَجابُوا لِلَّهِ وَالرَّسُولِ (Those who responded to Allah and the Apostle) and the subsequent verses.

(١٧٣) الَّذِينَ قالَ لَهُمُ النَّاسُ (173) Those to whom the people said meaning Nu'aym ibn Mas'ud al-Ashja'i, as stated in al-Majma' (المجمع) narrated from both of them (peace be upon them). إِنَّ النَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا لَكُمْ that the people have gathered against you meaning Abu Sufyan and his companions. ** فَاخْشَوْهُمْ فَزادَهُمْ إِيماناً وَقالُوا حَسْبُنَا اللهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ. so fear them. But this only increased their faith, and they said, "Allah is sufficient for us, and [He is] the best trustee."**

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the Battle of Badr al-Sughra (غزوة بدر الصغرى). That was because Abu Sufyan said on the Day of Uhud (يوم أُحد), when he intended to depart: "O Muhammad, our appointed time between us and you is the season of Badr al-Sughra (موسم بدر الصغرى) next year, if you wish." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "That is between us and you." When the following year came, Abu Sufyan set out with the people of Mecca until he stopped at Majannah (2) from the direction of Marr (3) al-Dhahrān (مر الظهران). Then Allah cast terror into his heart, and he decided to return.

He met Nu'aym ibn Mas'ud al-Ashja'i, who had arrived performing Umrah (معتمراً). Abu Sufyan said to him: "I promised Muhammad that we would meet at the season of Badr al-Sughra (موسم بدر الصغرى). This year is a year of drought, and nothing will benefit us except a year in which we can graze the trees and drink the milk. I have decided not to go out to it, but I dislike that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم should go out while I do not, as that would increase their boldness. So, go to Medina and discourage them (1). You will have ten camels from me, which I will place in the custody of Suhayl ibn 'Amr (سهيل بن عمرو)."

So Nu'aym came to Medina and found the people preparing for Abu Sufyan's appointment. He said: "What a terrible opinion you hold! They came to you in your homes and your settled place, and none escaped them except a fugitive (2). Do you wish to go out when they have gathered against you at the season? By Allah, none (3) of you will escape!" The companions of the Messenger of Allah disliked going out.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, I shall certainly go out, even if I am alone."
