As for the coward, he turned back, but as for the brave, he prepared for battle and said: ** حَسْبُنَا اللهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ (Allah is sufficient for us, and [He is] the best trustee). So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم went out with his companions until he reached Badr al-Sughra (بدر الصغرى), which was a watering place belonging to Banu Kinanah (بني كنانة) and had been the site of a market for them during the Jahiliyyah (الجاهلية), where they would gather for eight days every year.

He stayed at Badr, waiting for Abu Sufyan, but Abu Sufyan had already returned from Majannah (مجنة) to Mecca. The people of Mecca called them the Army of al-Sawīq (جيش السويق), saying: "You only went out to drink al-Sawīq (السويق)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his companions did not meet any of the polytheists at Badr. They reached the market, where they had trade goods, so they sold them, and the dirham (الدرهم) earned two dirhams. They returned to Medina safe and victorious (سالمين غانمين).

(1) And Habshī (حبشي) with a ḍammah (بالضم) is a mountain at the lower part of Mecca. It is said that the Aḥābīsh (أحابيش) of Quraysh were named after it, because Banu al-Muṣṭaliq (بني المصطلق) and Banu al-Hūn ibn Khuzaymah (بني الهون بن خزيمة) gathered there, opposed Quraysh, and swore by Allah: "We are a hand against others as long as night covers and day brightens, and as long as Habshī (حبشي) remains firm in its place." Thus, they were named the Aḥābīsh (أحابيش) of Quraysh after the name of the mountain. [Ṣiḥāḥ (صحاح)]

(2) Al-Majannah (المجنة): The land with many jinn (الجن), and a place near Mecca. Its mīm (ميم) may also be pronounced with a kasrah (تكسر). [Qāmūs (ق)]

(3) Baṭn Marr (بطن مر), also called Marr al-Dhahrān (مر الظهران), is a place one stage (مرحلة) away from Mecca. [Qāmūs (قاموس)]

(١٧٤) فَانْقَلَبُوا (So they returned) — meaning, they returned from Badr. بِنِعْمَةٍ مِنَ اللهِ (with a grace from Allah) — meaning, well-being (عافية), steadfastness upon faith (ثبات على الإيمان), and an increase therein (وزيادة فيه). وَفَضْلٍ (and a bounty) — meaning, profit in trade (وربح في التجارة). لَمْ يَمْسَسْهُمْ سُوءٌ (no evil touched them) — meaning, neither injury (جراحة) nor the enemy's plot (كيد عدو). وَاتَّبَعُوا رِضْوانَ اللهِ (and they followed Allah’s pleasure) — through their boldness (بجرأتهم) and their going forth (وخروجهم). وَاللهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَظِيمٍ (and Allah is dispenser of great bounty). He had bestowed His bounty upon them through steadfastness (بالتثبيت), the increase of faith (وزيادة الإِيمان), success (التوفيق) in hastening to Jihad (للمبادرة إلى الجهاد), firmness in religion (التصلب في الدين), showing boldness against the enemy (وإظهار الجرأة على العدو), and by protecting them from everything that would harm them (يسوؤهم), and by granting them benefit (واصابة النفع) along with the guarantee of reward (ضمان الأجر), until they returned with a grace from Him and a bounty. And in this is regret (تحسير) and censure (تخطئة) for the one who held back (للتخلف), since he deprived himself of what they attained (ما فازوا به).

فَانْقَلَبُوا بِنِعْمَةٍ مِنَ اللهِ وَفَضْلٍ لَمْ يَمْسَسْهُمْ سُوءٌ وَاتَّبَعُوا رِضْوَانَ اللهِ وَاللهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَظِيمٍ (3:174) (So they returned with a grace from Allah and a bounty, no evil having touched them, and they followed Allah’s pleasure, and Allah is dispenser of great bounty.)

(١٧٥) إِنَّما ذلِكُمُ الشَّيْطانُ (175) That is only Satan meaning by it the discourager (المُثَبِّط), and he is Nu'aym (نعيم). يُخَوِّفُ أَوْلِياءَهُ who frightens his followers [meaning] those who stayed behind (القَاعِدِينَ) from going out with the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). فَلا تَخافُوهُمْ وَخافُونِ So do not fear them, and fear Me regarding disobeying My command, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ if you are faithful. For faith necessitates preferring the fear of Allah over the fear of people.

(176) وَلا يَحْزُنْكَ (And let them not grieve you) And it was read with a dammah on the yā' and a kasrah on the zāy (يُحْزِنْكَ). ** الَّذِينَ يُسارِعُونَ فِي الْكُفْرِ (those who hasten to disbelief) fearing that they might harm you and assist against you, and they are the hypocrites from among those who lagged behind. إِنَّهُمْ لَنْ يَضُرُّوا اللهَ شَيْئاً (Indeed, they will not harm Allah in the least.) They will not harm the friends (أولياء) of Allah by their hastening into disbelief; rather, they only harm themselves by it. ** يُرِيدُ اللهُ أَلَّا يَجْعَلَ لَهُمْ حَظًّا فِي الْآخِرَةِ (Allah desires not to grant them any share in the Hereafter,) a portion (نصيباً) of the reward therein. And in this is an indication of the persistence of their transgression and their death upon disbelief, and that their disbelief reached the limit, such that the Most Merciful of the merciful desired that they should have no share of His mercy. ** وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ** (and there is a great punishment for them.) along with the deprivation of the reward.

(١٧٧) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الْكُفْرَ بِالْإِيْمانِ لَنْ يَضُرُّوا اللهَ شَيْئاً وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (177) Indeed those who have boughten disbelief for faith shall not harm Allah in the least, and there is a painful punishment for them. Confirmation and generalization.

(١٧٨) وَلا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّما نُمْلِي لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ لِأَنْفُسِهِمْ إِنَّما نُمْلِي لَهُمْ لِيَزْدادُوا إِثْماً وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ (178) And let those who disbelieve not suppose that the respite We grant them is better for their souls. We grant them respite only so that they may increase in sin, and there is a humiliating punishment for them. And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتاء). Al-Imlāʾ (الاملاء) is granting respite (الامهال) and prolonging life (واطالة العمر), or leaving them to their own devices (تخليتهم وشأنهم). The lām (اللام) is for consequence (للعاقبة), meaning: so that the consequence of their affair is the increase of sin (ازدياد الإثم).

In al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the disbeliever (الكافر): Is death better for him or life? He said: Death is better for the believer (للمؤمن) and the disbeliever (والكافر), because Allah says: وَما عِنْدَ اللهِ خَيْرٌ لِلْأَبْرارِ (What is with Allah is better for the righteous) And He says: وَلا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّما نُمْلِي لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ لِأَنْفُسِهِمْ (And let those who disbelieve not suppose that the respite We grant them is better for their souls.) the Ayah.

(١٧٩) ما كانَ اللهُ لِيَذَرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلى ما أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ (Allah would not leave the faithful in the state you are in) mixed together, such that your sincere ones are not known from your hypocrites, حَتَّى يَمِيزَ الْخَبِيثَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبِ (until He separates the wicked from the good.) until He distinguishes the hypocrite (المنافق) from the sincere one (المخلص) through difficult obligations (التكاليف الشاقة) which none endures or submits to except the pure, sincere ones (الخلص المخلصون). And يَمِيزَ was read (in another recitation) from (التمييز) (distinguishing). وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُطْلِعَكُمْ عَلَى الْغَيْبِ (Nor would Allah apprise you of the Unseen) so that He grants any one of you knowledge of the Unseen (علم الغيب), thereby apprising him of what is in the hearts, be it sincerity (إخلاص) or hypocrisy (نفاق). وَلكِنَّ اللهَ يَجْتَبِي مِنْ رُسُلِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ (but Allah chooses whomever He wishes from His apostles.) so He reveals to him and informs him of some of the hidden matters (المغيبات). فَآمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ (So believe in Allah and His apostles.) being sincere (مخلصين). وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا (And if you believe) with true faith (حق الإِيمان) وَتَتَّقُوا (and be wary [of Allah]) of hypocrisy (النفاق), فَلَكُمْ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ (there will be a great reward for you.) whose measure cannot be estimated.

** وَلا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ بِما آتاهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ هُوَ خَيْراً لَهُمْ (180) Let those who are stingy with what Allah has given them out of His grace not suppose that it is good for them. And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتاء) [i.e., wa lā taḥsabanna]. ** بَلْ هُوَ meaning, the stinginess (البخل), ** شَرٌّ لَهُمْ (rather it is worse for them) because it attracts punishment upon them. ** سَيُطَوَّقُونَ ما بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ (They will be collared with what they withheld on the Day of Resurrection); they will be bound by its evil consequence (وباله) like the binding of a collar (الطوق).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): There is no one who withholds anything from the zakāt of his wealth except that Allah will make that, on the Day of Resurrection, a serpent of fire collared around his neck, biting (يَنْهَش) his flesh until the reckoning is finished. And this is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: سَيُطَوَّقُونَ ما بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ (They will be collared with what they withheld on the Day of Resurrection), meaning what they withheld from the zakāt.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) said: There is no owner of wealth subject to zakāt—whether dates, crops, or vineyards—who withholds the zakāt of his wealth, except that Allah will make him wear the soil of his land as a necklace (قَلَّدَهُ) with which he is collared, from the seven earths (سبع أرضين), until the Day of Resurrection. وَلِلَّهِ مِيراثُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (And to Allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth.) And to Him belongs what is in them (the heavens and the earth) of that which is inherited. So what is the matter with these people that they are stingy toward Him with His wealth and do not spend it in the way of Allah? ** وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ (and Allah is well aware of what you do)—of withholding (المنع) and giving (الإِعطاء)—خَبِيرٌ (well aware), so He will recompense them. And it was recited with the yāʾ (بالياء) [i.e., yaʿmalūna] as a shift in address (الالتفات), and this is more emphatic in the threat (الوعيد).

The Almighty's saying: (١٨١) لَقَدْ سَمِعَ اللهُ قَوْلَ الَّذِينَ قالُوا إِنَّ اللهَ فَقِيرٌ وَنَحْنُ أَغْنِياءُ (181) Certainly Allah has heard the saying of those who said, 'Allah is poor and we are rich.' The Jews said this when they heard ** مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً** (Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan?), as it was said.

And al-Qummi said: By Allah, they did not see Allah so that they might know that He is poor, but rather they saw the friends (أولياء) of Allah as poor, so they said: "If He were rich, He would have enriched His friends (أولياء)." Thus, they boasted over Allah with wealth (الغنى).

In al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are those who claim that the Imam needs what they carry to him.

سَنَكْتُبُ ما قالُوا We shall write down what they said in the scrolls (صحائف) of the scribes (الكتبة) and preserve it in Our knowledge; We do not neglect it, because it is a grave word, as it is disbelief (كفر) in Allah and mockery of Him.

** وَقَتْلَهُمُ الْأَنْبِياءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍ. and their killing of the prophets unjustly.**

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Nay, by Allah, they did not kill them with their swords, but rather they publicized their affair and spread it against them, so they were killed. And it was read (سيكتب) with the ya (الياء) and the damma (ضمها) [i.e., سَيُكْتَبُ], and (وقتلهم) with the rafa' (الرفع) [i.e., وَقَتْلُهُمْ].

** وَنَقُولُ and We shall say, — and it was read with the ya (الياء) [i.e., وَيُقَالُ or وَيَقُولُ] — ** ذُوقُوا عَذابَ الْحَرِيقِ 'Taste the punishment of the burning.' And We shall take vengeance upon them for this saying.

(١٨٢) ذلِكَ بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَأَنَّ اللهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ (182) That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not tyrannical to the servants. Rather, He only punishes in accordance with justice, if He punishes and does not bestow favor.

footnote (١) نهشته الحية من بابي ضرب ونفع لسعته وعضته «مجمع».

(183) The Almighty's saying: الَّذِينَ قالُوا إِنَّ اللهَ عَهِدَ إِلَيْنا (Those who said, 'Allah has enjoined upon us) — He commanded us in the Tawrah (التوراة) and enjoined upon us — أَلَّا نُؤْمِنَ لِرَسُولٍ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَنا بِقُرْبانٍ تَأْكُلُهُ النَّارُ (that we shall not believe any apostle unless he brings us a sacrifice consumed by fire.') — until he brings us this specific miracle (المعجزة الخاصّة) which was [granted] to the prophets of the Children of Israel, which is that a sacrifice (قربان) is offered—and a qurban is that by which one seeks nearness to Allah, whether it be a slaughtered animal (ذبيحة) or otherwise—then the Prophet (peace be upon him) stands and supplicates, and fire descends from the heaven and consumes the sacrifice of the one from whom it was accepted. This is among their fabrications (مفترياتهم) and falsehoods (أباطيلهم), because this [act] only necessitates faith because it is a miracle (معجزة), and thus it is equal in that regard to all other miracles (المعجزات).

The Almighty's saying: قُلْ قَدْ جاءَكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِي بِالْبَيِّناتِ وَبِالَّذِي قُلْتُمْ فَلِمَ قَتَلْتُمُوهُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (Say, 'Apostles came to you before me with manifest proofs and with what you said. So why did you kill them, if you are truthful?') — This is a refutation (تكذيب) and an imposition (الزام), [stating] that apostles came to them before him, such as Zakariyya (زكريا) and Yahya (يحيى), with other miracles (معجزات أُخر) necessitating confirmation, and with what they proposed, yet they killed them. If the thing necessitating confirmation was the bringing of it [the sacrifice], and their refusal to believe was due to it, then why did they not believe in those who came with it in other miracles (معجزات أُخر), and [instead] dared to kill him?

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "There were five hundred years between the speakers [those who made the claim] and the killers [those who killed the prophets]. So Allah imposed the killing upon them because of their contentment (رضاهم) with what they had done." Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated the like of this in several narrations (روايات).

(184) فَإِنْ كَذَّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ جاؤُ بِالْبَيِّناتِ (184) If they deny you, apostles before you were denied, who came with manifest proofs, the miracles (المعجزات) وَالزُّبُرِ and the wisdom (الحكم) and the admonitions (المواعظ) that restrain (الزواجر) ** وَالْكِتابِ الْمُنِيرِ and the illuminating Book. which comprises the divine laws (الشرائع) and the rulings (الأحكام). And it was read (وقرئ) as وبالزبر.

(١٨٥) كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ (Every soul shall taste death) A promise and a threat for the one who affirms (the truth) and the one who denies (it).

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Whoever is killed has not tasted death." Then He (peace be upon him) said: "He must return until he tastes death."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Whoever is killed will be resurrected until he dies, and whoever dies will be resurrected until he is killed." The first narration has already passed in its entirety during the interpretation of the Almighty's saying: أَفَإِنْ ماتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ (3:144) from this surah.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that he said: "The inhabitants of the earth will die until no one remains. Then the inhabitants of the heavens will die until no one remains, except the Angel of Death (ملك الموت), the Bearers of the Throne (حملة العرش), Gabriel (جبرئيل), and Michael (ميكائيل)." He said: "Then the Angel of Death will come until he stands before Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and it will be said to him: 'Who remains?'—and He is more knowing. He will say: 'O Lord, none remains except the Angel of Death, the Bearers of the Throne, Gabriel, and Michael.' It will be said to him: 'Say to Gabriel and Michael, "Let them die."' The angels will say at that moment: 'O Lord, Your two Messengers and Your two Trustees (أميناك)!' He will say: 'I have decreed death upon every soul that contains the spirit (الروح).' Then the Angel of Death will come until he stands before Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and it will be said to him: 'Who remains?'—and He is more knowing. He will say: 'O Lord, none remains except the Angel of Death and the Bearers of the Throne.' He will say: 'Say to the Bearers of the Throne, "Let them die."' Then He (peace be upon him) said: 'He (the Angel of Death) will come, distressed and sorrowful, not raising his gaze.' It will be said to him: 'Who remains?'—and He is more knowing. He will say: 'O Lord, none remains except the Angel of Death.' It will be said to him: 'Die, O Angel of Death!' So he will die. Then He (Allah) will take the earth in His right hand and say: 'Where are those who used to call upon a partner with Me? Where are those who used to set up another god with Me?'"

وَإِنَّما تُوَفَّوْنَ أُجُورَكُمْ (and you will indeed be paid your full rewards) You will be given the recompense for your deeds, whether good or evil, completely and fully, ** يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (on the Day of Resurrection), the day you rise from the graves. And some rewards may occur before it, as indicated by the narrations concerning the reward and punishment of the grave.

** فَمَنْ زُحْزِحَ عَنِ النَّارِ** (So whoever is kept away from the Fire) By being distanced from it, ** وَأُدْخِلَ الْجَنَّةَ فَقَدْ فازَ ** (and admitted into paradise, he has certainly succeeded)—he has attained salvation and achieved the desired goal.

In al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace), relating from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in a hadith: "By My Might, I swear, and by My Majesty, I vow, that no servant among My servants takes Ali as a guardian (يَتَوَلَّى عَلِيّاً) except that I distance him (زَحْزَحْتُهُ) from the Fire and admit him into Paradise. And no one among My servants hates him except that I hate him and admit him into the Fire, and wretched is the destination."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The best among you are your generous ones (سُمَحَاؤُكُمْ), and the worst among you are your stingy ones (بُخَلَاؤُكُمْ). And among the purest aspects of faith is kindness (البر) toward brothers (الإِخوان) and striving to fulfill their needs. Indeed, the one who is kind toward his brothers is loved by the All-beneficent (الرحمن). In this is the humiliation (مَرْغَمَة) of Satan, distancing from the Fires (النيران), and entry into the Gardens (الجنان)."

وَمَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا (The life of this world) Meaning, its adornments (زخارفها) and superfluities (فضولها), ** إِلَّا مَتاعُ الْغُرُورِ (is nothing but the wares of delusion)—al-ghurur (الْغُرُورِ) is a verbal noun (مصدر) or the plural of ghar (غار).

(١٨٦) ** ** ولَتُبْلَوُنَ أي والله لتختبرن (186) And you will surely be tested (meaning: by Allah, you will surely be tried)

** فِي أَمْوالِكُمْ in your possessions by the obligation of spending (الإِنفاق) and what afflicts them from calamities (الآفات), وَأَنْفُسِكُمْ and yourselves by al-Jihad (الجهاد), killing (القتل), captivity (الأسر), wounds (الجراح), and what comes upon them from fears (المخاوف), diseases (الأمراض), and hardships (المتاعب).

في العلل عن الرضا عليه السلام : فِي أَمْوالِكُمْ بإخراج الزكاة وفي أنفسكم بالتوطين على الصبر In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): in your possessions by giving out al-Zakah (الزكاة), and in yourselves by accustoming yourselves to patience (الصبر).

وَلَتَسْمَعُنَّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا and you will surely hear from those who were given the Book before you and from those who associate partners [with Allah]

أَذىً كَثِيراً much abuse such as the satire (هجاء) of the Messenger, the slandering (الطعن) of the religion, the incitement (إغراء) of the disbelievers (الكفرة) against the Muslims, and other than that. He informed them of that before its occurrence so that they might accustom themselves to patience (الصبر) and endurance (الاحتمال), and prepare themselves to meet it, so that its sudden descent would not overwhelm them.

** وَإِنْ تَصْبِرُوا But if you are patient** regarding that

** وَتَتَّقُوا and are Godwary (meaning: avoiding the contravention (مخالفة) of Allah's command)

** فَإِنَّ ذلِكَ that (meaning: patience (الصبر) and Godwariness (التقوى))

** مِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُورِ is indeed among the matters requiring determination (meaning: among the matters upon which firmness of opinion (ثبات الرأي) is necessary for its execution (إمضائه)).

(١٨٧) وَإِذْ أَخَذَ اللهُ Remember the time when He took ** مِيثاقَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ. (187) And when Allah took the covenant of those who were given the Book:**

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means concerning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: لَتُبَيِّنُنَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ وَلا تَكْتُمُونَهُ (that you shall clarify it for the people and shall not conceal it). He said: [This applies] when it is revealed (إذا خرج) and is read with the yā’ (بالياء) in both [verbs]. ** فَنَبَذُوهُ meaning the covenant (الميثاق) ** وَراءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ (behind their backs). So they did not observe it and did not pay attention to it. Casting [something] behind one's back (النبذ وراء الظهر) is a metaphor (مثل) for abandoning consideration (ترك الاعتداد) and lack of attention (عدم الالتفات). The opposite of this is placing it before one's eyes (جعله نصب عينيه). ** وَاشْتَرَوْا بِهِ they took in exchange for it ** ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً (for a paltry gain) from the debris (حطام) and transient things (اعراضها) of the world (الدنيا). ** فَبِئْسَ ما يَشْتَرُونَ** (and evil is that which they buy!)

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Allah did not take a covenant upon the ignorant (أهل الجهل) that they should learn until He took a covenant upon the people of knowledge (أهل العلم) that they should teach.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a hadith wherein he mentions: that the enemies of the Messenger of Allah, those who deviate (الملحدين) concerning the signs (آيات) of Allah, (1) [this is] an interpretation (تأويل) of this Ayah, and its mention has preceded in the Sixth Introduction (المقدّمة السادسة).

(١٨٨) لا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِما أَتَوْا They are pleased with what they have done, whether it be deception (التدليس) and concealing the truth, or acts of obedience (الطاعات) and good deeds (الحسنات). And it was recited with the yāʾ (بالياء). ** وَيُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يُحْمَدُوا بِما لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا (188) and love to be praised for what they have not done— such as fulfilling the covenant (الْمِيثَاق), manifesting the truth, speaking truthfully, or every good deed (كل خير). ** فَلا تَحْسَبَنَّهُمْ This is an emphasis (تأكيد), and it was recited with the yāʾ (بالياء) and the ḍammah on the bāʾ (وضم الباء). ** بِمَفازَةٍ (188) do not suppose them to be safe— to be in a place of safety (بمنجاة). And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Far removed (ببعيد)." ** مِنَ الْعَذابِ وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (188) from the punishment, and there is a painful punishment for them. Due to their disbelief (كفرهم) and their deception (تدليسهم).

footnote (١) ولقد احضروا الكتاب كملا مشتملاً على التأويل والتنزيل والمحكم والمتشابه والناسخ والمنسوخ ولم يسقط حرف الف ولا لام فلما وقفوا على ما بينه الله من أسماء أهل الحق والباطل وان ذلك ان ظهر نقض ما عقدوه قالوا لا حاجة فيه نحن مستغنون عنه بما عندنا ولذلك قال** فَنَبَذُوهُ وَراءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَاشْتَرَوْا بِهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً فَبِئْسَ ما يَشْتَرُونَ** (But they cast it behind their backs and sold it for a paltry gain, and evil is that which they purchase!) ثم دفعهم الاضطرار بورود المسائل عليهم عما لا يعلمون إلى جمعه وتأليفه وتضمينه من تلقائهم ما يقيمون به دعائم كفرهم فصرخ مناديهم من كان عنده شيء من القرآن فليأتنا به ووكلوا تأليفه ونظمه إلى بعض من وافقهم على معاداة أولياء الله والفه على اختيارهم وتركوا منه ما قد رأوا أنّه لهم وهو عليهم ورأوا ما ظهر تناكره وتنافره وانكشف أهل الاستعباد عوارهم وافتراؤهم. (1) And they certainly brought the Book complete, comprising the interpretation (التأويل), the revelation (التنزيل), the decisive (المحكم), the ambiguous (المتشابه), the abrogating (الناسخ), and the abrogated (المنسوخ), and not a single letter alif (ألف) or lām (لام) was omitted. But when they came upon what Allah had clarified regarding the names of the people of truth (أهل الحق) and the people of falsehood (الباطل), and that if this were to appear, it would nullify what they had established, they said: "There is no need for it; we are self-sufficient concerning it with what we possess." And for that reason, He said: ** فَنَبَذُوهُ وَراءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَاشْتَرَوْا بِهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً فَبِئْسَ ما يَشْتَرُونَ (But they cast it behind their backs and sold it for a paltry gain, and evil is that which they purchase!) Then necessity compelled them, due to the questions that arose for them concerning matters they did not know, to gather it and compile it, and to include within it, from their own accord, that by which they could establish the pillars of their disbelief (كفرهم). So their caller shouted: "Whoever has anything of the Quran, let him bring it to us." And they entrusted its compilation and arrangement (نظمه) to some of those who agreed with them in hostility towards the friends of Allah (أولياء الله). And they compiled it according to their own choosing, and they omitted from it what they saw as being in their favor while it was actually against them. And they saw what appeared to be contradictory and discordant, and the people of servitude (أهل الاستعباد) exposed their defects (عوارهم) and their fabrication (افتراؤهم).

(١٨٩) وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (189) To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. So He possesses their affair ** وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ and Allah has power over all things. thus, He is capable of punishing them. (١٩٠) إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ لَآياتٍ لِأُولِي الْأَلْبابِ (190) Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day, there are signs for those who possess intellect. [They are] clear proofs (دلائل واضحة) of the Oneness (التوحيد) and the perfection of His knowledge (exalted is He), His wisdom, and the execution (نفاذ) of His power (قدرته) and His will (مشيته), for those who possess intellects (العقول) purified (الخالصة) from the impurities (شوائب) of sensation (الحس) and illusion (الوهم).

(١٩١) الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ in all circumstances and in all postures ** قِياماً وَقُعُوداً وَعَلى جُنُوبِهِمْ**. (191) Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and on their sides,

Narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever loves to graze in the gardens (رياض) of Paradise, let him increase the remembrance (ذكر) of Allah."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: The Messenger of Allah said: "Whoever increases the remembrance (ذكر) of Allah the Almighty, Allah loves him."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding His saying: ** الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ قِياماً (Those who remember Allah standing), he said: "The healthy person prays standing, and the sick person prays sitting, and 'on their sides' (وعلى جنوبهم) refers to the one who is weaker than the sick person who prays sitting."

And in al-Amali and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him عليه السلام: "The believer remains in prayer as long as he is in the remembrance (ذكر) of Allah, whether standing, sitting, or reclining. Indeed, Allah says: ** الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ قِياماً وَقُعُوداً وَعَلى جُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِي خَلْقِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and on their sides, and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth)," and they take heed (يعتبرون) through them both. and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "The best of acts of worship (العبادات) is the constant contemplation (إدمان التفكر) of Allah and His power (قدرته)."

And narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام), he said: "The Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) used to say: 'Awaken your heart through contemplation (التفكر), keep your side away from the night (i.e., stay awake for worship), and fear Allah, your Lord.'"

And narrated from Imam al-Rida عليه السلام: "Worship (العبادة) is not the abundance of prayer (الصلاة) and fasting (الصوم); rather, worship is contemplation (التفكر) of the command (أمر) of Allah."

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Contemplation (تفكر) for an hour is better than standing (in prayer) for a night." And in one narration: "than the worship (عبادة) of a year." And in another: "than sixty years." This variation is only due to the difference in the ranks (مراتب) of contemplation, the degrees (درجات) of the contemplators, and the types of things contemplated upon. ** رَبَّنا ما خَلَقْتَ هذا (Our Lord, You have not created this) creation ** باطِلاً (in vain)—meaning frivolously (عبثاً), wasted (ضائعاً), or without wisdom (حكمة). That is, they say this: ** سُبْحانَكَ (Glory be to You)—a declaration of Your transcendence (تنزيهاً لك) from frivolity (العبث) and the creation of falsehood (الباطل), which is an objection (to the idea of creation being vain). ** فَقِنا عَذابَ النَّارِ (So save us from the punishment of the Fire) due to the failure to reflect upon it and uphold what it necessitates. (They say:) 'Our Lord, You have not created this in vain. Glory be to You! So save us from the punishment of the Fire.

(١٩٢) رَبَّنا إِنَّكَ مَنْ تُدْخِلِ النَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُ وَما لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنْصارٍ (Our Lord! Whomever You commit to the Fire, You have certainly disgraced him, and the wrongdoers will have no helpers.)

The explicit noun (المظهر) was placed in the position of the implicit pronoun (المضمر) to indicate that their wrongdoing became the cause for their entry into the Fire and the cutting off of help from them in escaping it.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They will have no Imams (أئمة) whom they call by their names.

** رَبَّنا إِنَّنا سَمِعْنا مُنادِياً (Our Lord! We have indeed heard a caller) He is the Messenger, and it is said: the Quran. ** يُنادِي لِلْإِيمانِ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ فَآمَنَّا رَبَّنا فَاغْفِرْ لَنا ذُنُوبَنا (calling to faith, [saying,] 'Believe in your Lord!' So we believed. Our Lord! Forgive us our sins) our major sins (كبائرنا), for they possess consequences (تبعات) and tails (أذناب) [i.e., lingering effects]. ** وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئاتِنا (and absolve us of our misdeeds) our minor sins (صغائرنا), for they are considered ugly (مستقبحة) but are expiated for the one who avoids major sins (الكبائر). ** وَتَوَفَّنا مَعَ الْأَبْرارِ (and make us die with the virtuous.) being specified by their companionship, counted among their company.

(١٩٤) ** رَبَّنا وَآتِنا ما وَعَدْتَنا عَلى رُسُلِكَ (Our Lord! Give us what You have promised us through Your apostles) meaning, upon their tongues. They only asked for what they were promised, despite the fact that Allah does not break His promise, as an act of worship (تَعَبُّداً), submission (اسْتِكَانَة), and fearing that they might have been deficient in compliance (الِامْتِثَال). ** وَلا تُخْزِنا يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ (and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection) by protecting us from that which necessitates disgrace. إِنَّكَ لا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعادَ (Indeed You do not break the promise) by rewarding the believer and answering the caller. And the repetition of ** رَبَّنا (Our Lord) is for emphasis in earnest prayer (الِابْتِهَال) and to indicate the independence of the requests (الْمَطَالِب) and the loftiness of their status. It is narrated: Whoever is grieved by an affair and says "Our Lord (رَبَّنا)" five times, Allah delivers him from what he fears.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, when this verse was revealed, He said: "Woe to him who chews it between his jaws (لَاكَهَا بَيْنَ فكَّيْهِ) and does not reflect upon what is in it."

(١٩٥) فَاسْتَجابَ لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ إلى طلبهم Then their Lord answered them (in response) to their request, أَنِّي لا أُضِيعُ عَمَلَ عامِلٍ مِنْكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثى بَعْضُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ (saying,) 'I will not waste the work of any worker among you, male or female; you are all one and the same.' because the male is from the female and the female is from the male, or because they are both from a single origin, or due to the extreme closeness and unity, and their agreement in religion and obedience. And this (verse) is an objection (اعتراض). It is narrated that Umm Salamah said: "O Messenger of Allah, why are men mentioned (1) in the Hijrah (migration) but not women?" So Allah revealed: فَالَّذِينَ هاجَرُوا (So those who migrated) from their homelands and tribes for the sake of the religion, ** وَأُخْرِجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ وَأُوذُوا فِي سَبِيلِي and were expelled from their homes and tormented in My way, because of their faith in Allah and for His sake, ** وَقاتَلُوا (They fought) the disbelievers (الكفّار) وَقُتِلُوا and fought and were killed, in al-Jihad. And it was read with the precedence of وَقُتِلُوا (and were killed) [over وَقاتَلُوا (and fought)], and with the doubling (بتشديد) of its tā' (تائها). ** لَأُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ وَلَأُدْخِلَنَّهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ ثَواباً مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ وَاللهُ عِنْدَهُ حُسْنُ الثَّوابِ. I will surely absolve them of their misdeeds, and I will surely admit them into gardens with streams running in them, as a reward from Allah, and Allah is He with Whom is the best of rewards.**

In al-Amali: When the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) migrated from Mecca to Medina to join the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), he had fought (2) the horsemen of Quraysh. With him were Fatimah bint Asad, Fatimah the daughter of the Messenger of Allah, and Fatimah bint al-Zubayr. He traveled openly and victoriously until he stopped at Ḍajnan (3). He remained there for a day and a night, and a group of the weak among the believers joined him, including Umm Ayman, the freedwoman (مولاة) of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). That night, he and the Fatimahs were praying, and يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ قِياماً وَقُعُوداً وَعَلى جُنُوبِهِمْ and remember Allah standing, sitting, and on their sides, They continued thus until the dawn broke. He led them in the Dawn Prayer (صلاة الفجر), and then he proceeded on his way. And they continued to do this, station after station, worshipping Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), and supplicating to Him, until they arrived in Medina. The revelation concerning their affair had already descended before their arrival: الَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ قِياماً وَقُعُوداً (those who remember Allah standing, sitting...) and the subsequent verses.

Regarding His saying: مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثى (male or female), the male (الذكر) is Ali, and the females (الأنثى) are the Fatimahs. ** بَعْضُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ you are all one and the same.** meaning Ali is from Fatimah, or he said the Fatimahs are from Ali.

And al-Qummi (said): ** فَالَّذِينَ هاجَرُوا وَأُخْرِجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ** (So those who migrated and were expelled from their homes) means the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), Salman, and Abu Dharr when he was expelled, and Ammar, those who were tormented in the way of Allah.

I say: And the verses include everyone who is characterized by these attributes.

(1) The gist of her question is: What is the difference between men and women regarding the establishment of the reward for al-Hijrah for the former but not the latter? The gist of the answer in the Ayah is that al-Hijrah has requirements and rulings that are not suitable for women. It is also possible that the intent is the establishment of the reward for them as well, either specifically for the Fatimahs (الفواطم) or generally for others as well, provided they meet the condition of equality with them in manner and cause.

(2) قارعته means I struck him or argued with him, and فَقَرَعْتُهُ means I overcame him in argument. قارعته أقرعه (with two fatḥahs) means I overcame him (مجمع).

(3) ضجنان (like سَكْرَان) is a mountain near Mecca (ق).

(١٩٦) لا يَغُرَّنَّكَ تَقَلُّبُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فِي الْبِلادِ (Do not be deceived by the maneuvering of the faithless in the cities.)

Their expansion in their earnings, their trades, and their farms, and their abundance in their livelihood and their fortune. The address is for everyone, or for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and what is intended is his community (أمته).

It is narrated: That some of the Muslims used to see the polytheists (المشركين) in prosperity and ease of life, so they would say: "Indeed, the enemies of Allah are in the goodness that we see, while we have perished from hunger." So [the verse] was revealed.

(١٩٧) مَتاعٌ قَلِيلٌ (197) a brief enjoyment. That turning about is an enjoyment whose duration is short, insignificant compared to what Allah the Almighty has prepared for the believers. And in the Prophetic Hadith (الحديث النبوي): "The world (الدنيا) compared to the Hereafter (الآخرة) is nothing but like what one of you places his finger in the sea (اليم); let him look at what it returns with." ثُمَّ مَأْواهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهادُ Then their refuge will be hell, and it is an evil resting place. what they prepared for themselves.

(١٩٨) ** لكِنِ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا رَبَّهُمْ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٌ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها نُزُلاً مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ (198) But those who are wary of their Lord—for them there will be gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them, a hospitality from Allah. The nuzul (النزل) is what is prepared for the guest (النازل) in terms of food, drink, and provision (صلة). ** وَما عِنْدَ اللهِ and what is with Allah due to its abundance and permanence ** خَيْرٌ لِلْأَبْرارِ is better for the righteous than what the wicked (الفجار) indulge in, due to its scarcity, the swiftness of its disappearance, and its mixture with pains (الآلام).

(١٩٩) وَإِنَّ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ لَمَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ خاشِعِينَ لِلَّهِ لا يَشْتَرُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً (Indeed, among the People of the Book are those who believe in Allah and what has been sent down to you, humbling themselves before Allah, and they do not sell the signs of Allah for a paltry gain.) just as the professionals among their rabbis (أحبارهم) did. أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ (They shall have their reward with their Lord.) And they will be given their reward twice, just as He promised it in another verse (آية أُخرى). ** إِنَّ اللهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ (Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning.) due to His knowledge of the deeds and what every doer deserves of the recompense (الجزاء), so He hastens the recompense and delivers the promised reward quickly.

(٢٠٠) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اصْبِرُوا على الفرائض وَصابِرُوا على المصائب وَرابِطُوا على الأئمة. (O you who have faith, be patient, and be steadfast, and stand guard,) Thus it is narrated in al-Kafi from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): Be patient upon the calamities (المصائب), and be steadfast upon the obligatory duties (الفرائض), and stand guard upon the Imams (الأئمة). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Be patient regarding sins (المعاصي), and be steadfast upon the obligatory duties (الفرائض). And in another narration: Be patient upon your religion (دينكم), and be steadfast against your enemy (عدوكم) from those who oppose you, and stand guard for your Imam (إمامكم). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): And be steadfast upon dissimulation (التقية). And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Be patient upon the calamities (المصائب), and be steadfast against them (وَصابِرُوهم) regarding the trial (الفتنة), and stand guard for the one whom you follow as an example (تقتدون به). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ. (and be wary of Allah so that you may be felicitous.) Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Meaning, regarding what He commanded you with and made obligatory upon you. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The Ayah (1) was revealed concerning al-Abbas and concerning us. And the standing guard (الرباط) that we were commanded with had not yet occurred, but that will be from our offspring, the one who stands guard (المرابط), and from his offspring, the one who stands guard (المرابط). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him): Stand guard for the prayers (الصلوات). He said: Meaning, wait for them one after the other, because the standing guard (المرابطة) was not [in the military sense] at that time. And narrated from the Prophet (may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family): Part of the standing guard (الرباط) is waiting for the prayer after the prayer. The reward for reciting this Surah has already preceded at the end of al-Baqarah.

footnote (١) It is probable that the intent of his (peace be upon him) saying, "The Ayah was revealed..." is that they were commanded to stand guard for us (*برباطنا*) and to connect with us (*وصلتنا*), but they abandoned this and did not comply. This will occur during the time of the appearance of the *Qā'im* (peace be upon him), so those who remain of their offspring will stand guard for us (*فيرابطنا*) and will aid our *Qā'im*. Thus, the one who stands guard (*المرابط*) with *fatḥah* [on the *bā'*] will be from our offspring—meaning the *Qā'im* (may Allah hasten his relief)—and the one who stands guard (*المرابط*) with *kasrah* [on the *bā'*] will be from his offspring. It is also probable, based on this interpretation, that the *kasrah* is used for both, or the *fatḥah* is used for both. So reflect upon this.
